benefits: Atisarharo Ras treats significantlys Atisara (all types of diarrhoea and dysentery).
ingredients:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Rala (राल)	Shorea robusta resin (Dammar/sal tree resin)	Equal part
Mochras (मोचरस)	Salmalia malabarica (red silk cotton tree) resin	Equal part
Naga Phena / Samudra Phena (नागफेन/समुद्रफेन)	Cuttlefish bone (Sepia officinalis)	Equal part
Vatsaka Tvak / Kutaja (वत्सककी छाल)	Holarrhena antidysenterica bark	Equal part
Atisa (अतीस)	Aconitum heterophyllum (Indian atis root)	Equal part
Nagara / Shunthi (नागर/सोंठ)	Dry Ginger (Zingiber officinale)	Equal part
preparation: 1. Take equal parts of all six ingredients.\n2. Powder each finely and combine.\n3. Store in glass.
reference: Bharat Bhaishajya Ratnakar, part-1,verse 284, page no 101

name: Lokeshwar Ras
benefits: Treats significantlys Grahani Roga (chronic malabsorption/sprue/irritable bowel) and Atisara (diarrhoea/dysentery).
ingredients:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Shu. Gandhak (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	2 parts
Shankha Bhasma (शंखभस्म)	Incinerated Conch Shell 	8 parts
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	1 part
Yavakshara (यवक्षार)	Barley alkali	Half the Parada quantity
Chitraka Mool Kwath (चित्रकमूलक्वाथ)	Plumbago zeylanica root decoction — for Bhavana	As required
preparation:
1. In a stone mortar, make Kajjali from Parada and Gandhaka. Add Shankha Bhasma and mix well.
2. Give 7-7 Bhavanas with Arka Ksheera (madar milk sap) and Chitraka Mool Kwath (Plumbago decoction) — dry thoroughly between each.
3. Add Yavakshara at half the quantity of Parada. Mix.
4. With Arka Ksheera, form large Vataka (balls). Dry well.
5. Place in a vessel lined with lime solution on the inside. Seal as Sharava Samput with clay and fire in Laghu Puta (small firing).
6. When Svanga Sheetal: open Samput, remove,perform Mardana procedure in mortar and store in glass. This is Lokeshwara Ras.
anupana: Madhu (Honey) with Maricha Churna and Ghee

name: Lokanath Ras — Version 2
benefits: Treats significantlys Grahani Roga (malabsorption/sprue) and Atisara (diarrhoea/dysentery) — especially Vata-type Atisara.
ingredients: Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity\nRasa Sindura / Mruta Parada (रससिन्दूर)	Processed Mercury / Red Mercuric Sulphide	1 part\nShu. Gandhak (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	4 parts
preparation:
1.perform Mardana procedure Rasa Sindura in stone mortar. Add Shu. Gandhaka andperform Mardana procedure for 1 Yama (3 hours) until fully combined.
2. Fill this mixture into Badi Varatika (large cowrie shells).
3.perform Mardana procedure Tankana with Gau Dugdha (cow's milk) to paste. Apply to seal cowrie mouths firmly. Dry.
4. Place in clay vessel (Haandi), seal with clay, fire in Gajaputa.
5. When Svanga Sheetal: open.perform Mardana procedure the cowrie shells together with the medicine content. Store in glass.

name: Naga Sundar Ras
benefits: Treats significantlys: all Atisara (diarrhoea/dysentery), Guda Paribhransha (rectal prolapse) and Pravahika/Bimbi (dysentery with tenesmus — painful straining with discharge).
ingredients:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Naga Bhasma (नागभस्म)	Incinerated Lead 	23 gm (half Pala)
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	23 gm (half Pala)
Abhraka Bhasma (अभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated Mica	23 gm (half Pala)
Shu. Gandhak (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	23 gm (half Pala)
Rala (राल)	Shorea robusta resin (Dammar/sal resin) — melted	93 gm (2 Pala)
Vatajata Churna (वटजटाचूर्ण)	Banyan aerial root powder	12 gm
Baheda Phala Tvak Churna (बहेडाफलत्वक्चूर्ण)	Terminalia bellirica bark powder	12 gm
Saindhava Churna (सैन्धवचूर्ण)	Rock Salt powder	12 gm
Vacha Churna (वचचूर्ण)	Acorus calamus (sweet flag) powder	12 gm
Trikatu Churna (त्रिकटुचूर्ण)	Ginger+Black Pepper+Long Pepper powder	12 gm
Bhrishta Sveta Jeeraka Churna (भृष्टश्वेतजीरकचूर्ण)	Roasted white cumin powder	12 gm
Krishna Jeerak Churna (कृष्णजीरकचूर्ण)	Black cumin powder	12 gm
Haritaki Phala Tvak Churna (हरीतकीफलत्वक्चूर्ण)	Terminalia chebula bark powder	12 gm
Bhang Churna (भाँगचूर्ण)	Cannabis (Bhang/Vijaya) powder	12 gm
Ajwain Churna (अजवाइनचूर्ण)	Trachyspermum ammi powder	12 gm
Bhavana Dravyas (3 each)
preparation:
1. In a clean large stone mortar, firml yperform Mardana procedure Parada and Gandhaka to make Kajjali. Add Naga Bhasma and Abhraka Bhasma,perform Mardana procedure well.
2. In a large iron vessel, take 93 gm Rala (Shorea resin) and melt on low fire. Mix the Kajjali into the melted Rala and remove from fire. Cool and powder.
3. Add all 11 herb powders (12-12 gm each) and mix into the Kajjali-Rala mixture.
4. Give 3-3 Bhavanas each with Mahanimba Tvak Svaras, Kamboji Mool Ras, Nagabala Svaras and Guduchi Svaras —perform Mardana procedurefirmly each time.
5. Form tablets of Badara Asthi size (375 mg). Dry in shade and store in glass. This is Naga Sundar Ras.

name: Bhagandarahar Ras
benefits: Bhagandara (Fistula-in-Ano)
ingredients:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	1 part
Shu. Gandhak (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	2 parts (double the mercury)
Shu. Gila / Gairika (शु. गिल/गैरिक)	Purified Red Ochre (Iron oxide)	Equal to all above combined
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated Copper	Equal to all above combined
Loha Bhasma (लोहभस्म)	Incinerated Iron 	Equal to all above combined
Ghritkumari Svaras (घृतकुमारी स्वरस)	Aloe vera juice — for 3-day trituration	As required
Nimbu Ras (नींबूरस)	Lemon juice — for 7 Bhavanas	As required
preparation:
1. Make Kajjali from 1 part Shu. Parada and 2 parts Shu. Gandhaka.
2. Add Shu. Gila (Gairika/red ochre). Triturate with Ghritkumari Svaras (Aloe vera juice) for 3 days.
3. Add equal-weight Tamra Bhasma and Loha Bhasma. Mix, Triturate and form a ball. Place in a vessel.
4. Perform Sveda (steaming) for 2 Yama (6 hours) on an ash bed (Bhasma Pithara).
5. Give 7 Bhavanas with Nimbu Ras (lemon juice).
6. Fire in Puta. When cooled, Triturate and store in glass. This is Bhagandarahar Ras.

name: Ghanagarbha Ras
benefits: all Gudamaya (rectal/anal diseases — Arsha/hemorrhoids) and Bhagandara (fistula-in-ano).
ingredients:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Shu. Manaahshila (शु. मनःशिला)	Purified Realgar (Arsenic disulphide)	Equal part
Shu. Haritala (शु. हरताल)	Purified Orpiment (Arsenic trisulphide)	Equal part
Abhraka Bhasma (अभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated Mica 	Equal part
Haldi (हल्दी)	Turmeric (Curcuma longa)	Equal part
Tiksna Loha Bhasma (तीक्ष्णलोहभस्म)	 Incinerated Iron 	Equal part
Naga Bhasma (सीसाभस्म)	Incinerated Lead 	Equal part
Svarna Makshika Bhasma (सोनामक्खीभस्म)	Incinerated Chalcopyrite/Iron Pyrite  	Equal part
Kaanta Loha Bhasma (कान्तलोहभस्म)	Magnetic Incinerated Iron 	Equal part
Ghritkumari Svaras (घृतकुमारी)	Aloe vera juice — for Bhavana	As required
Vanj Kakodra Ras (बांझककोड़ारस)	Momordica dioica (spiny gourd) juice — for Bhavana	As required
Devadali / Bindal Ras (देवदाली/बिंडाल रस)	Luffa echinata juice — for Bhavana	As required
Rasa Sindura (रससिन्दूर)	Processed Mercury — added after firing, equal part	Equal part to total Bhasma
preparation:
1. Take equal parts of all 8 mineral ingredients. Triturate with Ghritkumari Svaras, Vanj Kakodra Ras and Devadali (Bindal) Ras — Trituratewell.
2. Form tablets. Dry well. Place in Samput, seal and fire on Upal (dried cow dung) fire.
3. When cooled, take the resulting Bhasma and add equal-part Rasa Sindura. Mix well.
4. Use at 2 Ratti (250 mg) dose. Store in glass.
reference: Rasendra Mangal, Bharat Bhaishajya Ratnakara-part 2- verse 1340- page no. 132

name: Kalagni Ras
benefits: Kalagni Ras (internal + external) gives benefit in Bhagandara (fistula-in-ano).
ingredients:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	Equal part
Shu. Gandhak (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	Equal part
Saindhava Lavana (सैन्धवलवण)	Rock Salt	Equal part
Shu. Tuttha (शु. तुत्थ)	Purified Copper Sulphate (Blue Vitriol)	Equal part
Naga Bhasma (नागभस्म)	Incinerated Lead 	Equal part
Jeeraka Churna (जीराचूर्ण)	Cuminum cyminum (Cumin) powder	Equal part
Tiktakoshataki Sara (तिक्तकोषातकीसार)	Luffa acutangula (bitter ridge gourd/Kadvi Nenua) juice	As required for Bhavana
preparation:
1. Make Kajjali from Shu. Parada and Shu. Gandhaka in a stone mortar.
2. Add Saindhava Lavana, Shu. Tuttha, Naga Bhasma and Jeeraka Churna. Mix all into Kajjali.
3. Give Bhavana with Tiktakoshataki Sara (Kadvi Nenua/bitter ridge gourd juice).
4. Form tablets of 250 mg. Dry in shade and store in glass.
5. This Vati can also be applied externally as Lepa (paste) directly on the Bhagandara wound for added benefit.

name: Bhagandarari Ras
benefits: Bhagandarari Ras rapidly treats significantlys Bhagandara Roga (fistula-in-ano) — even in severe/toxic cases.
ingredients:
Ingredients
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	Equal part
Shu. Gandhak (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	Equal part
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated Copper (Mruta)	Equal part
Abhraka Bhasma (अभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated Mica 	Equal part
Shu. Darada / Shu. Hingula (शु. दरद/शु. हिंगुल)	Purified Cinnabar (Mercuric Sulphide)	Equal part
Kali Mirch Churna (कालीमिर्चचूर्ण)	Black Pepper powder	Double of all above (2x)
Chitraka Ambu (चीतेके क्वाथ)	Plumbago zeylanica root decoction — for 3-day Triturateing	As required
preparation:
1. Make Kajjali from Shu. Parada and Shu. Gandhaka.
2. Add Tamra Bhasma, Abhraka Bhasma and Shu. Darada (Shingraf). Mix all into Kajjali.
3. Add Kali Mirch Churna at double quantity of each ingredient.
4. Triturate all together with Chitraka Kwath (Plumbago root decoction) for 3 days.
5. Form tablets of 3 Ratti (375 mg) each. Dry in shade and store in glass. This is Bhagandarari Ras.

name: Chaturanga Lepa
benefits: Pakva Bhagandara (Ripened/mature Fistula-in-Ano boil) — to open without surgery
ingredients:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Tamra Bhasma / Tamra Churna (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated Copper/powder	7 parts
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	1 part
Saindhava Lavana Churna (सैन्धवलवणचूर्ण)	Rock Salt powder	7 parts
Shu. Gandhaka Churna (शु. गन्धकचूर्ण)	Purified Sulphur powder	9 parts
Bhringaraja Svaras (भृङ्गराजस्वरस)	Eclipta alba (false daisy) juice — for 7-day Triturateing	As required
Jambiri Nimbu Ras (जम्बीरीरस)	Citrus limon (citron/lemon) juice — for 7-day Triturateing	As required
dose: Apply as thick paste directly on ripened Bhagandara fistula boil
preparation: 1. Make Kajjali from Shu. Parada and Shu. Gandhaka Churna. Add Tamra Bhasma and Saindhava Lavana Churna and mix.\n2. Triturate the combined mixture with Bhringaraja Svaras (Eclipta juice) and Jambiri Ras (lemon juice) — sitting in direct sunlight and Trituratefor 7 full days.\n3. When fully processed and dried, store in glass container. This is Chaturanga Lepa.

name: Chitravibhandako Rasa
benefits: Bhagandara (Fistula-in-Ano)
ingredients:
Ingredient
(Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	1 part
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	2 parts
Kumari Svaras (कुमारी स्वरस)	Aloe vera juice — for trituration	As required
Shu. Tamra Patra (शु. ताम्र पत्र)	Purified Copper sheet — for coating	As required
Jambiri Nimbu Rasa (जम्बीरी रस)	Lemon juice — for 7 putas	As required
preparation:
1. Make kajjali from 1 part Shu. Parada and 2 parts Shu. Gandhaka.
2. Triturate with ghee and Kumari (Aloe vera) juice for 2 days. Form a ball.
3. Apply the paste on a purified copper sheet. Place in a pot filled with ash and cook for 2 praharas (6 hours).
4. After cooling, remove, Triturate with lemon juice, form tablets and seal in samputa.
5. Give 7 putas (firings) with lemon juice. Triturate and store in glass vessel.

name: Narayana Rasa
benefits: Narayana Rasa treats significantlys: Nadivrana (sinus tracts), Gandamala (cervical lymphadenopathy/scrofula), Vicharchika (eczema), chronic infected wounds, Daad (ringworm), Puyakarna (suppurative ear disease), Shiroroga (head diseases), Shleepada/Filpaa (elephantiasis), cracking of skin of hands and feet, and Bhagandara (fistula-in-ano).
ingredients:
Ingredient
(Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Shu. Hingula (शु. हिंगुल)	Purified Cinnabar	Equal part
Saurashtra Mrittika (सौराष्ट्रमृत्तिका)	Saurashtra Clay	Equal part
Rasaut (रसौत)	Berberis extract	Equal part
Shu. Manashila (शु. मनःशिला)	Purified Realgar	Equal part
Shu. Guggul (शु. गूगल)	Purified Commiphora mukul	Equal part
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	Equal part
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated Copper 	Equal part
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	Equal part
Loha Bhasma (लोहभस्म)	Incinerated Iron 	Equal part
Saindhava Lavana (सैन्धवलवण)	Rock Salt	Equal part
Atees (अतीस)	Aconitum heterophyllum	Equal part
Chav (चव)	Piper cubeba	Equal part
Sarphonka (सारफोंका)	Tephrosia purpurea	Equal part
Bayibidanga (बायिबडंग)	Embelia ribes	Equal part
Ajwain (अजवायन)	Carum copticum (Carom seeds)	Equal part
Gajapippali (गजपीपल)	Long Pepper (large variety)	Equal part
Kali Mirch (कालीमिर्च)	Black Pepper	Equal part
Akkalkara Root (अक्कलकरा)	Anacyclus pyrethrum root	Equal part
Barun Chhaal (बरनेकी छाल)	Crataeva nurvala bark	Equal part
Ral (राल)	Resin	Equal part
Hara / Haritaki (हर)	Terminalia chebula	Equal part
Katu Taila (कड़वे तेल)	Bitter oil — for Triturate	As required
preparation: 1. Take all ingredients in equal quantities. First prepare kajjali from Shu. Parada and Shu. Gandhaka.\n2. Add powders of all other herbs and minerals. Triturate together with Katu Taila (bitter oil).\n3. Form pills (goliyas). Dry in shade and store.
reference: Rasatantrasara Sidhaprayaoga Samgraha, part 2- chapter Bhagandar-page 215

name: Ravi Tandava Rasa
benefits: Bhagandara (Fistula-in-Ano)
ingredients:
Ingredient
(Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	1 part
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	2 parts
Shu. Tamra Katora (शु. ताम्र कटोरी)	Purified Copper cup — equal weight	3 parts
Kumari Svaras (कुमारी स्वरस)	Aloe vera juice — for trituration	As required
Jambiri Nimbu Rasa (जम्बीरी रस)	Lemon juice — for 7 putas	As required
preparation:
1. Prepare kajjali from 1 part Shu. Parada and 2 parts Shu. Gandhaka in a stone mortar. Triturate with Kumari (Aloe vera) juice for 3 days. Form a large ball.
2. Prepare a purified copper cup (Tamra Katora) by quenching it seven times in taila (oil), takra (buttermilk), go-mutra (cow urine), arnaala (sour fermented liquid), and kulattha (horsegram decoction).
3. Place the kajjali ball inside the prepared copper cup. In a large pot, invert the cup, cover with a sharava (earthen saucer lid) and seal firmly with clay. Fill the pot with ash and fire on a stove for 6 hours (2 praharas).
4. After cooling, remove the ash and sharava. Take the medicine out of the copper cup, Triturate in mortar with Jambiri lemon juice and form tablets. Dry and seal in a samputa.
5. Repeat the firing (puta) 7 times with lemon juice. Store the final product in a glass vessel. This is Ravi Tandava Rasa.

name: Rooparaj Rasa
benefits: Rooparaj Rasa is the treats significantlyer of Bhagandara Roga (fistula-in-ano). By taking it with proper diet (pathya), Bhagandara — even the severe form — is cured rapidly in few doses.
ingredients:
Ingredient
(Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Rasasindura (रससिन्दूर)	Purified Mercuric Sulphide (processed)	2 parts
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated Copper 	4 parts
Kakajangha Rasa (काकजंघारस)	Dolichos biflorus / Kakajangha juice — for 5-day trituration	As required
Shahad / Madhu (शहद)	Honey — for final processing	As required
Suhaga / Tankana (सुहागा)	Borax — for final heating	As required
preparation:
1. Take Rasasindura (2 parts) and Tamra Bhasma (4 parts). Triturate together in a mortar (khalva) for 5 days with Kakajangha juice.
2. Form a ball, dry and place in a sealed purified copper samputa (closed crucible).
3. Keep the samputa in an earthen pot with a small hole at the bottom. Fill the pot with sand up to the neck. Place on a stove and fire continuously for 8 praharas (24 hours).
4. After cooling, remove the medicine from the samputa and Triturate finely.
5. Mix with honey (Madhu) and Suhaga (Borax). Place in an open crucible (Moosha) and heat until the medicine starts rotating/churning (a specific firing endpoint).
6. Cool, Triturate well and store in a glass container. This is Rooparaj Rasa.
anupana: Lick the medicine with Honey (Shahad), then drink Triphala decoction (Triphala Kwath).
reference: Bharat Bhaishajya Ratnakar, part-4,verse 6157, page no 453

name: Vijaya Gutika
benefits: Kasa (cough), Shvasa (asthma/breathlessness), Kshaya (tuberculosis/wasting disease), Gulma (abdominal tumour/bloating), Prameha (urinary disorders/diabetes), Vishama Jvara (intermittent/malarial fever), Shotha (oedema/swelling), Pandu (anaemia/jaundice), Kushtha (skin diseases), Grahani (malabsorption syndrome/IBS), Arsha (haemorrhoids/piles) and Bhagandara (fistula-in-ano).
ingredients:
Ingredient
(Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	1 part
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated Copper 	1 part
Loha Bhasma (लोहभस्म)	Incinerated Iron 	1 part
Shu. Bachnag / Vatsanabha (शु. बछनाग)	Purified Aconitum ferox	1 part
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	1 part
Bayibidanga (बायिबडंग)	Embelia ribes	1 part
Chitrak / Cheeta (चीता)	Plumbago zeylanica	1 part
Nagarmotha (नागरमोथा)	Cyperus rotundus	1 part
Nagakesara (नागकेसर)	Mesua ferrea	1 part
Renuka (रेणुका)	Vitex agnus-castus seeds	1 part
Pippali Moola (पीपलामूल)	Piper longum root	1 part
Bola (बोल)	Commiphora myrrha (Myrrh)	1 part
Guda / Jaggery (गुड़)	Jaggery — for forming pills	Double of all above combined
preparation:
1. Take all mineral and herbal ingredients in equal (1–1) parts. First prepare kajjali from Shu. Parada and Shu. Gandhaka.
2. Add powders of all other herbs and minerals. Triturate together in mortar well.
3. Finally add Guda (jaggery) at double the total quantity of all above. Mix and knead well.
4. Form pills of 5–5 masha (approx. 5g each) or Kala-pramana (black-gram size). Dry in shade and store.
reference: Bharat Bhaishajya Ratnakar, part-4,verse 7025, page no 762

name: Grahani Vajra Kapat Rasa
benefits: Grahani Vajra Kapat Rasa with honey Incinerated treats significantlys Vataj Grahani Roga (Vata-type malabsorption/IBS). Incinerated treats significantlys Grahani (malabsorption syndrome/chronic diarrhoea with undigested food) and Vatottha (Vata-origin) diseases.
ingredients:
Rasasindura (रससिन्दूर)	Purified Mercuric Sulphide	Equal part
Abhraka Bhasma (अभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated Mica 	Equal part
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	Equal part
Yavakshara Churna (यवक्षारचूर्ण)	Barley alkali powder	Equal part
Shu. Tankana (शु. टङ्कण)	Purified Borax	Equal part
Vacha Churna (वचचूर्ण)	Acorus calamus powder	Equal part
Shu. Bhanga Churna (शु. भांगचूर्ण)	Purified Cannabis powder	Equal part
Ativisha Churna (अतिविषाचूर्ण)	Aconitum heterophyllum powder	Equal to Rasasindura
Mocharas Churna (मोचरसचूर्ण)	Bombax malabaricum resin powder	Equal to Rasasindura
Jayanti (जयन्ती)	Sesbania sesban — for Bhavana	As required
Bhringaraja Svaras (भृंगराज स्वरस)	Eclipta alba juice — for trituration	As required
Jambiri Nimbu Rasa (जम्बीरी रस)	Lemon juice — for Bhavana	As required
preparation:
1. In a clean stone mortar, Triturate Rasasindura well. Add Shu. Gandhaka and mix. Then add Abhraka Bhasma, Tankana, Vacha Churna and Bhanga Churna.
2. Triturate all ingredients together with Jayanti juice, Bhringaraja juice and Jambiri (lemon) juice — 1 day each — and form a large ball.
3. Place this ball in an iron vessel (Lohapatra) and heat on a slow fire for 1.5 hours (half Yama). After cooling, remove.
4. Add equal-part Ativisha Churna and Mocharas Churna to the processed ball. Give 7 Bhavanas (trituration-drying cycles) with Bhanga (Cannabis) svarasa.
5. Dry, Triturate to fine powder. Either store as powder or form tablets of 500 mg. Collect in glass vessel. This is Grahani Vajra Kapat Rasa.
anupana: Madhu (Honey); followed by warm water with Chitraka + Bilva + Shunthee + Vida lavana (1–1g each)

name: Grahani Gajendra Rasa
benefits: Grahani (IBS/malabsorption), Atisara, Shoola, Gulma, Amlapitta, Kamala, Kushtha, Visarpa, Krimi and more
ingredients:
Paara (पारा)	Purified Mercury	Equal part
Gandhaka (गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	Equal part
Loha Bhasma (लोहभस्म)	Incinerated Iron	Equal part
Shankha Bhasma (शंखभस्म)	Incinerated Conch shell 	Equal part
Tankana (टंकण)	Borax	Equal part
Hingula (हिंगु)	Cinnabar	Equal part
Kapurkachri (कपूरकचरी)	Hedychium spicatum	Equal part
Talispatra (तालीसपत्र)	Abies webbiana leaves	Equal part
Nagarmotha (नागरमोथा)	Cyperus rotundus	Equal part
Dhaniya (घिनयां/धनिया)	Coriander seeds	Equal part
Zeera (जीरा)	Cuminum cyminum (Cumin)	Equal part
Saindhava (सेन्धानमक)	Rock Salt	Equal part
Dhay Phool (धाय का फूल)	Woodfordia fruticosa flowers	Equal part
Atees (अतीस)	Aconitum heterophyllum	Equal part
Shunthee (शोंठ)	Zingiber officinale (dry ginger)	Equal part
Griha Dhooma (रसोईघर का धूंवा)	Kitchen soot / smoke deposit	Equal part
Haritaki / Hara (हरें)	Terminalia chebula	Equal part
Shu. Mallataka (शुद्ध मल्लातक)	Purified Semicarpus anacardium (marking nut)	Equal part
Tejapatta (तेजपत्र)	Cinnamomum tamala (bay leaf)	Equal part
Jatiphala (जायफर)	Myristica fragrans (Nutmeg)	Equal part
Lavanga (लवंग)	Syzygium aromaticum (Cloves)	Equal part
Dalchini (दालचीनी)	Cinnamomum verum (Cinnamon)	Equal part
Elaichi (इलायची)	Elettaria cardamomum (Cardamom)	Equal part
Sugandh Bala (सुगन्धवाला)	Pavonia odorata / Valerian root	Equal part
Bel Giri (बेल की गिरि)	Aegle marmelos (Bael) fruit pulp	Equal part
Methi (मेथी)	Trigonella foenum-graecum (Fenugreek)	Equal part
Bhang (भांग)	Cannabis sativa	Equal part
Chhaagi Dugdha (छागी दूध)	Goat's milk — for pill making	As required
preparation: 1. Take all mineral and herbal ingredients in equal (same) parts. Triturate together thoroughly in a mortar.\n2. Triturate the combined powder with Chhaagi Dugdha (goat's milk) and form Rasa-baddha (resin-bound) pills.\n3. Dry the pills in shade and store in a glass vessel. This is Grahani Gajendra Rasa — as named by Shrimaan Gahnananda Natha.

name: Kanakasundar Rasa
benefits: Grahani (Malabsorption/IBS), Agnimaandya, Jvara, Atisara
ingredients:
Shu. Hingula (शु. हिंगुल)	Purified Cinnabar (Mercuric Sulphide)	Equal part
Maricha Churna (मिर्चचूर्ण)	Black Pepper powder	Equal part
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	Equal part
Pippali Churna (पिप्पलीचूर्ण)	Piper longum (Long Pepper) powder	Equal part
Shu. Tankana (शु. टङ्कण)	Purified Borax	Equal part
Shu. Vatsanabha Churna (शु. वत्सनाभचूर्ण)	Purified Aconitum ferox powder	Equal part
Shu. Dhatura Beeja Churna (शु. धतूरबीजचूर्ण)	Purified Datura metel seed powder	Equal part
Bhanga Svaras (भांगकाथ)	Cannabis juice — for Bhavana (3 hours trituration)	As required
preparation: 1. In a clean stone mortar, Triturate Hingula and Gandhaka together. Add all other powders (Maricha, Pippali, Tankana, Vatsanabha, Dhatura beeja).\n2. Triturate all with Bhanga Svaras (Cannabis juice) for 3 hours (one Yama). Triturate well.\n3. Form pills of Chana-matra (chickpea size, approx. 250 mg each). Dry in shade and store in glass vessel. This is Kanakasundar Rasa.

name: Laghu Siddha Abhraka Rasa
benefits: Grahani (Malabsorption/IBS) and Atisara (Diarrhoea)
ingredients:
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	Equal part
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	Equal part
Abhraka Bhasma (अभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated Mica 	Equal part
Shu. Hingula (शु. हिंगुल)	Purified Cinnabar (Mercuric Sulphide)	Equal part
Gavya Ghrita (गव्य घृत)	Cow's ghee — for processing	As required
preparation:
1. In an iron trough-shaped mortar (Loha Droni Khalva), take equal parts of Shu. Parada and Shu. Gandhaka. Triturate firmly to prepare a good kajjali.
2. Add Shu. Hingula and Abhraka Bhasma to the kajjali. Mix well. Add Gavya Ghrita (cow's ghee) and continue Triturate with an iron pestle for two days (Tapat Khalva method — heating the mortar).
3. When the kajjali becomes fluid and then dries, remove the mortar from fire. Let it cool.
4. Triturate the cooled product to fine powder and store in a glass vessel. This is Laghu Siddha Abhraka Rasa.
anupana: Anupana: Jeeraka Kaatha (cumin decoction) and Madhu (honey).

name: Pottali Rasa
benefits: Asadhya Grahani — Tridosha-predominant intractable malabsorption syndrome
ingredients:
Kaurdi Bhasma (कौड़ीभस्म)	Incinerated Cowrie shell 	10 grams (equal part)
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	10 grams (equal part)
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	10 grams (equal part)
Loha Bhasma (लोहभस्म)	Incinerated Iron	10 grams (equal part)
Shu. Tankana (शु. टङ्कण)	Purified Borax	10 grams (equal part)
Bhanga Svaras (भांग स्वरस)	Cannabis juice — for Bhavana (1 day)	As required
Choona (चूना)	Lime — for sealing cowrie shells	As required
Guda (गुड़)	Jaggery — for sealing cowrie shells	As required
dose: Dose: 500 mgMadhu (honey) and Bhrishtajeeraka Churna (roasted cumin powder) as Anupana.
preparation:
1. In a clean stone mortar, firmly Triturate Shu. Parada and Shu. Gandhaka to prepare good kajjali.
2. Add the remaining 3 substances (Kaurdi Bhasma, Loha Bhasma, Shu. Tankana) to the kajjali. Triturate all together with Bhanga Svaras (Cannabis juice) for one full day.
3. After drying, fill this powdered medicine inside Kaudiyan (cowrie shells). Seal the mouths of the cowries with lime (Choona) and jaggery (Guda) firmly.
4. Arrange the filled cowries in a Sharava Samputa (sealed earthen dish assembly) and fire in Varaha Puta (one firing cycle).
5. After cooling completely, open the samputa. Remove the lime seal from the cowrie mouths. Triturate the entire content — including the cowrie shells — to fine powder.
6. Collect in a glass vessel. This is Pottali Rasa.
anupana: Madhu (honey) and Bhrishtajeeraka Churna (roasted cumin powder)

name: Sheeghra Prabhava Rasa
benefits: Grahani (IBS/malabsorption) and Atisara (Diarrhoea)
ingredients:
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	50 g (equal part)
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	50 g (equal part)
Abhraka Bhasma (अभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated Mica 	50 g (equal part)
Tiksna Loha Bhasma (तीक्ष्णलोहभस्म)	Incinerated Iron 	50 g (equal part)
Shu. Haritala (शु. हरताल)	Purified Orpiment (Arsenic trisulphide)	50 g (equal part)
Shu. Manashila (शु. मन:शिला)	Purified Realgar (Arsenic disulphide)	50 g (equal part)
Shu. Sauveera Anjana (शु. सौवीराञ्जन)	Purified Antimonite (Stibnite) collyrium	50 g (equal part)
Vimala Bhasma (विमलभस्म)	Incinerated Iron Pyrite (Vimala/Makshika)	50 g (equal part)
Pippali Moola Churna (पिप्पलीमूलचूर्ण)	Piper longum root powder	50 g (half of above)
Chitraka Moola Churna (चित्रकमूलचूर्ण)	Plumbago zeylanica root powder	50 g (half of above)
Bhrishtajeeraka Churna (भृष्टजीरकचूर्ण)	Roasted Cumin powder	50 g (half of above)
Ajwain Churna (अजवाइनचूर्ण)	Carum copticum (Carom seeds) powder	50 g (half of above)
Nagarmotha Churna (नागरमोथाचूर्ण)	Cyperus rotundus powder	50 g (half of above)
Shu. Vatsanabha Churna (शु. वत्सनाभचूर्ण)	Purified Aconitum ferox powder	50 g (half of above)
Balaagra Majja Churna (बालाग्रमज्जाचूर्ण)	Young bamboo pith powder	50 g (half of above)
Bilva Shalatu Churna (बिल्वशलाटुचूर्ण)	Unripe Aegle marmelos (Bael) powder	50 g (half of above)
Mocharas Churna (मोचरसचूर्ण)	Bombax malabaricum resin powder	50 g (half of above)
Panchakola Kashaya (पञ्चकोलकषाय)	Decoction of 5 pungent herbs — for 5 Bhavanas	As required
Tila Taila (तिलतेल)	Sesame oil — for light frying of kajjali	100 ml
Araluvagra Rasa (अरलुवाग्ररस)	Cassia fistula (Amaltas) juice — for 10 Bhavanas	As required
preparation:
1. In a clean stone mortar, Triturate Shu. Parada and Shu. Gandhaka firmly to prepare kajjali. Add Haritala and Manashila and continue Triturate for 1 day.
2. Add all 4 Bhasmas (Abhraka, Tiksna Loha, Sauveera Anjana, Vimala Bhasma) and Triturate. Lightly fry this kajjali in a little Tila Taila (sesame oil) on mild heat.
3. Add the 9 herb-powders (from Pippali moola to Mocharas) — using half the quantity of the mineral kajjali as per Acharya's instruction. Triturate all well.
4. Give 5 Bhavanas (trituration-drying cycles) with Panchakola Kashaya (5-pungent-herb decoction).
5. Then give 10 more Bhavanas with Araluvagra (Cassia fistula/Amaltas) juice.
6. Dry well and form pills of 500 mg each. Store in glass vessel. This is Sheeghra Prabhava Rasa.
anupana: Shunthee (dry ginger) and Mustaka (Cyperus rotundus) decoction

name: Vahni Jvala Vati
benefits: Grahani, Rajayakshma, Mandaagni, Sootikaaroga
ingredients:
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	40 g
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	40 g
Abhraka Bhasma (अभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated Mica 	10 g
Trikatu Churna (त्रिकटुचूर्ण)	Shunthee + Maricha + Pippali powder (combined)	20 g
Matulunga Nimbu Rasa (मातुलुङ्ग रस)	Citrus medica (Citron/Lemon) juice — 7 drying cycles for Abhraka	As required
Trishuli / Shivalingi (त्रिशूली)	Bryonia laciniosa — Bhavana juice	As required
Bhringaraja (भृङ्गराज)	Eclipta alba — Bhavana juice	As required
Chhaageri (चाङ्गेरी)	Oxalis corniculata — Bhavana juice	As required
Thuhar / Sehund (थूहर)	Euphorbia neriifolia — Bhavana latex	As required
Tiksnaparkika (तीक्ष्णपर्णिका)	Sharp-leafed herb — Bhavana juice	As required
Shvetaaparaajita (श्वेताऽपराजिता)	White Clitoria ternatea — Bhavana juice	As required
Ghritkumari / Kanya (घृतकुमारी)	Aloe vera — Bhavana juice	As required
Matsyaakshi (मत्स्याक्षी)	Alternanthera sessilis — Bhavana juice	As required
Greeshmasundara (ग्रीष्मसुन्दर)	A summer seasonal herb — Bhavana juice	As required
Kirani / Karnmooti (किरणी/कर्णमोटी)	Heliotropium indicum — Bhavana juice	As required
Rudanti (रुदन्ती)	Cressa cretica — Bhavana juice	As required
Chitraka Moola (चित्रकमूल)	Plumbago zeylanica root — Bhavana juice/decoction	As required
Ardraka (आर्द्रक)	Zingiber officinale (fresh ginger) — Bhavana juice	As required
Dhatura (धतूरपञ्चाङ्ग)	Datura metel (5-part) — Bhavana juice	As required
Kakamachi (काकमाची)	Solanum nigrum — Bhavana juice	As required
Musali (मुसली)	Curculigo orchioides — Bhavana juice	As required
preparation:
1. In a clean stone mortar, Triturate Shu. Parada 40g and Shu. Gandhaka 40g firmly until Nashta Pishta (fully black, no mercury visible) kajjali is formed.
2. Separately, triturate Abhraka Bhasma 10g with Matulunga (lemon/citron) juice and dry — repeat 7 times (7 Bhavanas).
3. Add this processed Abhraka to the kajjali. Add Trikatu Churna 20g. Triturate all together.
4. Give 1–1 Bhavana (trituration + drying) from each of the 17 botanical juices listed above — giving each juice one full Bhavana in sequence.
5. Form pills of 500 mg (Dvipala matra). Dry in shade and store in glass vessel. This is Vahni Jvala Vati.
anupana: Betel leaf (Tambula Patra) coated with Trikatu Churna

name: Grahani Gajkeshari Rasa
benefits: Grahani (IBS/malabsorption) and Atisara (Diarrhoea
ingredients:
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	12 g
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	12 g
Kaurdi Bhasma (कौड़ीभस्म)	Incinerated Cowrie shell 	12 g
Svarna Maakshika Bhasma (स्वर्णमाक्षिकभस्म)	Incinerated Gold-Iron Pyrite (Chalcopyrite)	12 g
Shu. Gandhaka (extra) (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur (for second processing)	12 g
Maakshika Bhasma (माक्षिकभस्म)	Incinerated Honey-wax  / Iron Pyrite 	12 g
Abhraka Bhasma (अभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated Mica  (fully processed)	46 g
Shu. Vatsanabha Churna (शु. वत्सनाभचूर्ण)	Purified Aconitum ferox powder	12 g
Ativisha Churna (अतिविषाचूर्ण)	Aconitum heterophyllum powder	12 g
Duraalabha Churna (दुरालभाचूर्ण)	Fagonia cretica powder	12 g
Mocharas Churna (मोचरसचूर्ण)	Bombax malabaricum resin powder	12 g
Bhrishtajeeraka Churna (भृष्टजीरकचूर्ण)	Roasted Cumin powder	12 g
Jayanti Svaras (जयन्तीस्वरस)	Sesbania sesban juice — for Bhavana	As required
Jalipippali / Hastishundi (हस्तिशुण्डी)	Heliotropium indicum — for Bhavana	As required
Ashwagandha (अश्वगन्ध)	Withania somnifera — for Bhavana	As required
Panchakola Kashaya (पञ्चकोलकषाय)	5-pungent-herb decoction — for Bhavana	As required
preparation:
1. In a clean stone mortar, firmly Triturate Shu. Parada and Shu. Gandhaka (12g each) to make good kajjali. Add more Gandhaka and Kaurdi Bhasma and Svarna Maakshika Bhasma — mix well.
2. Place 20–20g portions of kajjali in a ghee-coated iron vessel (Loha Darvi). Heat on charcoal fire with stirring until it melts. Pour the melted kajjali onto a Vitasti-measure (approx. 2–3 kg) cow dung patty with ghee-coated banana leaf pieces (3–4 layers) on top. Form a Pottali (pouch) from banana leaf with cow dung (250g) and press the melted kajjali onto it, forming a flat Parpati disc.
3. After cooling, break the Parpati and Triturate it to fine powder in a mortar. Add all Bhasmas (Svarna Maakshika etc.) and all the herb powders. Triturate well.
4. Give 1–1 Bhavana each from: Jayanti svaras, Jalipippali (Hastishundi), Ashwagandha, and Panchakola Kashaya.
5. Form pills of 375 mg. Dry in shade and store in glass vessel. This is Grahani Gajkeshari Rasa — as composed by Acharya Nandi.
anupana: Ghrita (Ghee) and Shunthee Churna (dry ginger powder)

name: Grahani Kapat Rasa - 2
benefits: Shankha Bhasma (Conch shell ash) and Madhu (Honey) —lick
ingredients:
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	Equal part
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	Equal part
Ativisha Churna (अतिविषाचूर्ण)	Aconitum heterophyllum powder	Equal part
Haritaki Churna (हरीतकीचूर्ण)	Terminalia chebula (Haritaki) powder	Equal part
Abhraka Bhasma (अभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated Mica 	Equal part
Yavakshara Churna (यवक्षारचूर्ण)	Barley alkali powder	Equal part
Tankana Kshara Churna (टङ्कणक्षारचूर्ण)	Borax (calcined) powder	Equal part
Mocharas Churna (मोचरसचूर्ण)	Bombax malabaricum resin powder	Equal part
Vacha Churna (वचचूर्ण)	Acorus calamus powder	Equal part
Bhanga / Jaya Churna (भांगचूर्ण)	Cannabis sativa (Vijaya/Jayanti) powder	Equal part
Jambiri Nimbu Rasa (जम्बीरी रस)	Citrus limon (lemon) juice — for Triturate	As required
preparation:
1. In a clean stone mortar, prepare good kajjali from Shu. Parada and Shu. Gandhaka.
2. Add all other powders (Ativisha, Haritaki, Abhraka Bhasma, Yavakshara, Tankana Kshara, Mocharas, Vacha, Bhanga) to the kajjali. Mix well.
3. Triturate all together with Jambiri lemon juice (one Bhavana). Triturate for one full day.
4. Form pills of 3–3 Ratti (375 mg) each. Dry well in shade and store in glass vessel. This is Grahani Kapat Rasa.

name: Vajradhar Rasa
benefits: Grahani, Amlapitta, Pakti Shoola, Kasa, Mandaagni
ingredients:
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	2g each (or 20g each for larger batch)
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	2g equal
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated Copper 	2g equal
Abhraka Bhasma (अभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated Mica 	2g equal
Yavakshara (यवक्षार)	Barley (Hordeum vulgare) alkali	2g equal
Sarja Kshara (सर्जिक्षार)	Terminalia arjuna bark alkali / Sodium bicarbonate	2g equal
Tankana Kshara (टङ्कणक्षार)	Borax (calcined) alkali	2g equal
Varuna Tvak Churna (वरुणत्वक्चूर्ण)	Crataeva nurvala bark powder	2g equal
Vasa Churna (वासाचूर्ण)	Adhatoda vasica (Malabar nut) powder	2g equal
Apamarga Kshara (अपामार्गक्षार)	Achyranthes aspera (Chirchira) alkali	2g equal
Saindhava Lavana (सैन्धवलवण)	Rock Salt	2g equal
Chhaageri Svaras (चाङ्गेरीस्वरस)	Oxalis corniculata juice — for 1 Bhavana	As required
Hastishundi Svaras (हस्तिशुण्डीस्वरस)	Heliotropium indicum juice — for 1 Bhavana	As required
preparation:
1. In a clean stone mortar, firmly Triturate Shu. Parada and Shu. Gandhaka to make kajjali. Add both Bhasmas (Tamra and Abhraka) and all powdered herb-alkali ingredients. Triturate well.
2. Give 1 Bhavana with Chhaageri (Oxalis corniculata) svaras and 1 Bhavana with Hastishundi (Heliotropium indicum) svaras. Dry after each Bhavana.
3. Perform Sharava Samputa — seal in an earthen dish assembly — and fire in Kapota Puta (light/pigeon firing). Allow to cool completely.
4. Next day, open the samputa, take out the medicine, Triturate in mortar. Give one more Bhavana with Chhaageri svaras.
5. Form pills of 125 mg each. Dry in sunlight and store in glass vessel. This is Vajradhar Rasa. (Minimum practical pill: 250 mg.)
anupana: Kaanjika for Grahani; Aardra svaras for Shoola/Kasa; Dharosna Dugdha for Amlapitta

name: Gandhakadi Pottali Rasa - 1
benefits: Yakrit-Pleeha Roga (Liver and Spleen diseases)
ingredients:
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	Equal part
Shu. Haritala (शु. हरताल)	Purified Orpiment (Arsenic trisulphide)	Equal part
Svarna Maakshika Bhasma (स्वर्णमाक्षिकभस्म)	Incinerated Gold-Iron Pyrite (Chalcopyrite) 	Equal part
Shu. Manashila (शु. मन:शिला)	Purified Realgar (Arsenic disulphide)	Equal part
Pippali Kaatha (पिप्पलीकाथ)	Piper longum decoction — for 7 Bhavanas	As required
Snuhi Ksheera (स्नुहीक्षीर)	Euphorbia neriifolia latex — for 7 Bhavanas	As required
Gomutra (गोमूत्र)	Cow's urine — for 7 Bhavanas	As required
preparation: 1. Place all four equal-part ingredients in a mortar and triturate together.\n2. Give 7 Bhavanas each with: Pippali Kaatha (long pepper decoction), Snuhi Ksheera (Euphorbia latex) and Gomutra (cow's urine) — total 7 drying cycles.\n3. After drying,Triturate to very fine powder (Sookshma Churna) and store in a glass vessel. This is Gandhakadi Pottali Rasa.
anupana: Goghrita + Makshika (lick); then drink Panchakola Phaant

name: Vaishvanara Rasa
benefits: All types of Gulma (Vataja, Pittaja, Kafaja)
ingredients:
Aparajita Moola Churna (अपराजितामूलचूर्ण)	Clitoria ternatea root powder	1 part
Shu. Jayapala (शु. जयपाल)	Purified Croton tiglium (Croton seeds)	1 part
Shu. Kilihari Churna (शु. किलिहारीचूर्ण)	Purified Gloriosa superba (Flame lily) powder	1 part
Devadali Churna (देवदालिचूर्ण)	Luffa echinata (bitter gourd) powder	1 part
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	8 parts
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	8 parts
Yavichigna / Ksheeri Vriksha Svaras (यवचिघ्नाम्बु)	Barley-alkali water / Ksheeri Vriksha (latex-yielding tree) juice — for 15-day trituration	As required
preparation:
1. In a clean stone mortar, firmlyTriturate Shu. Parada and Shu. Gandhaka (8 parts each) to prepare good kajjali.
2. Add the four herb powders (Aparajita moola, Jayapala, Kilihari, Devadali — 1 part each) to the kajjali. Mix all together.
3. Triturate continuously with Yavichigna (barley alkali water) or Ksheeri Vriksha svaras for 15 days (paksha — half month).
4. After drying, perform Sharava Samputa and fire in Bhoodhara Puta. After cooling, open and remove the medicine.
5. Form pills of 3–3 Ratti (375 mg). Dry in shade and store in glass vessel. This is Vaishvanara Rasa.
anupana: Warm water + Goghrita + Saindhava (general); Honey + ginger (Vata/Kapha); Misri + Honey (Pitta)

name: Vangeshvara Rasa
benefits: Gulma (abdominal tumour), Pleeha (spleen disease), Udara Roga (abdominal diseases)
ingredients:
Rasasindura (रससिन्दूर)	Purified Mercuric Sulphide (processed)	46 g
Vanga Bhasma (वङ्गभस्म)	Incinerated Tin 	46 g
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	46 g
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated Copper 	46 g
Arka Ksheera (अर्कक्षीर)	Calotropis procera latex — for 1 Bhavana	As required
preparation:
1. In a mortar,Triturate Rasasindura first until very fine. Then add Vanga Bhasma, Shu. Gandhaka and Tamra Bhasma. Triturate all together.
2. Give 1 Bhavana with Arka Ksheera (Calotropis procera latex). Triturate well and form a ball. Dry.
3. Place in Sharava Samputa (sealed earthen dish assembly) and fire in Bhoodhara Yantra (one Puta). After cooling, remove the medicine.
4.Triturate to fine powder. Store in glass vessel. This is Vangeshvara Rasa.
anupana: Goghrita (lick); then Gomutra with Shvetapunarnava Churna or Haldi Churna

name: Agnikumar Rasa - 1
benefits: Gulma, Yakrit, Pandu, Vibandha, Shoola, Agnimaandya, Jvara, Jalodara
ingredients:
Shu. Jayapala (शु. जयपाल)	Purified Croton tiglium seeds	1 part
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	1 part
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	1 part
Amalaki Churna (आमलकीचूर्ण)	Emblica officinalis (Amla/Indian gooseberry) powder	1 part
Haritaki Churna (हरीतकीचूर्ण)	Terminalia chebula (Haritaki) powder	1 part
Bibhitaka Churna (बिभीतकचूर्ण)	Terminalia bellerica (Bahera) powder	1 part
Shunthee Churna (शुण्ठीचूर्ण)	Zingiber officinale (dry ginger) powder	1 part
Maricha Churna (मिर्चचूर्ण)	Piper nigrum (black pepper) powder	1 part
Pippali Churna (पिप्पलीचूर्ण)	Piper longum (long pepper) powder	1 part
Gomutra (गोमूत्र)	Cow's urine (1/16th ratio) — for 3-day trituration	16x the total weight
preparation: 1. In a clean stone mortar, firmlyTriturate Shu. Parada and Shu. Gandhaka to prepare kajjali. Then add Shu. Jayapala and all 6 herb powders (Amalaki, Haritaki, Bibhitaka, Shunthee, Maricha, Pippali).\n2. Triturate all 9 substances together with Gomutra (cow's urine — at 16 times the medicine quantity) for 3 days continuously.\n3. Form pills of Jangali Ber size (approx. 375 mg each). Dry well in shade and store in glass vessel. This is Agnikumar Rasa.

name: Deepatamara Rasa
benefits: Pittaja Gulma
ingredients:
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	48 g
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	48 g
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated Copper 	48 g
Shaakavriksha Panchaanga Kaatha (शाकवृक्षपञ्चाङ्गकाथ)	Moringa oleifera (Drumstick tree) 5-part decoction — for 3-day Bhavana	As required
Sarpaakshi Svaras (सर्पाक्षीस्वरस)	Ophiorrhiza mungos / Rauvolfia serpentina juice — for Bhavanas	As required
Shu. Jayapala (शु. जयपाल)	Purified Croton tiglium — added equal to final medicine weight	Equal to final processed weight
dose: 250 mg (2 Gunja) — but give 125 mg due to Jayapala content
preparation:
1. In a clean mortar, prepare kajjali from Shu. Parada and Shu. Gandhaka (48g each). Add Tamra Bhasma (48g) and triturate all together.
2. Give 3 days of Bhavana with Shaakavriksha Panchaanga Kaatha (Moringa tree 5-part decoction). Then give 1 day Bhavana with Sarpaakshi svaras.
3. After drying, perform Sharava Samputa and fire in Gaja Puta (large firing). After cooling, remove andTriturate.
4. Give 5 more Bhavanas with Sarpaakshi svaras, each followed by drying. Then fire 5 more times in Bhoodhara Puta.
5. After the final firing, remove, weigh the medicine and add an equal weight of Shu. Jayapala (purified Croton tiglium).
6. Form pills of 1 Ratti (125 mg) each. Dry in shade and store in glass vessel. This is Deepatamara Rasa.

name: Gulmanashana Ras
benefits: Gulmanashana Ras at Shaanaardha (approximately 1.5-2 gm) dose with Ushnodaka (warm water) rapidly treats significantlys: Pancha Gulma (all five types of Gulma — Vataja, Pittaja, Kaphaja, Sannipataja and Raktaja), Kshaya (tuberculosis/wasting disease), Shwasa (asthma/breathlessness) and Mandagni (weak digestive fire).
ingredients:
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	1 part
Shu. Gandhak (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	1 part (equal to Parada)
Saindhava Lavana (सैन्धवलवण)	Rock Salt	2 parts
Shu. Tankana (शु. टङ्कण)	Purified Borax	3 parts
Shu. Tutha Bhasma (शु. तुत्यभस्म)	Incinerated Purified Copper Sulphate	4 parts
Varatika Bhasma (वराटिकाभस्म)	Incinerated Cowrie Shell 	5 parts
Shankha Bhasma (शंखभस्म)	Incinerated Conch Shell 	6 parts
Maricha Churna (मरिचचूर्ण)	Black Pepper powder — added after Puta, equal to final medicine weight	Equal to final weight
Chitraka Mool Kashaya (चित्रकमूलक्वाथ)	Plumbago zeylanica root decoction — for Bhavana	As required
Karanja Mool Kashaya (करञ्जमूलक्वाथ)	Pongamia pinnata root decoction — for Bhavana	As required
Chirabila Svaras (चिरबिलस्वरस)	Holoptelea integrifolia juice — for Bhavana	As required
Ardraka Svaras (आर्द्रकस्वरस)	Fresh Ginger juice — for 3 Bhavanas + 3 Putas	As required
preparation: 1. Make Kajjali from Shu. Parada and Shu. Gandhaka. Add Saindhava Lavana through Shankha Bhasma — all ingredients in sequence. Mix well.\n2. Give Bhavana with Chitraka Mool Kashaya — 3 Bhavanas and 3 Kukkutaputa firings. Then give 3 Bhavanas with Karanja Mool Kashaya and 3 Kukkutaputa firings. Then give 3 Bhavanas with Ardraka Svaras and 3 Kukkutaputa firings.\n3. After final Puta: weigh the resulting medicine. Add exactly equal weight of Maricha Churna (black pepper powder). Mix well and store in glass.

name: Raktodar Kuthar Ras
benefits: Raktodar Kuthar Ras treats significantlys: Jalodar (ascites — fluid in abdomen) in women and Raktodar (blood ascites). Also acts as a powerful purgative (Tiksna Virechana).
ingredients:
Rasa Sindura (रससिन्दूर)	Processed Mercury / Red Mercuric Sulphide	Equal part
Shu. Tuttha (शु. तुत्थ)	Purified Copper Sulphate (Blue Vitriol)	Equal part
Shu. Jaipala (शु. जयपाल)	Purified Croton tiglium (croton/jamalgota) seeds	Equal part
Pippali Churna (पिप्पलीचूर्ण)	Piper longum (Long Pepper) powder	Equal part
Aarragvadha Phala Majja Jal (आरग्वधफलमज्जाजल)	Cassia fistula (golden shower) fruit pulp water — for Bhavana	As required
Snuhi Ksheera (स्नुहीक्षीर)	Euphorbia nerifolia milk sap — for Bhavana	As required
preparation:
1. In a stone mortar,Triturate Rasa Sindura first. Add Shu. Tuttha, Shu. Jaipala and Pippali Churna in equal parts. Mix andTriturate well.
2. Give 1 Bhavana with Aarragvadha Phala Majja aqueous solution (Cassia fistula pulp water). Dry.
3. Give 1 Bhavana with Snuhi Ksheera (Euphorbia milk). Dry.
4. Form very small tablets of 1 Ratti (125 mg) each. Dry in shade and store in glass.
anupana: Supakka Imli Phala Jaliya Ghol (Ripe tamarind fruit water solution)

name: Sarvanga Sundar Ras / Praneshwara Ras
benefits: Gulma, Shula, Pleeha Vriddhi, Pandu, Kamala, Grahani
ingredients:
Krishna Vajra Abhraka Bhasma (कृष्णवज्राभ्रकभस्म)	Black mica (Vajra Abhraka)	1 part
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	1 part
Shu. Gandhak (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	1 part
Taala Mooli Ras (तालमूलीरस)	A specific root juice — for Bhavana before Kupi	As required
Shwetmusali Kwath (श्वेतमुसलीक्वाथ)	Chlorophytum borivilianum decoction — for sealing Kupi ball	As required
Three Kshara (alkali salts): Yavakshara, Sarjikshara, Tankhanakshara		Equal to Parada weight
Five Lavana (salts): Saindhava, Sauvarchala, Samudra, Vidala Lavana, Sambhara Lavana		Equal to Parada weight
Thirteen Herb Powders (1-1 part each): Shunthi Churna, Maricha Churna, Pippali Churna, Amalaki Churna, Haritaki Churna, Bibhitaka Churna, Shu. Hingu, Shu. Guggul, Indrayava Churna, Gunja Bija Churna, Chitraka Mool Churna, Ajamoda Churna, Ajwain Churna		Equal to Parada weight
preparation: Stage 1 — Kupipakwa: Make Kajjali from equal parts Parada, Gandhaka and Krishna Vajra Abhraka Bhasma. Triturate with Taala Mooli Ras to make Kalka. Form ball, knead with Shwetmusali Kwath. Place in glass Kupi, seal mouth with chalk and clay. Coat with clay, dry in sunlight. Dig pit in ground, place Kupi and fire in Puta. Next day when Svanga Sheetal, remove and break glass Kupi to extract medicine.\nStage 2 — Post-Kupi: Weigh the extracted medicine. Take each of the 21 substances (3 Kshara + 5 Lavana + 13 herb powders) equal to this weight. Powder all finely. Mix all 21 powders with the Kupi medicine.Triturate firmly and store in glass.\nThis is Sarvanga Sundar Ras (also called Praneshwara Ras).

name: Shikhivadva Ras
benefits: Vataja Gulma.
ingredients:
Rasa Sindura (रससिन्दूर)	Processed Mercury / Red Mercuric Sulphide	Equal part
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated Copper	Equal part
Abhraka Bhasma (अभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated Mica	Equal part
Shu. Gandhak (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	Equal part
Svarna Makshika Bhasma (स्वर्णमाक्षिकभस्म)	Chalcopyrite/Iron Pyrite	Equal part
Ardraka Svaras (आर्द्रकस्वरस)	Fresh Ginger juice — for 1-day Bhavana	As required
Yavakshara Drava (यवक्षारद्रव)	Barley alkali solution — for 1-day Bhavana	As required
Vidanga (विडङ्ग)	Embelia ribes (false black pepper)	1 Karsha (12 gm)
Dadima (दाडिम)	Pomegranate (Punica granatum)	1 Karsha
Hingu (हिंगु)	Asafoetida (Ferula assa-foetida)	1 Karsha
Saindhava (सैन्धव)	Rock Salt	1 Karsha
Ela (एला)	Cardamom (Elettaria cardamomum)	1 Karsha
Sauvarchala (सौवर्चल)	Sauvarchala salt (black salt type)	1 Karsha
Matulunga Ras (मातुलुङ्गरस)	Citrus medica (citron) juice — forTriturateing Anupana	As required
Sura (सुरा)	Fermented liquid/medicated wine	As required
dose: 375 mg(3 Gunja) daily in betel leaf
preparation:
1.Triturate Rasa Sindura finely in a stone mortar. Add Abhraka Bhasma, Tamra Bhasma, Shu. Gandhaka and Svarna Makshika Bhasma in equal parts andTriturate.
2. Give 1 Bhavana with Yavakshara Drava (barley alkali solution) and 1 Bhavana with Ardraka Svaras (fresh ginger juice).
3. Form tablets of 375 mg each. Dry in shade and store in glass. This is Shikhivadva Ras.
4. For Anupana preparation:Triturate Vidanga, Dadima, Hingu, Saindhava, Ela and Sauvarchala (1 Karsha each) with Matulunga Ras. Take with Sura (fermented liquid).
anupana: Vidanga + Dadima + Hingu + Saindhava + Ela + Sauvarchala with Matulunga Ras — taken with Sura (fermented liquid).

name: Vidyadhara Ras
benefits: Gulma (Abdominal Tumor) and Pleeha Roga (Spleen Disorders)
ingredients:
Shu. Gandhak (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	Equal part
Shu. Haritala (शु. हरताल)	Purified Orpiment (Arsenic trisulphide)	Equal part
Svarna Makshika Bhasma (स्वर्णमाक्षिकभस्म)	Chalcopyrite/Iron Pyrite 	Equal part
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated Copper   (Mruta)	Equal part
Shu. Manaahshila (शु. मन:शिला)	Purified Realgar (Arsenic disulphide)	Equal part
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	Equal part
Pippali Kashaya (पिप्पलीक्वाथ)	Piper longum (Long Pepper) decoction — for 1 Bhavana	As required
Snuhi Ksheera (स्नुहीक्षीर)	Euphorbia nerifolia milk sap — for 1 Bhavana	As required
preparation:
1. Make Kajjali from Shu. Parada and Shu. Gandhaka. Add Shu. Haritala, Svarna Makshika Bhasma, Tamra Bhasma and Shu. Manaahshila in equal parts. Mix all andTriturate together for one day.
2. Give 1 Bhavana with Pippali Kashaya (long pepper decoction). Dry.
3. Give 1 Bhavana with Snuhi Ksheera (Euphorbia milk). Dry.
4. Form tablets of 375 mg each. Dry in shade and store in glass. This is Vidyadhara Ras.
anupana: Madhu (Honey); followed by 50 ml Gau Mutra (Cow's Urine)

name: Hridayarnav Rasa
benefits: Kaphajanit Hridaya Roga
ingredients:
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	1 part
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	1 part
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Copper ash	Equal to combined Para + Gandhaka (3 parts total)
Triphala Kaatha (त्रिफलाकाथ)	Decoction of Haritaki + Vibhitaka + Amalaki — for Triturate	As required
Kakamachi Svaras (काकमाचीस्वरस)	Solanum nigrum (Makoy) juice — for Triturate	As required
Anupana Preparation (Kakamachi fruit, Haritaki, Vibhitaka, Amalaki 1 Karsha each boiled in 32 tolas water reduced to 4 tolas)	For decoction Anupana	As required
preparation:
1. Prepare kajjali from equal parts of Shu. Parada and Shu. Gandhaka in a stone mortar.
2. Add Tamra Bhasma (equal to the combined weight of Para + Gandhaka). Mix well.
3. Triturate the mixture with Triphala decoction for one day. Then give one more day of trituration with Kakamachi (Makoy) svaras.
4. Form pills of Chana-matra (chickpea size, approx. 125–250 mg). Dry in shade and store in glass vessel. This is Hridayarnav Rasa.
anupana: Kakamachi-Triphala decoction (Makoy + Haritaki + Baheda + Amla boiled in water)

name: Nagarjunabhra
benefits: Nagarjunabhra treats significantlys: Hridaya Roga (heart disease of all types), all Shoola (pain/colic), Arsha (haemorrhoids), nausea, hiccough, Chardi (vomiting), Aruchi (anorexia), Atisara (diarrhoea), Mandaagni (weak digestion), Raktapitta (haemorrhagic disorders), Kshaya (tuberculosis/wasting), Shotha (oedema), Udara Roga (abdominal/liver diseases), Amlapitta (hyperacidity/GERD), Vishama Jvara (intermittent/malarial fever) and many other diseases. It is a Baladayaka (strength-giving), Vrishya (aphrodisiac) and Rasayana (rejuvenating) medicine.
ingredients: Vajra Abhraka Bhasma (वज्राभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated Mica — processed through Sahasra Puta (1000 firings)	As required (primary ingredient)\nArjuna Tvak Svaras / Kaatha (अर्जुनत्वक्)	Terminalia arjuna bark juice or decoction — for 7-day trituration	As required
preparation: 1. Take Vajra Abhraka Bhasma (Incinerated Mica processed through Sahasra Puta — 1000 firing cycles — as per classical preparation method).\n2. In a stone mortar, triturate the Vajra Abhraka Bhasma with Arjuna Tvak (Terminalia arjuna bark) juice or decoction for seven consecutive days (one full day of Triturate each day).\n3. Dry in shade. Form pills of 1 Ratti (approx. 125 mg) each. Store in glass vessel. This is Nagarjunabhra.

name: Trinetra Rasa
benefits: All types of Hridaya Roga — Vataja, Pittaja, Kaphaja, Tridoshaja and Krimijanya
ingredients:
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	Equal part
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	Equal part
Abhraka Bhasma (अभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated Mica 	Equal part
Arjuna Tvak Rasa / Kaatha (अर्जुनत्वक्)	Terminalia arjuna bark juice or decoction — for 21 Bhavanas in sunlight	As required
preparation: 1. Take equal parts of Shu. Parada, Shu. Gandhaka and Abhraka Bhasma. Prepare kajjali in a stone mortar.\n2. Give 21 Bhavanas (trituration-drying cycles) in sunlight with Arjuna bark juice (or decoction) — Triturate and sun-drying each time.\n3. After 21 Bhavanas, dry well and store the powder in a glass vessel. This is Trinetra Rasa.
reference: Bharat Bhaishajya Ratnakar, part-2, verse 2726, page no 482

name: Panchanan Rasa
benefits: Hridaya Roga (Heart Disease)
ingredients:
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	Equal part
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	Equal part
Go Dugdha (गोदूध)	Cow's milk — for 1-day Bhavana	As required
Amalaki Svaras (आँवला)	Phyllanthus emblica (Indian Gooseberry) juice — for 1-day Bhavana	As required
Mulethi / Yashtimadhu (मुलेठी)	Glycyrrhiza glabra (Liquorice) decoction — for 1-day Bhavana	As required
Kharjura Rasa (खजूर)	Phoenix dactylifera (Date palm) juice — for 1-day Bhavana	As required
preparation: 1. Prepare kajjali from equal parts of Shu. Parada and Shu. Gandhaka in a stone mortar.\n2. Give 1 Bhavana (trituration-drying) each with: (i) Gostan / cow's milk, (ii) Amalaki juice, (iii) Yashtimadhu decoction and (iv) Kharjura juice — one day with each liquid.\n3. After all Bhavanas, dry well. Form pills of 1 Ratti (125 mg) each. Store in glass vessel. This is Panchanan Rasa.
anupana: Amalaka Churna (Amla powder) and Mishri (Sugar candy)

name: Chintamani Rasa
benefits: all types of Hridaya Roga (heart diseases), all Phupphusa Roga (lung diseases — pneumonia, bronchitis, etc.), all 20 types of Prameha (diabetes and urinary disorders), severe Shvasa (asthma/breathlessness) and the most difficult-to-treat Kasa (cough/tuberculosis). It is Balapushti (strength and nourishment-giving) and Hridya (cardiac tonic).
ingredients:
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	1 part
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	1 part
Abhraka Bhasma (अभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated Mica 	1 part
Loha Bhasma (लोहभस्म)	Incinerated Iron 	1 part
Vanga Bhasma (वङ्गभस्म)	Incinerated Tin	1 part
Shu. Shilajatu (शिलाजीत)	Purified Asphaltum (Shilajit)	1 part
Chandi Bhasma (चांदीभस्म)	Incinerated Silver 	Half part (1/2)
Svarna Bhasma (स्वर्णभस्म)	Incinerated Gold ash	One-quarter part (1/4)
Chitraka Kaatha (चीतेके काथ)	Plumbago zeylanica root decoction — for 7 Bhavanas	As required
Arjuna Kaatha (अर्जुनके काथ)	Terminalia arjuna bark decoction — for 7 Bhavanas	As required
Bhringaraja Svaras (भांगरे स्वरस)	Eclipta alba juice — for 7 Bhavanas	As required
preparation: 1. First prepare kajjali from Shu. Parada and Shu. Gandhaka in a stone mortar. Add Abhraka Bhasma, Loha Bhasma, Vanga Bhasma, Shilajatu, Chandi Bhasma and Svarna Bhasma. Triturate all together well.\n2. Give 7 Bhavanas (trituration-drying cycles) with Chitraka decoction. Then 7 Bhavanas with Bhringaraja svaras. Then 7 Bhavanas with Arjuna bark decoction — each set of 7 separately (total 21 Bhavanas).\n3. Form pills of 1 Ratti (125 mg) each. Dry thoroughly in shade. Store in glass vessel. This is Chintamani Rasa.

name: Trivishtapar Ras
benefits: Hrid Roga (heart diseases) and Shula (colic/pain).
ingredients:
Shu. Hingula (शु. हिंगुल)	Purified Cinnabar (Mercuric Sulphide)	Equal part
Chandi Bhasma (चांदीभस्म)	Incinerated Silver 	Equal part
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated Copper 	Equal part
Abhraka Bhasma (अभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated Mica	Equal to combined above
Shu. Gandhak (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	1/5th of Abhraka Bhasma
Shu. Vatsanabha (शु. वत्सनाभ)	Purified Aconitum ferox (aconite)	1/16th part
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	Double the total above
Jambiri Nimbu Ras (जम्बीरी नींबूरस)	Citron/lemon juice — for 1-day trituration	As required
Trifala Kwath (त्रिफलाक्वाथ)	Trifala decoction — 4x medicine quantity, for cooking	4x medicine
Dashamool Kwath (दशमूलक्वाथ)	Dashamool decoction — 4x, for sequential cooking	4x medicine
Shatavari Ras/Kwath (शतावरीरस/क्वाथ)	Asparagus racemosus juice/decoction — 4x, final cooking	4x medicine
preparation:
1.Triturate Shu. Hingula, Chandi Bhasma and Tamra Bhasma together to make Pitthi (paste). Add Abhraka Bhasma (equal to all three), Shu. Gandhaka (1/5th), Shu. Visha (1/16th) and Shu. Parada (double all others). Mix well.
2. Triturate all together with Jambiri Nimbu Ras (lemon/citron juice) in a stone mortar for 1 day.
3. Place in a clay vessel (Mrinmaya Bhajana). Cook sequentially: first with 4x quantity Trifala Kwath, then with 4x Dashamool Kwath, then with 4x Shatavari Ras/Kwath — each time cooking until condensed.
4. Form tablets of 3 Gunja (375 mg) each. Dry in shade and store in glass. This is Trivishtapar Ras.
reference: Bharat Bhaishajya Ratnakar, part-2,verse 2760, page no 494

name: Prabhakar Vati
benefits: Hrid Roga (all types of heart diseases).
ingredients:
Svarna Makshika Bhasma (स्वर्णमाक्षिकभस्म)	Incinerated Chalcopyrite/Iron Pyrite 	Equal part
Loha Bhasma (लोहभस्म)	Incinerated Iron 	Equal part
Vanshalochana / Tugakshiri (बंसलोचन/तुगाक्षीरी)	Bambusa arundinacea (bamboo) silica/tabasheer	Equal part
Shilajatu (शिलाजतु)	Asphaltum (Shilajit/mineral pitch)	Equal part
Arjuna Tvak Kwath (अर्जुन छाल क्वाथ)	Terminalia arjuna bark decoction — for Bhavana	As required
preparation:
1. Take equal parts of Svorna Makshika Bhasma, Loha Bhasma, Vanshalochana and Shilajatu.
2. Place in a stone mortar andTriturate together well.
3. Give Bhavana with Arjuna Tvak Kwath (Terminalia arjuna bark decoction) for 1 day —Triturate thoroughly.
4. Form tablets of 2 Gunja (250 mg) each. Dry in shade and store in glass. This is Prabhakar Vati.
anupana: Arjuna Tvak Kwath (Terminalia arjuna bark decoction)

name: Vishveshwara Ras
benefits: Phuphuasa Roga (Lung Diseases) and Hrid Roga (Heart Diseases)
ingredients:
Svarna Bhasma (स्वर्णभस्म)	Incinerated Gold 	Equal part (1 Tola)
Abhraka Bhasma (अभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated Mica	Equal part (1 Tola)
Loha Bhasma (लोहभस्म)	Incinerated Iron 	Equal part (1 Tola)
Vajra Bhasma / Hira Bhasma (वज्रभस्म)	Incinerated Diamond 	Equal part (1 Tola)
Rasa Sindura (रससिन्दूर)	Processed Mercury / Red Mercuric Sulphide	Equal part (1 Tola)
Shu. Gandhak (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	Equal part (1 Tola)
Vaikanta Bhasma (वैकान्तभस्म)	Vaikanta metal ash	Equal part (1 Tola)
Arjuna Tvak Kwath (अर्जुनकी छालका क्वाथ)	Terminalia arjuna bark decoction — for Bhavana	As required
preparation: 1. Make Kajjali from Rasa Sindura and Shu. Gandhaka. Add Svorna Bhasma, Abhraka Bhasma, Loha Bhasma, Vajra Bhasma and Vaikanta Bhasma in equal parts. Mix all.\n2. Give Bhavana with Arjuna Tvak Kwath (Terminalia arjuna bark decoction) —Triturate in mortar for 1 day with the decoction.\n3. Form tablets of 1 Ratti (125 mg) each. Dry in shade and store in glass. This is Vishveshwara Ras.
anupana: Arjuna Tvak Kwath (Terminalia arjuna bark decoction)

name: Kalyana Sundar Ras
benefits: Uras Toya (Udara Stoya — hydrothorax/pleural effusion — fluid filling in the chest/lung covering), Hrid Roga (heart diseases), Vaksho Vata Roga (Vata disorders of the chest), Uras Srak (haemoptysis — blood spitting from chest) and Phuphuasa Roga (lung/pulmonary diseases).
ingredients:
Rasa Sindura (रससिन्दूर)	Processed Mercury / Red Mercuric Sulphide	Equal part
Abhraka Bhasma (अभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated Mica	Equal part
Chandi Bhasma / Tara Bhasma (चांदीभस्म)	Incinerated Silver 	Equal part
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated Copper 	Equal part
Svarna Bhasma (सोनाभस्म)	Incinerated Gold 	Equal part
Shu. Hingula (शु. हिंगुल/शिंगरफ)	Purified Cinnabar (Mercuric Sulphide)	Equal part
Chitraka Kwath (चीतेके क्वाथ)	Plumbago zeylanica decoction — for initial trituration	As required
Hastishunda Svaras (हाथीसूंडी रस)	Heliotropium indicum (elephant trunk plant) juice — 7 Bhavanas	As required
preparation: 1. Place all six ingredients in equal parts in a stone mortar. Triturate with Chitraka Kwath (Plumbago decoction) —Triturate well.\n2. Give 7 Bhavanas with Hastishunda Svaras (Heliotropium indicum juice).\n3. Form tablets of 1 Gunja (125 mg) each. Dry in shade and store in glass. This is Kalyana Sundar Ras.

name: Shankar Vati
benefits: all Phuphuasa (lung) diseases, all Hridaya (heart) diseases, Jeerna Jwara (chronic fever), all 20 types of Prameha (urinary disorders including diabetes), Kasa (cough), Shwasa (asthma), Amavata (rheumatoid arthritis) and even severe Grahani (malabsorption/IBS). It increases Bala (strength) and Pushti (body nourishment).
ingredients:
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	4 parts (2 Tola)
Shu. Gandhak (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	8 parts (8 Tola)
Loha Bhasma (लोहभस्म)	Incinerated Iron 	3 parts (3 Tola)
Naga Bhasma (नागभस्म)	Incinerated Lead 	2 parts (2 Tola)
Kakamachi Svaras	Solanum nigrum (black nightshade) juice	1 Bhavana
Chitraka Tvak Kwath	Plumbago zeylanica bark decoction	1 Bhavana
Ardraka Svaras	Fresh Ginger juice	1 Bhavana
Jayanti Patta Svaras	Sesbania sesban leaf juice	1 Bhavana
Vasa Patta Svaras	Adhatoda vasica (Malabar nut) leaf juice	1 Bhavana
Bilva Tvak Kwath	Aegle marmelos (bael) bark decoction	1 Bhavana
Arjuna Tvak Kwath	Terminalia arjuna bark decoction	1 Bhavana
preparation: 1. Make Kajjali from Shu. Parada (4 parts) and Shu. Gandhaka (8 parts). Add Loha Bhasma (3 parts) and Naga Bhasma (2 parts).Triturate all together.\n2. Give one Bhavana each with all 7 Bhavana substances (Kakamachi Svaras, Chitraka Kwath, Ardraka Svaras, Jayanti Svaras, Vasa Svaras, Bilva Kwath, Arjuna Kwath) —Triturate well after each.\n3. Form tablets of 2 Gunja (250 mg = 2 Ratti) each. Dry in shade and store in glass. This is Shankar Vati — as proclaimed by Lord Shiva.

name: Mahajwarankush Rasa
benefits: all eight types of Jvara (fever): Ekaahika (daily fever), Dvaahika (alternate-day fever), Tryaahika (tertian/3-day fever), Chaturthaka (quartan/4-day fever), Vishama Jvara (irregular/malarial fever) and Tridosha Jvara (fever from all three Doshas)
ingredients:
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	1 part
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	1 part
Shu. Visha / Vatsanabha (शु. विष)	Purified Aconitum ferox	1 part
Shu. Dhatura Beeja (धत्तूरबीज)	Purified Datura metel seeds	3 parts (three times each of the above)
Vyosha / Trikatu Churna (व्योष चूर्ण)	Shunthee + Pippali + Maricha (Trikatu) combined powder	Double of all 4 above combined (12 parts)
Jambiri Nimbu Majja (जम्बीरीमज्जा)	Citrus limon (lemon) pulp — Anupana	As required
Adraka Svaras (अदरक स्वरस)	Zingiber officinale (fresh ginger) juice — Anupana	As required
preparation:
1. Take equal parts of Shu. Parada, Shu. Gandhaka and Shu. Visha. Prepare kajjali by Triturate together.
2. Add Shu. Dhatura Beeja (3 parts — three times each of the above) and Vyosha / Trikatu Churna (double the combined total of all four).
3. Triturate all together with water and form pills of 2 Gunja (240 mg). For children: pills of Sarshapa (mustard seed) size.
4. Store in glass vessel. This is Mahajwarankush Rasa.
anupana: Jambiri lemon pulp and fresh Adraka (ginger) juice

name: Chandroday Rasa
benefits: Jvara (all fevers including chronic/Jeerna Jvara), Kasa (cough), Shvasa (asthma/breathlessness), Unmada (insanity/mental disorders) and Dhanur Vata (tetanus/spasm disorders).
ingredients:
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	1 part
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	1 part
Shu. Vanga (शु. वङ्ग)	Purified Tin	1 part
Abhraka Bhasma (अभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated Mica	1 part
Jambiri Nimbu Svaras (जम्बीरी रस)	Lemon juice — for 7 Bhavanas + 7 Putas	As required
Kumari Svaras (कुमारीस्वरस)	Aloe vera juice — for 7 Bhavanas + 7 Putas	As required
Chitra Moola Svaras (चित्रकमूलस्वरस)	Plumbago zeylanica root juice — for 7 Bhavanas + 7 Putas	As required
preparation:
1. In a stone mortar, place Shu. Parada. In an iron ladle, melt Shu. Vanga (tin) and quickly pour it into the mortar with mercury. Triturate rapidly until amalgamated. Add Shu. Gandhaka and Triturate firmly to make good kajjali. Add Abhraka Bhasma and mix well.
2. Give Bhavana with Jambiri lemon juice and fire in Laghu Puta. Repeat this (Bhavana + Puta) 7 times total.
3. Then give 7 more Bhavanas and Laghu Putas with Kumari (Aloe vera) svaras.
4. Then give 7 more Bhavanas and Laghu Putas with Chitraka moola svaras. Total: 21 Bhavana-Puta cycles.
5. Store as powder or form pills of 250 mg. Dry in shade and store in glass vessel. This is Chandroday Rasa.
anupana: Guda + Bhrishtajeeraka (for fever); Kumari / Triphala (for Kasa-Shvasa); Guduchi Kaatha (for Unmada-Dhanur Vata)

name: Mrityunjaya Rasa
benefits: All types of Jvara — Vataja, Sannipata, Ajeerna, Vishama and Mrityu-Rupa Jvara
ingredients:
Shu. Visha / Vatsanabha (शु. विष)	Purified Aconitum ferox — purified in cow's urine, dried in shade	1 part
Maricha (मारीच)	Black Pepper (Piper nigrum)	1 part
Pippali (पिप्पली)	Long Pepper (Piper longum)	1 part
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	1 part
Shu. Tankana (शु. टङ्कण)	Purified Borax	1 part
Shu. Hingula (शु. हिंगुल)	Purified Cinnabar — purified in Jambiri lemon juice	2 parts (double)
Shu. Parada (optional) (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury — if added (1 part), Hingula may be omitted	1 part (optional)
preparation:
1. Purify Visha (Aconite) by soaking and washing in Gomutra (cow's urine); dry in shade.
2. Purify Hingula by Triturate thoroughly with Jambiri lemon juice (if Parada is not used separately).
3. Triturate all ingredients together in a mortar: Visha (1 part), Maricha (1 part), Pippali (1 part), Gandhaka (1 part), Tankana (1 part) and Hingula (2 parts).
4. Form pills of Mudga-matra (green gram/Moong bean size, approx. 60–125 mg each). Store in glass vessel. This is Mrityunjaya Rasa.
anupana: All fevers (general): Lick with Honey (Madhu).Vata Jvara : Dadhi-udaka (curd-water).Severe Sannipata Jvara (all-Dosha fever): Adraka (fresh ginger) juice. Ajeerna Jvara : Jambiri lemon juice. Vishama Jvara (intermittent/malarial fever): Ajaji (cumin) with old Jaggery (Purana Guda).

name: Swachhanda Bhairav Rasa
benefits: Sannipata Jvara (Three-Dosha fever) and Nava Jvara (New/Acute fever)
ingredients:
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated Copper 	Equal part
Shu. Visha / Vatsanabha (शु. विष)	Purified Aconitum ferox	Equal part
Swarna Rasa / Liquid (स्वर्ण रस)	Gold-processed liquid — for 100 Bhavanas	As required
Adraka Svaras (अदरक स्वरस)	Zingiber officinale (fresh ginger) juice — Anupana	As required
Shakara (शक्कर)	Sugar — Anupana	As required
Saindhava Lavana (सेन्धा नमक)	Rock Salt — Anupana	As required
preparation: 1. Take equal parts of Tamra Bhasma and Shu. Visha (purified Aconite). Triturate together in a mortar.\n2. Give 100 Bhavanas (trituration-drying cycles) with Svarna-processed liquid (gold juice). Triturate and dry each time.\n3. Form pills of half Gunja (60 mg) each. Store in glass vessel. This is Swachhanda Bhairav Rasa.
anupana: Adraka (ginger) juice + Shakara (sugar) + Saindhava Lavana (rock salt)

name: Jvarakeshari Rasa
benefits: Jvarakeshari Rasa treats significantlys: all types of Jvara (fever), Pitta Jvara, Sannipata Jvara, Daah Jvara, Vishama Jvara, Pleeha Vriddhi (splenomegaly), Agnimaandya (impaired digestion), Ajeerna (indigestion), Shotha (oedema), Shoola (colic), Gulma (abdominal mass), all 18 Pitta-origin diseases and Tarun Jvara (acute new fever). Being a mild laxative (Mridu Virechaka), it is especially excellent for children and those with constipation (Mridu Koshtha).
ingredients:
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	Equal part
Shu. Visha / Vatsanabha (शु. विष)	Purified Aconitum ferox	Equal part
Vyosha / Trikatu (व्योष/त्रिकटु)	Shunthee + Pippali + Maricha combined	Equal part
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	Equal part
Triphala (त्रिफला)	Haritaki + Vibhitaka + Amalaki combined powder	Equal part
Shu. Jayapala (शु. जयपाल)	Purified Croton tiglium seeds	Equal part
Bhringaraja Svaras (भांगरे रस)	Eclipta alba juice — for Triturate	As required
preparation:
1. Take equal parts of all 6 ingredients. Prepare kajjali from Shu. Parada and Shu. Gandhaka first. Add all remaining powdered ingredients.
2. Triturate all together with Bhringaraja (Eclipta alba) juice.
3. Form pills of 1 Gunja (120 mg) for adults. For children: Sarshapa (mustard seed) size pills, approx. 30 mg.
4. Dry carefully and store in glass vessel. This is Jvarakeshari Rasa.
anupana: All fevers (general): Coconut water (Naarikela Jala) — drink 3 Karsha (approx. 36g) of coconut water additionally. Pitta Jvara (Pitta-type fever): Sita (sugar candy/Mishri). Sanipata Jvara (all-Dosha fever): Maricha (black pepper). Daah Jvara (fever with burning sensation) and Vishama Jvara (intermittent fever): Pippali + Jeeraka (cumin) powder.

name: Chintamani Ras for Sannipata Jwara
benefits: Sannipata Jwara (all three-Dosha combined fever), all varieties of Jeerna Jwara (chronic lingering fever), Pleeha Vriddhi (splenomegaly), Adhmaana (abdominal distension/tympanitis with gas), Kasa (cough), Shwasa (asthma/breathlessness) and Mandagni (weak digestive fire).
ingredients:
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	Equal part
Shu. Vatsanabha (शु. वत्सनाभ)	Purified Aconitum ferox (Aconite)	Equal part
Shu. Gandhak (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	Equal part
Shu. Tankana (शु. टाँखण)	Purified Borax	Equal part
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated Copper 	Equal part
Yavakshara (यवाखार)	Barley alkali	Equal part
Shunthi (सोंठ)	Dry Ginger (Zingiber officinale) — part of Trikatu	Equal part
Pippali (पीपर)	Long Pepper (Piper longum) — part of Trikatu	Equal part
Maricha (मिर्च)	Black Pepper (Piper nigrum) — part of Trikatu	Equal part
Shu. Haritala (हरताल)	Purified Orpiment (Arsenic trisulphide)	Equal part
Haritaki (हरें)	Terminalia chebula — part of Trifala	Equal part
Bibhitaka (बहेड़ा)	Terminalia bellirica — part of Trifala	Equal part
Amalaki (आँवला)	Emblica officinalis — part of Trifala	Equal part
Madhu (शहद)	Honey — for trituration medium (100 times)	As required
preparation: 1. Take all 13 ingredients in equal parts. Add Madhu (honey) as medium.\n2. Triturate all together 100 times (Shatam Vaaran) — very thorough extendedTriturate. This intensive 100-time trituration is essential.\n3. Form tablets of exactly 1 Gunja (120 mg) each. Store in glass.
anupana: with Shunthi Churna (dry ginger powder). After taking: drink pure Narikela Jala (coconut water).

name: Hinguleshwara Ras
benefits: Hinguleshwara Ras treats significantlys: Vata Jwara (Vata-fever), Vishama Jwara (intermittent/irregular/malarial fever), Shirahshula with fever, Purana Jwara (chronic fever) and Shivara Jwara (fever with shivering). For Kaphaja Jwara: most effective with Ardraka + Vasa + Madhu combination.
ingredients:
Pippali Churna (पीपर का चूर्ण)	Piper longum (Long Pepper) powder	Equal part
Shu. Hingula (शुद्ध हिंगुल)	Purified Cinnabar (Mercuric Sulphide)	Equal part
Shu. Vatsanabha / Visha (विष)	Purified Aconitum ferox (Aconite)	Equal part
Madhu (मधु/शहद)	Honey — for administration	As required
preparation: 1. Purify Visha (Shu. Vatsanabha) very thoroughly separately — grind it alone first. Then add Pippali Churna and Shu. Hingula in equal parts.\n2. Grind all three together in a stone mortar until well mixed.\n3. Form tablets of 1 Ratti (120 mg). Store in glass. For dosing: give 2 Ratti (240 mg) with Madhu.
anupana: Madhu (Honey); for Kaphaja Jwara: Ardraka Svaras + Vasa Svaras + Madhu

name: Meghanad Ras
benefits: Vata Jwara
ingredients:
Kansya Bhasma (कांस्यभस्म)	Incinerated Bell Metal / Bronze 	1 part
Pitta Bhasma (पित्तजभस्म)	Bile/Pitta salt ash (Ox bile processed ash)	1 part
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated Copper	1 part
Shu. Gandhak (गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	3 parts
Meghanada / Tanduliyashaka Svaras (मेघनाद/तण्डुलीयशाक स्वरस)	Amaranthus spinosus (prickly amaranth) juice — for Bhavana	As required
preparation: 1. Take Kansya Bhasma, Pitta Bhasma and Tamra Bhasma — 1 part each. Add Shu. Gandhaka 3 parts. Grind well in a stone mortar.\n2. Give Bhavana with Meghanada (Tanduliyashaka/Amaranthus spinosus) Svaras (prickly amaranth plant juice). The Svakashaya (own decoction/juice) of the name-giving plant Meghanada is specifically used.\n3. Form tablets of 1-1 Ratti (125 mg) each. Dry and store in glass. This is Meghanad Ras.
anupana: Ardraka Svaras (Fresh Ginger juice) and Madhu (Honey)

name: Trailokya Sundar Ras
benefits: Nava Jwara (new/acute fever).
ingredients:
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	100 gm (equal part)
Shu. Gandhak (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	100 gm (equal part)
Saindhava Lavana (सैन्धवलवण)	Rock Salt	1 Tola (equal part)
Jeeraka (जीरक)	Cumin (Cuminum cyminum)	1 Tola (equal part)
Ardraka (आर्द्रक)	Fresh Ginger (Zingiber officinale)	1 Tola (equal part)
preparation:
1. Grind Shu. Parada (100 gm) and Shu. Gandhaka (100 gm) in a stone mortar for 2-3 days until excellent Kajjali is formed.
2. Add water, form one large ball. Dry well.
3. Place the dry ball in a new clay Sharava (saucer). Cover with a copper bowl (Tamra Katora) on top. Seal the junction firmly with Vahni Mrittika (fire-hardened clay). Dry.
4. Place the sealed Samput in a Balukayantha (sand-bath) vessel. Fire on intense heat for 2 Muhurta (approximately 3 hours).
5. When Svanga Sheetal: open carefully. Scrape off the Parpati (thin crust layer) from the underside of the copper bowl and the top of the clay saucer. Grind and store in glass. This is Trailokya Sundar Ras.

name: Chandra Shekhar Ras
benefits: Nirama Jwara (Fever without ama/toxins)
ingredients: Udakamanjari Ras (उदकमञ्जरी रस)	Udakamanjari herbal preparation/decoction	As base (principal)\nManashila (मनःशिला)	Realgar (Arsenic Disulphide — As2S2)	In place of Sharkara (sugar)\nSharkara (शर्करा) [replaced]	Sugar (replaced by Manashila in this variant)	Replaced
dose: 1/4 Ratti to 3/4 Ratti (15 mg to 60 mg)
preparation:
1. Take the Udakamanjari Ras base preparation.
2. In place of Sharkara (sugar), add Manashila (purified Realgar/Arsenic Disulphide).
3. Grind (Ghont) all ingredients together as before (Purvavat) to form Vati (tablets).
4. Dry in shade and store in glass. This is Chandra Shekhar Ras.

name: Bolbaddha Ras
benefits: Kasa (Cough) and Shwasa (Asthma)
ingredients:
Paradabhasma (Rasa Sindura) (पारदभस्म / रससिन्दूर)	Mercury ash (Red Mercuric Sulphide)	1 part
Shu. Vatsanabha Churna (शु. वत्सनाभचूर्ण)	Purified Aconitum ferox powder	1 part
Shu. Gandhaka Churna (शु. गन्धकचूर्ण)	Purified Sulphur powder	2 parts
Shu. Raktabola Churna (शु. रक्तबोलचूर्ण)	Purified Red Myrrh powder	1 part
Shu. Haritala (शु. हरताल)	Purified Orpiment (Arsenic trisulphide)	1 part
Shu. Hingu Churna (शु. हींगचूर्ण)	Purified Asafoetida powder	1 part
Banyakakoti (बन्याककोटी)	Kankola (Piper cubeba)	1 part
Svarna Makshika Bhasma (स्वर्णमाक्षिकभस्म)	IncineratedChalcopyrite/Iron Pyrite 	1 part
Haldi Churna (हल्दीचूर्ण)	Turmeric powder	1 part
Kantakari Churna (कण्टकारीचूर्ण)	Solanum surattense powder	1 part
Yavakshara Churna (यवक्षारचूर्ण)	Barley alkali powder	1 part
Shu. Kilihara Churna (शु. किलिहारीचूर्ण)	Purified Gloriosa superba powder	1 part
Yavakshara Churna (यवक्षारचूर्ण)	Barley alkali powder	1 part
Saindhava Churna (सैन्धवचूर्ण)	Rock Salt powder	1 part
Yashtimadhu Sattva or Mahuaphul (यष्टीमधुसत्त्व / महुआफूल)	Liquorice extract or Mahua flower	1 part
preparation: 1. First,treats significantly Rasa Sindura thoroughly in a clean stone mortar. Then add Shu. Gandhaka and Haritala and triturate.\n2. Subsequently, add all remaining substances and give Bhavana (levigation) with Ardraka Svaras (ginger juice) for seven days.\n3. Then form pills of 375 mg each (size of a small jujube/Badara), dry well in shade, and store in a glass vessel. This is called Bolbaddha Ras.

name: Kasahara Ras
benefits: Kasa Roga (Cough disorder)
ingredients: Rajata Bhasma (रजतभस्म)	Incinerateds Silver 	1 part\nShu. Haritala (शु. हरताल)	Purified Orpiment (Arsenic trisulphide)	1 part\nShu. Manaahshila (शु. मन:शिला)	Purified Realgar (Arsenic disulphide)	4 parts
preparation:
1. In a stone mortar, triturate Rajata Bhasma, Shu. Haritala, and Shu. Shila (Manaahshila) together.
2. Then add Vasa Patra Svaras (Adhatoda vasica leaf juice) and Gokshura Kwatha (Tribulus terrestris decoction) and triturate for 1–1 day alternately.
3. When dry, place in a glass bottle (Kachakupi) and process in a Baluka Yantra (sand bath) for 6 hours (Sveda/Paka).
4. When Svangasheetal (body-temperature cooled), remove the medicine and store in a glass vessel.
anupana: Trikatu Churna + Nirgundi Mool Churna with Madhu (honey)

name: Kasanashan Ras
benefits: Kasa Roga (Cough disorder)
ingredients:
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated Copper 	Equal part
Tiksna Loha Bhasma (तीक्ष्णलोहभस्म)	Incinerated Sharp Iron 	Equal part
Abhraka Bhasma (अभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated Mica 	Equal part
Agastya Patra Svaras (अगस्त्यपत्रस्वरस)	Sesbania grandiflora leaf juice — for Bhavana	As required
Kasamarda Patra Svaras (कासमर्दपत्रस्वरस)	Cassia occidentalis leaf juice — for Bhavana	As required
Triphala Kwatha (त्रिफलाक्वाथ)	Triphala decoction — for Bhavana	As required
Amlavetas Kwatha (अम्लवेतसक्वाथ)	Garcinia decoction — for Bhavana	As required
Vasa Svaras (वासास्वरस)	Adhatoda vasica juice — for Anupana	As required
preparation: 1. In a stone mortar, mix all three Bhasmas together and triturate with Agastya Patra Svaras, Kasamarda Patra Svaras, Triphala Kwatha, and Amlavetas Kwatha — one day with each.\n2. Form pills of 250 mg, dry thoroughly in sunlight, and store in a glass vessel.

name: Ratnakaranda Ras
benefits: Kasa, Shwasa, Hikka, Kshaya, Udar and Kushtha Roga
ingredients:
Shu. Bhunaaga Sattva Bhasma (शु. भूनागसत्त्वभस्म)	Purified Tin (Vanga) essence ash	1 gm each
Abhraka Sattva Bhasma (अभ्रकसत्त्वभस्म)	Incinerated Mica 	1 gm each
Kanta Loha Bhasma (कान्तलोहभस्म)	Incinerated Magnetic Iron 	1 gm each
Svarna Bhasma (स्वर्णभस्म)	Incinerated Gold 	1 gm each
Abhraka Bhasma (अभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerateds Mica 	1 gm each
Rajata Bhasma (रजतभस्म)	Incinerateds Silver 	1 gm each
Moti Bhasma (मोतीभस्म)	Incinerated  Pearl 	1 gm each
Pravala Bhasma (प्रवालभस्म)	Incinerated  Coral	1 gm each
Marakata Mani Bhasma (मरकतमणिभस्म)	Incinerated Emerald 	1 gm each
Pushparaga Bhasma (पुष्परागभस्म)	Incinerated Topaz	1 gm each
Heera Bhasma (हीरकभस्म)	Incinerated  Diamond 	1 gm each
Neelam Bhasma (नीलमभस्म)	Incinerated Blue Sapphire 	1 gm each
Gomeda Bhasma (गोमेदभस्म)	Incinerated Hessonite Garnet 	1 gm each
Vaidurya Bhasma (वैदूर्यभस्म)	Incinerated  Cat's Eye 	1 gm each
Manikya Bhasma (माणिक्यभस्म)	Incinerated  Ruby 	1 gm each
Svarna Makshika Bhasma (स्वर्णमाक्षिकभस्म)	Incinerated  Chalcopyrite 	1 gm each
Vaikanta Bhasma (वैकान्तभस्म)	Incinerated  Vaikanta metal 	1 gm each
Shu. Rajavar ta Bhasma (शु. राजावर्तभस्म) — extra	Purified Lapis Lazuli ash (extra)	3 gm
Moti Bhasma (added after) (मोतीभस्म)	Incinerated  Pearl  — added after firing	6 gm
Maricha Churna (added after) (मरिचचूर्ण)	Black Pepper powder — added after firing	15 gm
Amlavetas Kwatha (अम्लवेतसक्वाथ)	Garcinia decoction — for Bhavana/trituration	As required
preparation:
1. Place all Bhasmas in a clean stone mortar and triturate with Amlavetas Kwatha (Garcinia decoction) for one full day.
2. When dry, seal in a Musha (crucible) with Sandhimandhana (clay sealing) and fire (Dhama) until Akasha Darshan — white-blue luminous flame appears at the Musha opening.
3. When Svangasheetal, remove the medicine and again give Bhavana with Amlavetas Kwatha and fire again until Akasha Darshan appears. Repeat this process 100 times.
4. Then take out the medicine, add 6 gm Moti Bhasma and 15 gm Maricha Churna,treats significantly together in a mortar, and store in a glass vessel. This is called Ratnakaranda Ras.

name: Suryavarta Ras
