benefits: Shwasa Roga (Asthma/Respiratory disorder)
ingredients:
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	1 part
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	½ part
Shu. Tamra Patra (शु. ताम्रपत्र)	Purified Copper sheet — for coating	1½ parts
Ghritkumari Svaras (घृतकुमारी स्वरस)	Aloe vera juice — for trituration/Bhavana	As required
preparation:
1. In a clean stone mortar, firmly triturate Parada and Gandhaka for 2–3 days to form good Kajjali.
2. Then give Bhavana with Ghritkumari Svaras (Aloe vera juice) and apply the paste as a coating on a Tamra Patra (copper sheet).
3. Place the coated sheet in a Sharava Samputa (clay dish sealed unit), seal with Sandhimandhana (clay), and keep it in a Handi (clay pot) with the remaining space filled with sand. Cook on a stove for one full day.
4. The next day, when Svangasheetal (cooled to body temperature), open the Samputa and remove the medicine.
5. Triturate in a mortar and store in a glass vessel. This is called Suryavarta Ras.

name: Shwasahara Vataka
benefits: Kasa (Cough) and Shwasa (Asthma)
ingredients:
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	50 gm (1 Pala)
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	50 gm (1 Pala)
Shunthi Churna (शुण्ठीचूर्ण)	Dry Ginger powder	50 gm
Pippali Churna (पिप्पलीचूर्ण)	Long Pepper powder	50 gm
Maricha Churna (मरिचचूर्ण)	Black Pepper powder	50 gm
Vanga Bhasma (वङ्गभस्म)	Incinerated Tin 	50 gm (1 Pala)
Gau Mutra (गोमूत्र)	Cow's urine — for trituration	As required
preparation:
1. In a clean stone mortar, firmly triturate Parada and Gandhaka together to form good Kajjali.
2. Then add all other substances and triturate with Gomutra (cow's urine) for three days.
3. Then triturate again for three more days with Gomutra.
4. Form pills of 12 gm each (Aksha Pramaana) and dry well in shade, then store in a glass vessel. In the modern context, pills of 375 mg are prepared and stored in glass.
anupana: Vasa Patra Svaras (Adhatoda leaf juice) and Madhu (honey)

name: Bhagottara Gutika
benefits: Kasa (Cough) and Shwasa (Asthma)
ingredients:
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	50 gm (1 part)
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	100 gm (2 parts)
Pippali Churna (पिप्पलीचूर्ण)	Long Pepper powder	150 gm (3 parts)
Haritaki Churna (हरीतकीचूर्ण)	Terminalia chebula powder	200 gm (4 parts)
Bibhitaka Churna (बिभीतकचूर्ण)	Terminalia bellirica powder	250 gm (5 parts)
Vasa Churna (वासाचूर्ण)	Adhatoda vasica powder	300 gm (6 parts)
Bhargi Churna (भार्गीचूर्ण)	Clerodendrum serratum powder	350 gm (7 parts)
Babbula Tvak Kwatha (बब्बूलत्वक्काथ)	Acacia arabica bark decoction — 21 Bhavanas	As required
preparation:
1. In a clean stone mortar, firmly triturate Parada and Gandhaka to form good Kajjali.
2. Then add the fine powders of all the herbal ingredients (Pippali onwards) and mix with Babbula Tvak Kwatha (Acacia bark decoction), giving 21 Bhavanas (levigations) over 21 days — triturating well each day.
3. Form pills of 1–1 gm each (Acharya originally prescribed 12 gm / Aksha size, but today 1 gm is appropriate).
4. Dry well in shade and store in a glass vessel. This is called Bhagottara Vati.

name: Nilakantha Ras
benefits: Kasa (Cough) and Shwasa (Asthma)
ingredients:
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	1 part
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated Copper	1 part
Loha Bhasma (लोहभस्म)	Incinerated Iron 	1 part
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	1 part
Shu. Vatsanabha Churna (शु. वत्सनाभचूर्ण)	Purified Aconitum ferox powder	1 part
Shunthi Churna (शुण्ठीचूर्ण)	Dry Ginger powder	1 part
Pippali Churna (पिप्पलीचूर्ण)	Long Pepper powder	1 part
Maricha Churna (मरिचचूर्ण)	Black Pepper powder	1 part
Haritaki Churna (हरीतकीचूर्ण)	Terminalia chebula powder	1 part
Bibhitaka Churna (बिभीतकचूर्ण)	Terminalia bellirica powder	1 part
Amalaki Churna (आमलकीचूर्ण)	Emblica officinalis powder	1 part
Chhoti Elaichi Churna (छोटीइलायचीचूर्ण)	Small Cardamom powder	1 part
Dalchini Churna (दालचीनीचूर्ण)	Cinnamon powder	1 part
Tejapata Churna (तेजपातचूर्ण)	Cinnamomum tamala leaf powder	1 part
Renukabij Churna (रेणुकाबीजचूर्ण)	Vitex agnus-castus seed powder	1 part
Nagaramotha Churna (नागरमोथाचूर्ण)	Cyperus rotundus powder	1 part
Gandira Durva (गण्डीर दूर्वा)	Desmostachya bipinnata (Durva grass)	1 part
Nagakeshara Churna (नागकेशरचूर्ण)	Mesua ferrea powder	1 part
Chitraka Mool Churna (चित्रकमूलचूर्ण)	Plumbago zeylanica root powder	1 part
Guda (गुड)	Jaggery — double all combined (38 parts)	38 parts (double)
preparation:
1. In a clean stone mortar, firmly triturate Parada and Gandhaka together to form good Kajjali.
2. Then add all other fine-powdered substances to the Kajjali and mix thoroughly.
3. Next, prepare a firm Chasni (thick syrup) of Guda (jaggery) — double in quantity to all combined powders — in a clean vessel on low fire.
4. When the Guda syrup reaches the right consistency (thread-forming stage / Kadi Chasni), add the combined powder mixture and blend well.
5. Form pills (Vati/Gutika) of 6 gm each. Note: if Guda is simply mixed without cooking to proper Chasni stage, the pills will melt.
6. Store in a glass vessel.

name: Shwasantaka Ras
benefits: Kasa (Cough) and Shwasa (Asthma)
ingredients:
Shuddha Parada (शुद्ध पारद)	Purified Mercury	160 gm (16 parts)
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated Copper	160 gm (16 parts)
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	80 gm (8 parts / half of above)
Saindhava Lavana Churna (सैन्धवलवणचूर्ण)	Rock Salt powder	80 gm (equal to Gandhaka)
Pippali Churna (पिप्पलीचूर्ण)	Long Pepper powder	60 gm (6 parts)
Jambira Nimbu Svaras (जम्बीरीनिम्बुस्वरस)	Lemon juice — for Bhavana	As required
preparation:
1. In a clean stone mortar, firmly triturate Parada and Gandhaka to form good Kajjali.
2. Then add Tamra Bhasma and triturate for one day.
3. Subsequently add Saindhava Lavana and Pippali Churna and give Bhavana with Jambiri Nimbu Svaras (lemon juice).
4. Form one large ball and dry well.
5. Seal in Sharava Samputa and process in Baluka Yantra (sand bath) for one full day.
6. When Svangasheetal (cooled to body temperature), remove from Samputa, Triturate finely in a mortar, and store in a glass vessel. This is called Shwasantaka Ras.

name: Shwasa-Kasa-Kari Keshari Ras
benefits: Shwasa (Asthma) and Kasa (Cough)
ingredients:
Rajata Bhasma (रजतभस्म)	Incinerated Silver 	1 part each
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated Copper	1 part each
Shu. Parada / Rasa Sindura (शु. पारद / रससिन्दूर)	Purified Mercury / Red Mercuric Sulphide	1 part each
Shu. Manaahshila (शु. मन:शिला)	Purified Realgar (Arsenic disulphide)	1 part each
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	1 part each
Shu. Haritala (शु. हरताल)	Purified Orpiment (Arsenic trisulphide)	1 part each
Vasa Patra Svaras (वासापत्रस्वरस)	Adhatoda vasica leaf juice — for Bhavana	As required
Tulsi Svaras (तुलसीस्वरस)	Ocimum sanctum (Holy Basil) juice — for Bhavana	As required
Ardraka Svaras (आर्द्रकरस)	Ginger juice — for Bhavana	As required
Shu. Gandhaka (added after firing) (शु. गन्धक — पुनः)	Purified Sulphur — added after Puta, equal to fired weight	Equal to fired medicine weight
Trikatu Kwatha (त्रिकटुक्वाथ)	Three-pungent decoction — for second Bhavana	As required
preparation:
1. First, in a clean stone mortar, place Parada and Gandhaka and firmly triturate to form good Kajjali.
2. Then add Manaahshila and Haritala and continue to form good Kajjali.
3. Then add both Bhasmas (Rajata and Tamra).
4. Thereafter, triturate with Vasa Patra Svaras (Adhatoda leaf juice), Tulsi Svaras, and Ardraka Svaras — 3–3 hours each, separately.
5. Form one large ball and dry well.
6. Seal in Sharava Samputa with clay and fire in Bhoodhara Puta for 6 hours.
7. When Svangasheetal, remove the medicine, Triturate in mortar, and add Shu. Gandhaka equal to 1 part of Parada again.
8. Then give 7–7 Bhavanas with Vasa Patra Svaras and Trikatu Kwatha.
9. Form pills of 375 mg, dry well in shade, and store in a glass vessel.

name: Kushtha Kuthar Ras - 2
benefits: Kushtha Nashak
ingredients:
Rasa Sindura (रससिन्दूर)	Incinerated Mercuric Sulphide	48 gm
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	48 gm
Loha Bhasma (लोहभस्म)	Incinerated  Iron 	48 gm
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated  Copper 	48 gm
Shu. Guggulu (शु. गुग्गुलु)	Purified Commiphora mukul resin	48 gm
Triphala Churna (त्रिफलाचूर्ण)	Three-fruit powder (Haritaki+Bibhitaka+Amalaki)	48 gm
Shu. Kupilu Churna (शु. कुपिलुचूर्ण)	Purified Strychnos nux-vomica powder	48 gm
Chitraka Mool Churna (चित्रकमूलचूर्ण)	Plumbago zeylanica root powder	48 gm
Shu. Shilajatu (शु. शिलाजतु)	Purified Asphaltum (mineral pitch)	48 gm
Karanja Bija Churna (करञ्जबीजचूर्ण)	Pongamia pinnata seed powder	192 gm (64 Nishka)
Tamra Bhasma (extra) (ताम्रभस्म — अतिरिक्त)	Incinerated Copper  — extra for final mixing	192 gm (64 Nishka)
Madhu (मधु)	Honey — for mixing	As required
Goghrita (गोघृत)	Cow's ghee — for mixing	As required
preparation:
1. In a mortar, grind Rasa Sindura thoroughly.
2. Then add Gandhaka, Loha Bhasma, and Tamra Bhasma and triturate.
3. Then add Triphala Churna, Shu. Kupilu Churna, and Chitraka Churna.
4. Next, dissolve Shu. Guggulu and Shilajatu in warm water and blend well into the above mixture.
5. Then add Karanja Bija Churna and the extra Tamra Bhasma and mix with Madhu (honey) and Goghrita (cow's ghee) — i.e., add enough honey and ghee so the mixture becomes lickable/semi-solid in consistency.
6. Store in a Snigdha (oiled) vessel or glass jar.

name: Vijaya Vati
benefits: Kushtha Roga (Skin diseases)
ingredients:
Renuka Bija Churna (रेणुकाबीजचूर्ण)	Vitex agnus-castus seed powder	1 part
Pippali Mool Churna (पिप्पलीमूलचूर्ण)	Long Pepper root powder	1 part
Bakuchi Bija Churna (बाकुचीबीजचूर्ण)	Psoralea corylifolia seed powder	1 part
Shu. Kupilu Churna (शु. कुपीलुचूर्ण)	Purified Strychnos nux-vomica powder	1 part
Ashwagandha Churna (अश्वगन्धाचूर्ण)	Withania somnifera powder	1 part
Palasha Bija Churna (पलाशबीजचूर्ण)	Butea monosperma seed powder	1 part
Shunthi Churna (शुण्ठीचूर्ण)	Dry Ginger powder	1 part
Maricha Churna (मरिचचूर्ण)	Black Pepper powder	1 part
Pippali Churna (पिप्पलीचूर्ण)	Long Pepper powder	1 part
Vacha Churna (वचचूर्ण)	Acorus calamus powder	1 part
Indravaruni Churna (इन्द्रवारुणीचूर्ण)	Citrullus colocynthis powder	1 part
Shu. Gandhaka Churna (शु. गन्धकचूर्ण)	Purified Sulphur powder	1 part
Kushtha Churna (कूठचूर्ण)	Saussurea lappa (Costus) root powder	1 part
Shu. Vatsanabha Visha Churna (शु. वत्सनाभिविषचूर्ण)	Purified Aconitum ferox powder	1 part
Rasa Sindura (रससिन्दूर)	Incinerated Mercuric Sulphide	1 part
Guda (गुड)	Old Jaggery — for preparing Chasni (syrup)	15 parts
Madhu (मधु)	Honey — mixed with jaggery syrup	As required
preparation:
1. First, grind Rasa Sindura thoroughly in a mortar.
2. Then add Shu. Gandhaka and triturate.
3. Subsequently, combine all fine powders of the above herbs together.
4. Prepare a Kadi Chasni (firm thread-forming syrup) of 15 parts old Guda (jaggery), then add the combined powder mixture and blend well.
5. Form pills of 375 mg each.
6. Store in a glass vessel.

name: Arogyavardhani Vati
benefits: Kushtha, Jwara
ingredients:
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	1 part
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	1 part
Loha Bhasma (लोहभस्म)	Incinerated Iron 	1 part
Abhraka Bhasma (अभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated Mica 	1 part
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated Copper 	1 part
Triphala Churna (त्रिफलाचूर्ण)	Three-fruit powder (double = 2 parts each)	6 parts (2 each)
Shu. Shilajatu (शु. शिलाजतु)	Purified Asphaltum	3 parts
Shu. Guggulu (शु. गुग्गुलु)	Purified Commiphora mukul (old)	4 parts
Chitraka Mool Churna (चित्रकमूलचूर्ण)	Plumbago zeylanica root powder	4 parts
Kutaki Churna (कुटकीचूर्ण)	Picrorhiza kurroa powder (equal to all above combined)	22 parts
Nimba Patra Svaras (निम्बपत्रस्वरस)	Neem leaf juice — for 2 days of trituration	As required
preparation:
1. First, make good Kajjali from Parada and Gandhaka in a stone mortar.
2. Then gradually add each of the three Bhasmas (Loha, Abhraka, Tamra) one by one and triturate.
3. In a large tray, dissolve Shilajatu and Guggulu in warm water.
4. Add the Kajjali mixture into this.
5. Then also add Triphala Churna, Chitraka Churna, and Kutaki Churna to this mixture.
6. Then add clean, washed Nimba Patra Svaras (Neem leaf juice) and mix in a machine (mixer/grinder) for two days.
7. Form pills the size of a Badari fruit (jujube) and dry well in shade.

name: Kanakasundara Ras
benefits: Kapha and Vata-Kapha Kushtha Roga (Skin diseases)
ingredients:
Svarna Bhasma (सुवर्णभस्म)	Incinerated  Gold 	1 part
Abhraka Sattva Bhasma (अभ्रकसत्त्वभस्म)	Incinerated  Mica extract	1 part
Rasa Sindura (रससिन्दूर)	Red Mercuric Sulphide	2 parts (double)
Trikatu Churna (त्रिकटुचूर्ण)	Three-pungent powder (Shunthi+Pippali+Maricha)	1 part
Chitraka Mool Churna (चित्रकमूलचूर्ण)	Plumbago zeylanica root powder	1 part
Vidanga Churna (विडङ्गचूर्ण)	Embelia ribes powder	1 part
Shu. Vatsanabha Churna (शु. वत्सनाभचूर्ण)	Purified Aconitum ferox powder	½ part
Shu. Kakdasingi Churna (शु. काकडाशृंगीचूर्ण)	Purified Pistacia integerrima (Kakdasingi gall) powder	1 part
Daru Haldi Churna (दारुहल्दीचूर्ण)	Berberis aristata (Tree turmeric) root powder	1 part
Aja Mutra (अजमूत्र)	Goat's urine — for trituration	As required
preparation:
1. In a mortar, grind Rasa Sindura thoroughly.
2. Then add both Bhasmas (Svarna and Abhraka Sattva).
3. Then add Trikatu Churna, Chitraka Mool Churna, Vidanga Churna — 1 part each; Shu. Vatsanabha Churna half part; Shu. Kakdasingi Churna 1 part; and Daru Haldi Churna 1 part — and triturate together.
4. Then triturate with Bakari ke Mootra (goat's urine) for one full day.
5. Form pills of 125 mg and dry well in shade.
6. Store in a glass vessel.
anupana: Kushtha-alleviating herb juice (Neem leaf juice or Guduchi Svaras or Kwatha)

name: Krishna Manikya Ras
benefits: Kushtha Roga (Skin diseases)
ingredients:
Svarna Makshika Bhasma (स्वर्णमाक्षिकभस्म)	Incinerated  Chalcopyrite/Iron Pyrite 	10 gm (1 part)
Shu. Gandhaka Churna (शु. गन्धकचूर्ण)	Purified Sulphur powder	10 gm (1 part)
Tiksna Loha Bhasma (तीक्ष्णलोहभस्म)	Sharp Incinerated Iron 	10 gm (1 part)
Kanta Loha Bhasma (कान्तलोहभस्म)	Magnetic Incinerated Iron 	10 gm (1 part)
Abhraka Bhasma (अभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated Mica 	10 gm (1 part)
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury — double	20 gm (2 parts)
Satuka / Vishatinduka (सतुक / विषतिन्दुक) If unavailable: Shu. Vatsanabha Churna	Strychnos nux-vomica Substitute: Purified Aconitum ferox powder	1/10th of all = 7 gm (substitute: 7 gm)
Manjishthadi Kashaya (मञ्जिष्ठादिकाथ)	Manjishtha (Rubia cordifolia)-based decoction — for cooking	As required
preparation:
1. First, in a stone mortar, firmly triturate Parada and Gandhaka together to form good Kajjali.
2. Then add all other medicines and give Bhavana with Manjishthadi Kashaya (Rubia cordifolia-based decoction).
3. Form one large ball, dry well.
4. Seal in Sharava Samputa with clay sealing (Sandhilepan) and dry.
5. Then place this Samputa in a Baluka Yantra (sand bath) and cook for 6 hours.
6. When Svangasheetal (cooled to body temperature), open the Samputa, remove the medicine, grind in a mortar, and store in a glass vessel.

name: Kushthanashana Ras / Shataru Kushthahar Ras
benefits: Shataru Kushtha (severe/hundred-type skin disease)
ingredients:
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	200 gm
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	200 gm
Svarna Makshika Bhasma (सुवर्णमाक्षिकभस्म)	Incinerated Chalcopyrite/Iron Pyrite 	200 gm
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated Copper 	200 gm
Shu. Shilajatu (शु. शिलाजतु)	Purified Asphaltum (mineral pitch)	200 gm
Amlavetas Churna (अम्लवेतसचूर्ण)	Garcinia indica powder	200 gm
Purana Guda (पुराना गुड)	Old Jaggery — 1/8th of all combined	150 gm
Goghrita (गोघृत)	Cow's ghee — for mixing	As required
Madhu (मधु)	Honey — for mixing	As required
preparation:
1. First, in a mortar, firmly triturate Parada and Gandhaka together to form good Kajjali.
2. Then add the remaining substances one by one and triturate. Note: Shilajatu should be dissolved in a little warm water before mixing.
3. Then prepare a Chasni (syrup) of 150 gm Purana Guda (old jaggery) and add all the powders into this Guda Chasni.
4. Mix well.
5. Form pills of 375 mg each, dry well in shade, and store in a glass vessel.

name: Narayana Ras
benefits: Kushtha, Arsha, Bhagandara, Gulma, Pandu and Kapha diseases
ingredients:
Rasa Sindura (रससिन्दूर)	Incinerated Red Mercuric Sulphide  	1 part
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	1 part
Shu. Guggulu (Purana) (शु. गुग्गुलु — पुराना)	Purified old Commiphora mukul resin	2 parts
Triphala Churna (त्रिफलाचूर्ण)	Three-fruit powder (Haritaki+Bibhitaka+Amalaki)	4 parts
Eranda Taila (एरण्डतैल)	Castor oil — Anupana vehicle	46 gm (1 Pala)
preparation:
1. First, in a mortar, Triturate Rasa Sindura finely.
2. Then add Shu. Gandhaka and triturate.
3. Then add Triphala Churna and triturate further.
4. Dissolve Shu. Guggulu in warm water and add this dissolved Guggulu to the above mixture, triturate and blend well.
5. Form pills of 1–1 gm and dry well in shade.
6. When dry, store in a glass vessel.

name: Parpati Ras — for Charmakushtha
benefits: Charma Kushtha (Psoriasis / Scaly skin disease)
ingredients:
Rasa Parpati (रसपर्पटी)	Mercury-Sulphur Parpati compound	1 part
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated Copper 	1/4 part (one-quarter)
Satuka Visha (सतुकविष) Alternative: Shu. Vatsanabha Churna	Purified Strychnos nux-vomica Alternative: Purified Aconitum ferox powder	1/4 part (one-quarter)
Madhu (मधु)	Honey — Anupana vehicle	As required
preparation: 1. First, Triturate Rasa Parpati in a mortar.\n2. Then add Tamra Bhasma and Satuka Visha (or substitute Shu. Vatsanabha Visha in the absence of Satuka Visha) and triturate together for 1 hour.\n3. Store in a glass vessel.

name: Prataplankeshwara Ras
benefits: Vipadika Kushtha (Cracked heel / hand-foot cracking skin disease)
ingredients:
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	1 part
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	1 part
Shu. Tankana Churna (शु. टङ्कणचूर्ण)	Purified Borax powder	1 part
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated Copper 	1 part
Kushtha Churna (कूठचूर्ण)	Saussurea lappa (Costus) root powder	1 part
Loha Bhasma (लोहभस्म)	Incinerated Iron	1 part
Pippali Churna (पिप्पलीचूर्ण)	Long Pepper powder	1 part
Dhattura Patra Svaras (धत्तूरपत्रस्वरस)	Datura leaf juice — for Bhavana/trituration	As required
preparation: 1. In a mortar, firmly triturate Parada and Gandhaka to form good Kajjali.\n2. Then add all the remaining substances to this Kajjali and triturate with Dhattura Patra Svaras (Datura leaf juice).\n3. Form pills of 250 mg, dry well in shade, and store in a glass vessel.

name: Sarveshwara Ras - 1
benefits: Prasupti Kushtha (Anaesthetic/numb-type skin disease)
ingredients:
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated Copper 	46 gm (1 Pala)
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	46 gm (1 Pala)
Abhraka Bhasma (अभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated Mica	46 gm (1 Pala)
Loha Bhasma (लोहभस्म)	Incinerated Iron 	12 gm (1 Karsha)
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	12 gm (1 Karsha)
Snuhi Ksheera (स्नुहीक्षीर)	Euphorbia nerifolia milk sap — for Bhavana	As required
Arka Ksheera (अर्कक्षीर)	Calotropis procera milk sap — for Bhavana	As required
Eranda Patra Svaras (एरण्डपत्रस्वरस)	Castor plant juice — for Bhavana	As required
Jambira Nimbu Svaras (जम्बीरीनिम्बुस्वरस)	Lemon juice — for Bhavana	As required
Ushira Kwatha (उशीरक्वाथ)	Vetiveria zizanioides decoction — for Bhavana	As required
Pippali Churna (पिप्पलीचूर्ण)	Long Pepper powder — added after Puta	12 gm (1 Karsha)
Shu. Vatsanabha Churna (शु. वत्सनाभचूर्ण)	Purified Aconitum ferox powder — added after Puta	3 gm
preparation:
1. In a mortar, firmly triturate Parada and Gandhaka to form good Kajjali.
2. Then add all three Bhasmas (Tamra, Abhraka, Loha) to the Kajjali and triturate.
3. Triturate with Snuhi Ksheera, Arka Ksheera, Eranda Mool (castor root), Jambiri Nimbu Svaras, and Ushira Kwatha — one day each (1–1 day per Bhavana liquid).
4. Form a ball, dry, seal in Sharava Samputa with clay sealing, and cook in Baluka Yantra (sand bath) on mild, medium, and high fire progressively for 3 days.
5. On the fourth day, when Svangasheetal, remove the Sharav and take out the medicine.
6. Triturate again in mortar and then add Pippali Churna 12 gm and Shu. Vatsanabha Visha Churna 3 gm and triturate together for 1 hour.
7. Store in a glass vessel.

name: Jalajamrita Ras
benefits: Prameha (Urinary/Diabetic disorders) and Mutrakuchhra (Dysuria)
ingredients:
Take equal parts of the following; Sitopala (Mishri) = equal to all combined:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Vamsha Lochana (वंशलोचन)	Bamboo silica (Tabasheer)	Equal part
Abhraka Bhasma (अभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated Mica 	Equal part
Vanga Bhasma (वङ्गभस्म)	Incinerated Tin 	Equal part
Shu. Manaahshila (शु. मैनसिल)	Purified Realgar (Arsenic disulphide)	Equal part
Satu / Giloy Sattva (सत-गिलोय)	Tinospora cordifolia essence extract	Equal part
Bakayan Bija Churna (बकायन बीज)	Melia azedarach seed powder	Equal part
Jeevaniya Gana Kwatha (जीवनीयगणक्वाथ)	Life-sustaining herbs group decoction — for Bhavana	As required (3 Bhavanas)
preparation:
1. Take Vamsha Lochana, Abhraka Bhasma, Vanga Bhasma, Shu. Manaahshila (purified realgar), Satu (Giloy Sattva), and Bakayan Bija Churna — all in equal parts.
2. Give 3–3 Bhavanas (levigations) with Vidari Kand Svaras (Pueraria tuberosa juice) and the juices/decoctions of Jeevaniya Gana herbs (Ashtavarga group or similar life-sustaining herbs).
3. After levigation, powder the mixture finely.
4. Then mix in equal-weight Mishri (rock candy/Sitopala) and store safely in a glass vessel.
reference: Bharat Bhaishajya Ratnakar, part-2, verse 2112, page no 280

name: Tarkeshwara Ras
benefits: Bahumootra (Polyuria / Excessive urination / Diabetes insipidus)
ingredients:
Take equal parts of the following:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Chandrodaya (चन्द्रोदय) (Rasa Sindura / Mruta Soota)	Red Mercuric Sulphide / Processed Mercury	Equal part
Abhraka Bhasma (अभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated Mica 	Equal part
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	Equal part
Udumbara Phala Churna (उदुम्बरफलचूर्ण)	Ripe Ficus racemosa fruit powder — Anupana	12 gm (1 Karsha)
preparation: 1. Take Chandrodaya (Rasa Sindura), Abhraka Bhasma, and Shu. Gandhaka — all in equal parts.\n2. Triturate together in a mortar for one full day with Madhu (honey).\n3. Store in a glass vessel.
anupana: Gau Dugdha (cow's milk); or Udumbara Phala Churna with Madhu and Ghrita
reference: Bharat Bhaishajya Ratnakar, part-2, verse 2613, page no 433

name: Laghu Lokeshwara Ras
benefits: Mutrakuchhra (Dysuria / Painful / Difficult urination)
ingredients:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Rasa Sindura / Mruta Soota (रससिन्दूर)	Red Mercuric Sulphide (processed mercury)	1 part
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	4 parts
Shu. Tankana (शु. टङ्कण)	Purified Borax — for sealing cowrie shells	1/4 part
Jatimool Churna (जातिमूलचूर्ण)	Jasminum officinale root powder — for Anupana	46 gm (1 Pala)
preparation:
1. In a clean mortar, triturate Rasa Sindura and Shu. Gandhaka together for 2–3 days to form good Kajjali.
2. Then take large cowrie shells (Varataka) and fill the medicine inside.
3. Triturate Shu. Tankana (1/4 part) in buffalo's or cow's milk and apply this Tankana paste as coating on the mouths (openings) of the cowrie shells.
4. When dry, place all filled cowrie shells in a large Sharava Samputa, seal with clay sealing (Kapadi Mitti), and fire in Gajaputa.
5. The next day, when Svangasheetal, open the Samputa, remove the clay coating, remove the Tankana (glass-like) crust from the cowrie shell mouth.
6. Triturate the cowrie shell along with the medicine in a mortar, and store in a glass vessel.
anupana: Jatimool Churna with Sarkara Sharbat (sugar syrup)

name: Mutrakuchhranta Ras
benefits: Mutrakuchhra (Dysuria / Painful / Difficult urination)
ingredients:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Shu. Tuttha (शु. तुत्थ)	Purified Copper Sulphate (Blue Vitriol)	1 part
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	1 part
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated Copper 	1 part
preparation:
1. In a mortar, triturate all three substances together — first Parada and Tuttha, then add Tamra Bhasma.
2. Give Bhavana with Shatavari Svaras (Asparagus racemosus juice) and form a ball.
3. Tie the ball in a clean cloth (Vastrabaddha).
4. Place in a vessel of Sarso Taila (mustard oil) and process by Dolaayantra method (suspended bundle in oil) for 3 hours.
5. Remove the bundle and allow to dry.
6. Triturate in mortar and store in a glass vessel.

name: Pashanabhedi Ras - 1
benefits: 220 mg
ingredients:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	1 part
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	2 parts (double)
Arkamool Churna (अर्कमूलचूर्ण)	Calotropis procera root powder	1 part
Punarnava Mool Churna (पुनर्नवामूलचूर्ण)	Boerhavia diffusa root powder	1 part
Vasa Panchang Churna (वासापञ्चाङ्गचूर्ण)	Adhatoda vasica five-parts powder	1 part
Shvetaparajita Mool Churna (श्वेतापराजितामूलचूर्ण)	Nyctanthes arbor-tristis root powder	1 part
Kulatha Kwatha (कुलथ्यक्काथ)	Horse gram decoction — for Anupana	As required
preparation:
1. In a clean mortar, firmly triturate Parada and Gandhaka (double Parada) to form good Kajjali.
2. Add the fine powders of the four herbal substances — Arkamool, Punarnava Mool, Vasa Panchang, and Shvetaparajita Mool — and mix.
3. Give Bhavana with the Svaras (juice) of each herb separately — 1–1 Bhavana each, i.e., triturate with each herb's juice for one day each (3 days).
4. After Bhavana, place in a Musha (crucible) and dry.
5. Process in Jala Yantra (water bath / steam cooking) for 3 days.
6. Remove the medicine, powder finely in a mortar, and form pills of 375 mg.
7. Dry and store in a glass vessel.
anupana: Indrayana Phala Svaras + Bhumyamalaka Svaras + Kulatha Kwatha

name: Sudhanidhi Ras
benefits: Sudhanidhi Ras treats significantlys Murchha (syncope/fainting).
ingredients: Pippali Churna (पिप्पलीचूर्ण)	Long Pepper powder	As required (equal or as needed)\nRasa Sindura (रससिन्दूर)	Incinerated Red Mercuric Sulphide 	As required\nMadhu (मधु)	Honey — for trituration and vehicle	As required
preparation: 1. Take Rasa Sindura and mix with Pippali Churna (Long Pepper powder) and Madhu (honey).\n2. Triturate together until properly incorporated (Murchha/levigation state achieved).\n3. Store in a glass vessel.

name: Mutrakuchhrara / Mutrakuchhra Har
benefits: Pittaja Mutrakuchhra (Pitta-type Dysuria / Burning urination)
ingredients:
Vidari Kanda (विदारीकन्द)	Pueraria tuberosa root	1 Tola (12 gm) each
Gokshura (गोखरु)	Tribulus terrestris fruit/whole plant	1 Tola (12 gm) each
Yashtimadhu (मुलैठी / यष्टिमधु)	Glycyrrhiza glabra (Liquorice) root	1 Tola (12 gm) each
Nagakeshara (नागकेसर)	Mesua ferrea flower	1 Tola (12 gm) each
Jala (जल)	Water — for decoction (cook 32 Tola down to 8 Tola)	32 Tola (384 ml) — reduce to 8 Tola
Madhu (मधु)	Honey — added to strained decoction	As required
Parada Bhasma (पारदभस्म) Alternative: Rasa Sindura	Mercury ash Alternative: Red Mercuric Sulphide	Small dose (Valla Pramaana)
preparation:
1. Take Vidari Kanda, Gokshura, Yashtimadhu, and Nagakeshara — 1–1 Tola (12 gm each) — and cook together in 32 Tola (approximately 384 ml) of water.
2. Cook on low fire until only 8 Tola (approximately 96 ml) of water remains.
3. Strain through a clean cloth.
4. Add Shahad (Madhu/honey) to this warm strained decoction.
5. Then add Parada Bhasma (or Rasa Sindura if Parada Bhasma is unavailable) in a small Valla dose and mix well.

name: Mutrakuchhra Har Loham
benefits: Mutrakuchhra (Dysuria / Difficult/Painful urination)
ingredients: Loha Bhasma (लोहभस्म)	Incinerated Iron 	As required (3 doses)\nMadhu (मधु)	Honey — for mixing and vehicle	As required
preparation: 1. Take well-prepared Loha Bhasma (iron Incinerated) and Triturate it into very fine powder (Sukshma Churna).\n2. Mix with Madhu (honey) thoroughly.
reference: Bharat Bhaishajya Ratnakar, part-4, verse 5620, page no 238

name: Trinetrakhya Ras - 9
benefits: Mutrakuchhra (Dysuria / Difficult/Painful urination)
ingredients:
Vanga Bhasma (वङ्गभस्म)	Incinerated Tin 	Equal part
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	Equal part
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	Equal part
Durva Svaras (दूर्वास्वरस)	Cynodon dactylon (Doob grass) juice — for Bhavana	1 day each
Yashtimadhu Kwatha (यष्टिमधुक्वाथ)	Glycyrrhiza glabra decoction — for Bhavana	1 day each
Gokshura Svaras (गोखरु स्वरस)	Tribulus terrestris juice — for Bhavana	1 day each
Shalmali Phool Svaras (शाल्मलीफूलस्वरस)	Bombax ceiba flower juice — for Bhavana	1 day each
Durva + Yashti + Shalmali milk/water (for Payasa Anupana)	Equal parts — for preparing rice Payasa (Anupana)	Equal parts as needed
preparation:
1. Take Vanga Bhasma, Shu. Parada, and Shu. Gandhaka in equal parts and make good Kajjali in a stone mortar.
2. Transfer to an iron vessel and triturate for 1 full day.
3. Then give Bhavana with Durva (Doob grass), Yashtimadhu (liquorice), Gokshura (Tribulus), and Shalmali Phool (Silk-cotton flower) — 1–1 day each (juice or decoction).
4. Then seal in a Musha and fire in Bhudhara Puta.
5. When Svangasheetal, remove and again give the same Bhavanas and add more Vanga Bhasma.
6. Form pills of 3–3 Ratti (375 mg).
anupana: Cold water (morning); Payasa (rice kheer with Durva+Yashti+Shalmali) with food

name: Varunadhya Loham
benefits: Varunadhya Loham treats significantlys: Mootraghaat (urinary obstruction), Mutrakuchhra (dysuria), Ashmari (kidney stones), Prameha (urinary/diabetic disorders), and Vishamajwara (intermittent fever). Additionally promotes strength, nourishment, aphrodisiac action, and longevity.
ingredients:
Varuna Twak Churna (वरुणत्वकचूर्ण)	Crataeva nurvala (Varuna) bark powder	92 gm (2 Pala)
Amalaki Churna (आमलकीचूर्ण / श्रांवले)	Emblica officinalis (Indian gooseberry) powder	46 gm (1 Pala)
Dhaya ke Phool Churna (धायके फूलचूर्ण)	Woodfordia fruticosa flower powder	23 gm (half Pala)
Haritaki Churna (हरडचूर्ण / हरीतकीचूर्ण)	Terminalia chebula powder	23 gm (half Pala)
Shataparna / Shriparna Churna (शृष्टपर्णीचूर्ण)	Premna integrifolia leaf powder	23 gm (half Pala — 1 Tola)
Loha Bhasma (लोहभस्म)	Incinerated Iron 	12 gm (1 Karsha / 1 Tola)
Abhraka Bhasma (अभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated Mica 	12 gm (1 Karsha / 1 Tola)
preparation: 1. Take all the above substances and prepare fine Churna (powder) of each separately.\n2. Then combine all together and mix thoroughly to make a uniform Churna.\n3. Store in a clean glass vessel.
anupana: Cold water (morning); Payasa (rice kheer with Durva+Yashti+Shalmali) with food
reference: Bharat Bhaishajya Ratnakar, part-4, verse 6963, page no 730

name: Madhukadi Loham
benefits: Netra Roga (Eye diseases)
ingredients:
Yashtimadhu Churna (यष्टिमधुचूर्ण / मुलैठी)	Glycyrrhiza glabra (Liquorice) root powder	Equal part
Haritaki Churna (हरीतकीचूर्ण / हरें)	Terminalia chebula powder	Equal part
Bibhitaka Churna (बिभीतकचूर्ण / बहेड़ा)	Terminalia bellirica powder	Equal part
Amalaki Churna (आमलकीचूर्ण / आमला)	Emblica officinalis powder	Equal part
Loha Bhasma (लोहभस्म)	Incinerated Iron 	Equal part
Goghrita (गोघृत)	Cow's ghee — Anupana vehicle	6 Masha (approx. 6 gm)
Madhu (मधु)	Honey — Anupana vehicle	2 Tola (approx. 24 gm)
preparation: 1. Take Yashtimadhu (Mulaithi) Churna, Haritaki Churna, Bibhitaka Churna, Amalaki Churna, and Loha Bhasma — all in equal parts.\n2. Triturate all together in a mortar until uniformly mixed.\n3. Store in a glass vessel.

name: Makshikadi Vati
benefits: All types of Netra Roga (Eye diseases) with complications
ingredients:
Svarna Makshika Bhasma (स्वर्णमाक्षिकभस्म)	Incinerated Chalcopyrite/Iron Pyrite 	1 Tola (12 gm)
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	6 Masha (half Tola = 6 gm)
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	6 Masha (half Tola = 6 gm)
Abhraka Bhasma (अभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated Mica 	6 Masha (half Tola = 6 gm)
Mukta Bhasma (मुक्ताभस्म / मोती)	Incinerated Pearl 	2 Masha (2 gm)
Svarna Bhasma (स्वर्णभस्म)	Incinerated Gold 	2 Masha (2 gm)
Makoya Patra Svaras (मकोयपत्रस्वरस)	Solanum nigrum leaf juice — 3 Bhavanas	As required
Padma Patra (पद्मपत्र)	Lotus leaves — for wrapping pills	As required
preparation:
1. First make good Kajjali from Parada and Gandhaka.
2. Then add Svarna Makshika Bhasma, Abhraka Bhasma, Mukta Bhasma, and Svarna Bhasma — and triturate all together.
3. Give 3 Bhavanas with Makoya Patra Svaras (Solanum nigrum leaf juice).
4. Form pills of 2–2 Ratti (250 mg).
5. Wrap pills in lotus (Padma) leaves, tie and bury in a Dhanya Rashi (grain heap) for 1 month — this is a traditional Puta/maturing method.
6. After 1 month remove and use. Store in a glass vessel.
anupana: As per disease and constitution (Yathayoga Anupana)

name: Nayanachandra Loham
benefits: All Netra Roga (Eye diseases)
ingredients:
Shunthi Churna (शुण्ठीचूर्ण / सोंठ)	Dry Ginger powder	1 part each
Maricha Churna (मरिचचूर्ण / मिर्च)	Black Pepper powder	1 part each
Pippali Churna (पिप्पलीचूर्ण / पीपल)	Long Pepper powder	1 part each
Haritaki Churna (हरीतकीचूर्ण / हरें)	Terminalia chebula powder	1 part each
Bibhitaka Churna (बिभीतकचूर्ण / बहेड़ा)	Terminalia bellirica powder	1 part each
Amalaki Churna (आमलकीचूर्ण / आमला)	Emblica officinalis powder	1 part each
Kakdasingi Churna (काकड़ासिंगीचूर्ण)	Pistacia integerrima gall powder	1 part each
Kachura / Shathi Churna (कचूरचूर्ण / शठी)	Hedychium spicatum powder	1 part each
Rasna Churna (रास्नाचूर्ण)	Pluchea lanceolata powder	1 part each
Ativisha Churna (अतीसचूर्ण)	Aconitum heterophyllum (purified) powder	1 part each
Draksha Churna (द्राक्षाचूर्ण / मुनक्का)	Vitis vinifera (raisins) powder	1 part each
Neelotpala Churna (नीलकमलचूर्ण)	Nymphaea stellata (Blue lotus) powder	1 part each
Kakoli Churna (काकोलीचूर्ण)	Roscoea procera powder	1 part each
Yashtimadhu Churna (यष्टिमधुचूर्ण / मुलैठी)	Glycyrrhiza glabra powder	1 part each
Nagakeshara Churna (नागकेसरचूर्ण / कंधी)	Mesua ferrea powder	1 part each
Laghu Kantakari Churna (लघुकण्टकारीचूर्ण / छोटी कटेली)	Solanum xanthocarpum powder	1 part each
Brihad Kantakari Churna (बृहतकण्टकारीचूर्ण / बड़ी कटेली)	Solanum indicum powder	1 part each
Loha Bhasma (लोहभस्म)	Incinerated Iron  — equal to all combined	9 parts (= all herbs combined)
Abhraka Bhasma (अभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated Mica  — equal to all combined	9 parts (= all herbs combined)
Triphala Kwatha (त्रिफलाक्वाथ)	Three-fruit decoction — 1 day Bhavana	As required
Tila Taila (तिलतेल)	Sesame oil — 1 day Bhavana	As required
Bhringaraja Svaras (भृंगराजस्वरस)	Eclipta alba juice — 1 day Bhavana	As required
preparation:
1. Prepare fine Churna of all the 17 herbal substances listed above (1 part each).
2. Add Loha Bhasma and Abhraka Bhasma — each equal in quantity to all herbs combined (i.e., 9 parts each of Loha and Abhraka).
3. Combine all and give 1–1 day Bhavana with Triphala Kwatha, Tila Taila (sesame oil), and Bhringaraja Svaras (Eclipta alba juice) respectively.
4. Triturate well with each and form pills the size of a Bera Guthi (Indian jujube seed = approx. 1–2 gm).
5. Store in a glass vessel.
reference: Bharat Bhaishajya Ratnakar, part-3, verse 36oo, page no 224

name: Netrashani Ras
benefits: Sarakta Netra (red eyes), Rakta Pitta (blood disorders in eyes), Naktandhya (night blindness), Timira (cataract/dim vision), Kacha (corneal opacity), Neelikaa (eye discoloration), Patalaa (retinal disorders), Netrabudda (eye tumour), Abhishyanda (conjunctivitis), Adhimantha (glaucoma), Pishthaka (chronic eye ulcer), and all Vata-Pitta-Kapha Netra Roga.
ingredients:
Abhraka Bhasma (अभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated Mica 	1 Pala (60 gm) each
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated Copper 	1 Pala (60 gm) each
Loha Bhasma (लोहभस्म)	Incinerated Iron 	1 Pala (60 gm) each
Svarna Makshika Bhasma (स्वर्णमाक्षिकभस्म)	Incinerated Chalcopyrite/Iron Pyrite 	1 Pala (60 gm) each
Rasanjana (रसाञ्जन / रसौत)	Berberis aristata extract	1 Pala (60 gm) each
Shu. Amlasara Gandhaka (शु. आमलासारगन्धक)	Patan-Yantra purified Sulphur	1 Pala (60 gm) each
Triphala Kwatha (त्रिफलाक्वाथ)	Three-fruit decoction — 1 Bhavana	As required
Bhringaraja Svaras (भृंगरेकरस)	Eclipta alba juice — 1 Bhavana	As required
Pippali Mool Churna (पीपलामूलचूर्ण)	Long Pepper root powder	1 Masha each
Yashtimadhu Churna (यष्टिमधुचूर्ण / मुलैठी)	Glycyrrhiza glabra powder	1 Masha each
Ela Churna (एलाचूर्ण / इलायची)	Cardamom powder	1 Masha each
Punarnava Churna (पुनर्नवाचूर्ण)	Boerhavia diffusa powder	1 Masha each
Devadaru Churna (देवदारुचूर्ण)	Cedrus deodara powder	1 Masha each
Patha Churna (पाठाचूर्ण)	Cissampelos pareira powder	1 Masha each
Bhringara Churna (भंगराचूर्ण)	Eclipta alba powder	1 Masha each
Kachura / Shathi Churna (कचूरचूर्ण / शठी)	Hedychium spicatum powder	1 Masha each
Vacha Churna (वचचूर्ण)	Acorus calamus powder	1 Masha each
Neelotpala Churna (नीलोत्पलचूर्ण)	Nymphaea stellata (Blue lotus) powder	1 Masha each
Safed Chandan Churna (सफेदचन्दनचूर्ण)	Santalum album (White Sandalwood) powder	1 Masha each
Goghrita (गोघृत)	Cow's ghee — for mixing dose	As required
Madhu (मधु)	Honey — for mixing dose	As required
preparation:
1. Take all six primary Bhasmas (Abhraka, Tamra, Loha, Svarna Makshika, Rasanjana, and Shu. Amlasara Gandhaka) — 1 Pala each — and combine in a mortar.
2. Triturate with 1 Bhavana each of Triphala Kwatha and Bhringaraja Svaras.
3. Then add fine powders of all 11 additional herbs (1 Masha each) and triturate together using an iron pestle in an iron vessel.
4. Mix well with Ghrita and Madhu.
5. Administer 1 Masha dose with warm water as Anupana.
reference: Bharat Bhaishajya Ratnakar, part-3, verse 3666, page no 241

name: Gandhaka Rasayanam
benefits: Gandhaka Rasayanam is a Rasayana (rejuvenating tonic) that promotes Divya Drishti (enhanced vision), longevity, strength, skin health, and overall vitality. Classically indicated as a long-term Rasayana therapy for eye health and rejuvenation.
ingredients:
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	8 Pala (368 gm)
Tiksna Loha Bhasma (तीक्ष्णलोहभस्म / मृत तीक्ष्ण लोह)	 Incinerated Iron  (processed)	2 Pala (92 gm)
Ghrita Kumari Svaras (घृतकुमारीस्वरस)	Aloe vera juice — for Surya Paka (sun cooking) over 21 days	Sufficient quantity
Gau Dugdha (गौदुग्ध)	Cow's milk — Anupana vehicle for 1 year	As required
preparation:
1. Take Shu. Gandhaka (8 Pala) and Tiksna Loha Bhasma (2 Pala).
2. Soak in Ghrita Kumari (Aloe vera) juice (Ghee Kumari Ras) and place in the sun (Surya Paka method) for 3 weeks (21 days), adding more juice when it dries.
3. This is the Surya Paka (sun-cooked) preparation method. The medicine absorbs the Aloe vera juice over 21 days of sun processing.
4. After completion, store in a glass vessel.
reference: Bharat Bhaishajya Ratnakar, part-2, verse 1536, page no 99

name: Triphalaadi Churna
benefits: All eye diseases; Arsha, Bhagandara, Prameha, Kushtha, Halimaka; Rasayana for longevity and vitality
ingredients: Triphala (त्रिफला)	Haritaki + Vibhitaka + Amalaki combined powder (equal parts)	Equal part\nLoha Churna / Loha Bhasma (लोहचूर्ण / लोहभस्म)	Iron powder (or Incinerated Iron — preferred)	Equal part\nMulethi / Madhuka (मुलेठी)	Glycyrrhiza glabra (Liquorice) powder	Equal part
preparation: 1. Take equal parts of Triphala (combined), Loha Churna (or preferably Loha Bhasma — iron ash) and Mulethi (Madhuka).\n2. Triturate all to fine powder and mix thoroughly.\n3. Store in a glass vessel.
reference: Bharat Bhaishajya Ratnakar, part-2, verse 2741, page no 487

name: Triphala Rasayana
benefits: Eye diseases (Netra Roga); Rasayana for longevity (300 years), clear vision and eloquent speech
ingredients:
Triphala Churna (त्रिफलाचूर्ण)	Haritaki + Vibhitaka + Amalaki combined powder	100 Pala (approx. 4.8 kg)
Bhringaraja Svaras (भृंगराजस्वरस)	Eclipta alba juice — for 7 Bhavanas (shade-drying)	As required
Shu. Gandhaka Churna (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur powder	25 Pala (1/4 of Triphala)
Shu. Parada Kajjali (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury kajjali (with equal Gandhaka)	12.5 Pala (1/2 of Gandhaka)
preparation:
1. Take 100 Pala of Triphala Churna. Give 7 Bhavanas with Bhringaraja (Eclipta alba) juice — Triturate and shade-drying each time.
2. Prepare kajjali from equal parts of Shu. Parada and Shu. Gandhaka.
3. Add Shu. Gandhaka Churna (25 Pala = 1/4 of Triphala) to the Triphala. Then add Parada kajjali (12.5 Pala = half of Gandhaka).
4. Triturate all together in a mortar.
5. Store in glass vessel.

name: Triphalaadi Yoga
benefits: Samasta Netra Roga (All Eye Diseases)
ingredients:
Haritaki (हरें)	Terminalia chebula powder	2 parts
Vibhitaka (बहेड़ा)	Terminalia bellirica powder	3 parts
Amalaki (आमला)	Phyllanthus emblica (Amla) powder	2 parts
Loha Churna (लोहचूर्ण)	Iron powder	1 part
Patola (पटोल)	Trichosanthes dioica (Pointed gourd) powder	1 part
Madhuyashtika / Mulethi (मुलेठी)	Glycyrrhiza glabra (Liquorice) powder	1 part
Yavaasa Sharkara / Khanda (यवासशर्करा)	Sugar from Alhagi pseudalhagi or plain Sugar candy	8 parts
preparation: 1. Weigh all ingredients in the proportions stated above.\n2. Triturate all to fine powder.\n3. Mix thoroughly and store in a glass vessel.

name: Timirhara Anjana - 1
benefits: Timira Roga (Cataract / Visual Dimness)
ingredients:
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	16 g
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury (double of Gandhaka)	32 g
Shu. Sauveera Anjana (शु. सौवीराञ्जन)	Purified Antimonite / Stibnite collyrium (1/8 of Parada)	6 g
Kapittha Phala Rasa (कपित्थफलरस)	Limonia acidissima (Wood apple) fruit juice — for Triturate	As required
preparation:
1. In a clean mortar, Triturate Shu. Parada (32g) and Shu. Gandhaka (16g) firmly to prepare kajjali.
2. Add Shu. Sauveera Anjana (6g) and Triturate together for 1 full day.
3. Add Kapittha fruit juice (Wood apple) and Triturate for 1 more day.
4. Collect in a glass vessel.
anupana: External — applied to eyes with Netra Shalaka (eye rod) daily

name: Garuda Anjana
benefits: Regular daily application of Garuda Anjana with honey or cow's ghee gives the person vision equal to that of Garuda (the divine eagle) — bestowing extraordinary clarity, sharpness and power of vision (Garuda Drishti Samam Drisham).
ingredients:
Kataka Phala Majja Churna (कतकफलमज्जाचूर्ण)	Strychnos potatorum (clearing nut) seed powder	Equal part
Saindhava (सैन्धव)	Rock Salt	Equal part
Shu. Tuttha (शु. तुत्थ)	Purified Copper sulphate (Chalcanthite)	Equal part
Rasanjana (रसाञ्जन)	Berberis aristata (Daruharidra) extract	Equal part
Shunthee Churna (शुण्ठीचूर्ण)	Zingiber officinale (Dry ginger) powder	Equal part
Pippali Churna (पिप्पलीचूर्ण)	Piper longum (Long pepper) powder	Equal part
Maricha Churna (मरिचचूर्ण)	Piper nigrum (Black pepper) powder	Equal part
Shu. Phitkari (शु. फिटकरी)	Purified Alum (Potassium alum)	Equal part
Nagarmotha Churna (नागरमोथाचूर्ण)	Cyperus rotundus powder	Equal part
Varatika Bhasma (वराटिकाभस्म)	Incinerated Cowrie shell 	Equal part
Amalaka Churna (आमलाचूर्ण)	Phyllanthus emblica (Amla) powder	Equal part
Haritaki Churna (हरीतकीचूर्ण)	Terminalia chebula powder	Equal part
Vibhitaka Churna (विभीतकचूर्ण)	Terminalia bellirica powder	Equal part
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated Copper 	Equal part
Loha Bhasma (लोहभस्म)	Incinerated Iron 	Equal part
Karpur (कर्पूर)	Cinnamomum camphora (Camphor)	Equal part
Katuki Churna (कटुकीचूर्ण)	Picrorhiza kurroa powder	Equal part
Samudra Phena Churna (समुद्रफेनचूर्ण)	Sepia officinalis (Cuttlefish bone) powder	Equal part
Vacha Churna (वचचूर्ण)	Acorus calamus powder	Equal part
Manushya Narakpala Bhasma (मनुष्य नरकपाल भस्म)	Human skull ash (classical Ayurvedic preparation)	Equal part
Naga Bhasma (नागभस्म)	Incinerated Lead 	Equal part
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	Equal part
Shu. Tankana (शु. टङ्ण)	Purified Borax	Equal part
Shu. Nila Anjana (शु. नीलाञ्जन)	Purified Blue vitriol / Indigo collyrium	Equal part
Yashtimadhu Churna (यष्टिमधुचूर्ण)	Glycyrrhiza glabra (Liquorice) powder	Equal part
Karanja Valkala Svaras (करञ्जवल्करस)	Pongamia pinnata bark juice — for 3 or 7 Bhavanas	As required
preparation:
1. First, in a clean mortar, Triturate Shu. Parada + Shu. Nila Anjana + Shu. Tuttha together.
2. Then add Naga Bhasma, Tamra Bhasma and Loha Bhasma and Triturate well.
3. Add all remaining herb powders (Katuka Phala Majja to Yashtimadhu) and Triturate all together thoroughly.
4. Give 3 or 7 Bhavanas (trituration-drying cycles) with Karanja Valkala Svaras (Pongamia pinnata bark juice). Dry well after each Bhavana.
5. Collect in a glass vessel.
anupana: Mixed with Honey or Cow's Ghee and applied with Netra Shalaka

name: Kameshwara Ras
benefits: Shotha (Oedema/Swelling) and Pandu (Anaemia)
ingredients:
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	46 gm (1 Pala)
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	46 gm (1 Pala)
Snuhi Ksheera (स्नुहीक्षीर)	Euphorbia nerifolia milk sap — for Bhavana on Kajjali	140 gm (3 Pala)
Haritaki Churna (हरीतकीचूर्ण)	Terminalia chebula powder	140 gm (3 Pala)
Nagaramotha Churna (नागरमोथाचूर्ण)	Cyperus rotundus powder	70 gm
Chhoti Elaichi Churna (छोटी इलायचीचूर्ण)	Small Cardamom powder	70 gm
Tejapata Churna (तेजपातचूर्ण)	Cinnamomum tamala leaf powder	70 gm
Shunthi Churna (शुण्ठीचूर्ण)	Dry Ginger powder	46 gm
Pippali Churna (पिप्पलीचूर्ण)	Long Pepper powder	46 gm
Maricha Churna (मरिचचूर्ण)	Black Pepper powder	46 gm
Pippali Mool Churna (पिप्पलीमूलचूर्ण)	Long Pepper root powder	46 gm
Shu. Vatsanabha Churna (शु. वत्सनाभचूर्ण)	Purified Aconitum ferox powder	46 gm
Nagakeshara Churna (नागकेसरचूर्ण)	Mesua ferrea powder	12 gm (1 Karsha)
Renuka Bija Churna (रेणुकाबीजचूर्ण)	Vitex agnus-castus seed powder	23 gm (half Pala)
Purana Guda (पुरानागुड)	Old Jaggery — for Chasni/syrup	2,335 gm (half Tula)
Ghrita Kumari Svaras (घृतकुमारीस्वरस)	Aloe vera juice — for trituration	As required
Goghrita (गोघृत)	Cow's ghee — small amount for final trituration	As required
preparation:
1. First, in a mortar, firmly triturate Parada and Gandhaka to form good Kajjali.
2. Triturate Kajjali with herb powders first.
3. Give Bhavana with Aloe vera juice + a little Ghrita.
4. Separately prepare Kadi Chasni (firm syrup) of Guda.
5. Add the Kajjali-herb mixture to the syrup and filter through a strainer as a thick Churna.
6. Store in a glass vessel or form as Bera-size pills (500 mg).

name: Kamala Pranut Ras / Panchasya Ras
benefits: Kamala (Jaundice / Liver disease)
ingredients:
Tiksna Loha Bhasma (तीक्ष्णलोहभस्म)	Incinerated  Iron	Equal part each
Svarna Makshika Bhasma (स्वर्णमाक्षिकभस्म)	Incinerated  Chalcopyrite/Iron Pyrite	Equal part each
Kanta Loha Bhasma (कान्तलोहभस्म)	Incinerated  Magnetic Iron 	Equal part each
Abhraka Bhasma (अभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated  Mica 	Equal part each
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated  Copper 	Equal part each
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	Equal part each
Shu. Haritala (शु. हरताल)	Purified Orpiment (Arsenic trisulphide)	Equal part each
Devadali Svaras (देवदालीस्वरस)	Luffa echinata juice — for Bhavana + Baluka Yantra	As required
Guduchi / Amrita Churna (गुडूचीचूर्ण)	Tinospora cordifolia powder	Equal to Parada quantity
Kamala Pushpa Churna (कमलपुष्पचूर्ण / नीलोत्पल)	Blue Lotus flower powder	Equal to Parada quantity
Kamala Kanda Churna (कमलकन्दचूर्ण / कल्हारकन्द)	Nymphaea lotus (water lily) rhizome powder	Equal to Parada quantity
Draksha Kalka (द्राक्षाकल्क)	Vitis vinifera (raisins) paste	Equal to Parada quantity
Yashtimadhu Kwatha (यष्टिमधुक्वाथ)	Glycyrrhiza glabra decoction — for Bhavana	As required
Madhu (मधु)	Honey — for final mixing/Anupana	As required
Sita / Mishri (सिता/मिश्री)	Rock candy/Sugar — for final mixing/Anupana	As required
preparation:
1. First, in a mortar, triturate Parada and Shu. Haritala to form good Kajjali.
2. Then add all other Bhasmas (Tiksna Loha, Svarna Makshika, Kanta Loha, Abhraka, Tamra) and mix thoroughly in the Kajjali.
3. Give 1 Bhavana with Devadali Svaras (Luffa echinata juice), triturate well, then dry.
4. Place in a Sharava Samputa with clay sealing and process in Baluka Yantra (sand bath).
5. When Svangasheetal, remove and Triturate in mortar for 3 days.
6. Add Guduchisattva or Guduchi Churna, Kamala Pushpa Churna, Kalhara Kanda Churna, and Draksha Kalka — each equal to Parada's weight — to the Baluka Yantra-processed medicine.
7. Give Bhavana with Yashtimadhu Kwatha and triturate.
8. Form pills of 3–3 Ratti (375 mg), dry in shade, and store in a glass vessel.

name: Triyoni Ras
benefits: Shotha (Oedema/Swelling) and Pandu (Anaemia)
ingredients:
Shu. Tamra Patra (शु. ताम्रपत्र)	Purified Copper foil sheets	4 parts
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury — for coating copper foil	1 part
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur — spread below and above Tamra Patra	4 parts
Matsyakshi Churna (मत्स्याक्षीचूर्ण)	Alternanthera sessilis powder — spread over Gandhaka	1 part
Nimbu Svaras (निम्बुस्वरस)	Lemon juice — for solar processing (3–4 days)	As required
Haritaki Churna (हरीतकीचूर्ण)	Terminalia chebula powder — Anupana	2 gm per dose
Guda (गुड)	Jaggery — Anupana	2 gm per dose
preparation:
1. In a mortar, triturate Parada and Gandhaka (Kajjali) and coat the copper foil thoroughly so Parada adheres well to the Tamra Patra.
2. Then add Nimbu Svaras (lemon juice) sufficiently to submerge the copper foil, and place in strong sunlight for 3–4 days.
3. Then in a Sharava: first spread 2 parts Shu. Gandhaka; lay the Tamra Patra (coated with Parada) on top; cover again with the remaining 2 parts Shu. Gandhaka powder so the Tamra Patra is sandwiched.
4. Sprinkle Matsyakshi (Alternanthera sessilis) Churna 1 part on top of the Gandhaka.
5. Seal in Sharava Samputa with clay (Sandhilepana) tightly.
6. Cook in Baluka Yantra for 6 hours (two Yaama/Prahara).
7. When Svangasheetal, open, remove the medicine, Triturate in mortar, and store in a glass vessel.

name: Hamsamandura
benefits: Pandu, Kamala, Halimak, Urustambha, and Arsha
ingredients:
Shu. Mandura (शु. मण्डूर)	Purified Iron rust/calx	1 part
Gomutra (गोमूत्र)	Cow's urine — for cooking Mandura	8 parts
Goghrita (गोघृत)	Cow's ghee — added at end of cooking	1 part (equal to Mandura)
Shunthi Churna (शुण्ठीचूर्ण)	Dry Ginger powder	1 part each
Maricha Churna (मरिचचूर्ण)	Black Pepper powder	1 part each
Pippali Churna (पिप्पलीचूर्ण)	Long Pepper powder	1 part each
Haritaki Churna (हरीतकीचूर्ण)	Terminalia chebula powder	1 part each
Bibhitaka Churna (बिभीतकचूर्ण)	Terminalia bellirica powder	1 part each
Amalaki Churna (आमलकीचूर्ण)	Emblica officinalis powder	1 part each
Musta Churna (मुस्ताचूर्ण)	Cyperus rotundus powder	1 part each
Vidanga Churna (विडङ्गचूर्ण)	Embelia ribes powder	1 part each
Chavya Churna (चव्यचूर्ण)	Piper retrofractum powder	1 part each
Chitraka Mool Churna (चित्रकमूलचूर्ण)	Plumbago zeylanica root powder	1 part each
Daruharidra Churna (दारुहल्दीचूर्ण)	Berberis aristata powder	1 part each
Pippali Mool Churna (पिप्पलीमूलचूर्ण)	Long Pepper root powder	1 part each
Devadaru Churna (देवदारुचूर्ण)	Cedrus deodara powder	1 part each
preparation:
1. Triturate Shu. Mandura finely.
2. Cook in 8 times its quantity of Gomutra (cow's urine) on low fire in an iron vessel (Loha Patra) until the Gomutra evaporates and Mandura is well-cooked.
3. Separately prepare fine Churna of all the above herbs (1 part each).
4. At the end of cooking, add Goghrita (cow's ghee — equal to Mandura quantity) to the Mandura, mix all herb powders in thoroughly.
5. Filter through cloth strainer, and store in a glass vessel.

name: Jaypala Ras
benefits: Shotha (Oedema/Swelling) and Pandu (Anaemia)
ingredients:
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	Equal part
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	Equal part
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated  Copper  (Mruta Tamra)	Equal part
Shu. Jaypala (शु. जयपाल)	Purified Croton tiglium (Jamal Gota) seed	Equal part
Shu. Guggulu (शु. गुग्गुलु)	Purified Commiphora mukul resin	Equal part
Goghrita (गोघृत)	Cow's ghee — for forming pills	As required
preparation:
1. First, in a mortar, firmly triturate Parada and Gandhaka to form good Kajjali.
2. Then add Tamra Bhasma and Shu. Jaypala (Purified Croton tiglium) to the Kajjali and triturate.
3. Dissolve Shu. Guggulu in warm water and blend into the above mixture.
4. Add Goghrita (cow's ghee) and triturate all well.
5. Form pills of 125 mg (Valla Pramaana).
6. Dry in shade and store in a glass vessel.

name: Kaalavidhvamsana Rasa
benefits: Pandu Roga (Anaemia / Pallor disease)
ingredients:
Rasasindura (रससिन्दूर)	Purified Mercuric Sulphide	1 part
Svarna Bhasma (स्वर्णभस्म)	Incinerated Gold 	1 part
Rajata Bhasma (रजतभस्म)	Incinerated Silver	1 part
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated Copper 	1 part
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury (additional)	4 parts
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	24 parts
Loha Bhasma (लोहभस्म)	Incinerated Iron	6 parts
Shu. Vatsanabha Churna (शु. वत्सनाभचूर्ण)	Purified Aconitum ferox powder — added last	1/10th part of total
Jambiri Nimbu Svaras (जम्बीरी रस)	Lemon juice — for trituration and Gandhaka processing	As required
Dhattura Phala / Patra Svaras (धत्तूरफल/पत्रस्वरस)	Datura metel seed/leaf juice — for Dolayantra	As required
Kantakari Kaatha (कण्टकारीकाथ)	Solanum xanthocarpum decoction — Bhavanas	As required
Brihati Svaras (बृहतीकाथ)	Solanum indicum juice — Bhavanas	As required
Arka Patra Svaras (अर्कपत्रस्वरस)	Calotropis procera leaf juice — Bhavanas	As required
Karanja Patra Svaras (करञ्जपत्रस्वरस)	Pongamia pinnata leaf juice — Bhavanas	As required
Haritaki Kaatha (हरीतकीकाथ)	Terminalia chebula decoction — Anupana	As required
preparation:
1. Triturate Rasasindura to fine powder. Add Svarna, Rajata and Tamra Bhasma. Triturate with Jambiri lemon juice for 1 day in sunlight. Add Shu. Parada (4 parts) and Triturate to paste-like consistency.
2. Fill the prepared paste into hollowed Datura seeds (sized by dose). Tie with thread and wrap in cloth to form a Pottali. Hang in Dolayantra filled with Datura patra svaras and process on mild heat for 3 days, refilling juice as it dries.
3. On day 4, remove Pottali. In Ishtika Yantra, spread Jambiri-processed Shu. Gandhaka powder below, place Pottali, cover above with more Gandhaka powder and fire in Laghu Puta. Repeat several times.
4. Then give 2 Bhavanas and 2 Laghu Putas each with: Brihati svaras, Kantakari Kaatha and Arka-Karanja patra svaras (total 6 Bhavanas, 6 Putas). Add equal Loha Bhasma and Triturate with Kantakari svaras for 1 day. Give 3 more Laghu Putas with Vanopal.
5. Weigh the final product. Add 1/10th part Shu. Vatsanabha Churna. Triturate well and store in glass vessel.
anupana: Haritaki Kaatha (Terminalia chebula decoction) or Gomutra

name: Panduhariharitaki
benefits: Pandu Roga (Anaemia / Pallor disease)
ingredients:
Sahachara (सहचरयवकुट)	Barleria prionitis / Strobilanthes ciliatus	7 Pala (325g)
Bhringaraja (भृङ्गराजयवकुट)	Eclipta alba	7 Pala (325g)
Shatavari (शतावरीयवकुट)	Asparagus racemosus	7 Pala (325g)
Punarnava (पुनर्नवायवकुट)	Boerhavia diffusa	7 Pala (325g)
Badi Haritaki (हरीतकी)	Terminalia chebula — large fruits	360 Nag (360 fruits)
Go Dugdha (गोदूध)	Cow's milk — for second cooking	As required
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury — for kajjali	140g
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur — for kajjali and Parpati	140g
Guduchi Sattva (गूडूचीसत्त्व)	Pure starch extract of Tinospora cordifolia	8 Pala (375g)
Madhu (मधु)	Honey — for pill making and storage	As required
preparation:
1. Make decoction of Sahachara, Bhringaraja, Shatavari and Punarnava (7 Pala each) in 8 times water (18 litres). Reduce to one quarter and filter.
2. In this decoction, cook 360 large Haritaki fruits until the decoction is absorbed. When Haritaki cools, cook again in cow's milk until milk reduces to one-quarter or dries. Remove seeds from cooled Haritaki. Keep the fruit flesh/skins separate.
3. Prepare good kajjali from Shu. Parada 140g + Shu. Gandhaka 140g. Make Rasa Parpati (flat thin cake). Break and powder the Parpati finely.
4. Mix Parpati powder with Guduchi Sattva (375g). Add a little Honey. Form 360 pills. Slightly dry them.
5. Place 1 pill inside each Haritaki fruit skin/covering. Tie with thread. Fill all 360 Haritakis into a large glass jar. Cover with pure Honey. Tie cloth over jar mouth.
6. After 1 week: perform Virechana (purgation) to cleanse the bowels. Then take 1 stuffed Haritaki with 1 Vati in the morning daily with cow's milk.

name: Pittapandu Vari Gutika
benefits: Pittaja Pandu Roga (Pitta-type Anaemia / Pallor disease)
ingredients:
Rasasindura (रससिन्दूर)	Purified Mercuric Sulphide	4 parts
Loha Bhasma (लोहभस्म)	Incinerated Iron	2 parts
Chitraka Moola Churna (चित्रकमूलचूर्ण)	Plumbago zeylanica root powder	2 parts
Nagarmotha Churna (नागरमोथाचूर्ण)	Cyperus rotundus powder	2 parts
Vidanga Churna (विडङ्गचूर्ण)	Embelia ribes (false black pepper) powder	2 parts
Trikatu Churna (त्रिकटचूर्ण)	Shunthee + Maricha + Pippali combined powder	2 parts
Triphala Churna (त्रिफलाचूर्ण)	Haritaki + Vibhitaka + Amalaki combined powder	2 parts
Kutaja Tvak Churna (कुटजत्वक्चूर्ण)	Holarrhena antidysenterica bark powder	2 parts
Madhu (मधु)	Honey — for pill binding	As required
preparation: 1. In a mortar, Triturate Rasasindura to fine powder. Add Loha Bhasma and all other powdered ingredients. Mix thoroughly.\n2. Add Madhu (honey) and form pills of 375 mg each.\n3. Dry well and store in glass vessel.

name: Trailokya Sundar Rasa
benefits: Shotha (Oedema) and Pandu Roga (Anaemia)
ingredients:
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	1 part
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	1 part
Loha Bhasma (लोहभस्म)	Incinerated Iron	1 part
Abhraka Bhasma (अभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated Mica 	1 part
Guduchi Sattva (गुडूचीसत्त्व)	Tinospora cordifolia pure starch extract	1 part
Varahikanda (वाराहीकन्द)	Dioscorea bulbifera (Air potato) powder	1 part
Triphala Churna (त्रिफलाचूर्ण)	Haritaki + Vibhitaka + Amalaki combined powder	1 part
Sahajana Moola Churna (सहजनमूलचूर्ण)	Moringa oleifera (Drumstick tree) root powder	1 part
Bhringaraja Svaras (भृङ्गराजस्वरस)	Eclipta alba juice — for 1 Bhavana	As required
preparation:
1. In a stone mortar, prepare kajjali from Shu. Parada and Shu. Gandhaka. Add Loha Bhasma and Abhraka Bhasma and Triturate well.
2. Add all four herbal powders (Guduchi Sattva, Varahikanda, Triphala, Sahajana Moola) and Triturate all together.
3. Give one Bhavana (trituration + drying) with Bhringaraja svaras. Dry well in shade.
4. Form pills of 375 mg each. Dry thoroughly in shade and store in glass vessel.
anupana: Madhu (Honey) + Goghrita (Cow's ghee) + Mishri Churna (Sugar candy powder)

name: Vijaya Gutika
benefits: Shotha (Oedema) and Pandu Roga (Anaemia)
ingredients:
Haritaki Churna (हरीतकीचूर्ण)	Terminalia chebula powder	140g
Chitraka Moola Churna (चित्रकमूलचूर्ण)	Plumbago zeylanica root powder	140g
Choti Elaichi Churna (छोटी इलायचीचूर्ण)	Elettaria cardamomum (small cardamom) powder	23g
Dalchini (दालचीनी)	Cinnamomum verum (Cinnamon) powder	23g
Tejpata Churna (तेजपातचूर्ण)	Cinnamomum tamala (Bay leaf) powder	23g
Nagarmotha Churna (नागरमोथाचूर्ण)	Cyperus rotundus powder	23g
Renuka Beeja Churna (रेणुकाबीजचूर्ण)	Vitex agnus-castus (Nagkeshar alternative) seed powder	23g
Nagkeshar Churna (नागकेशरचूर्ण)	Mesua ferrea (Indian rose chestnut) powder	12g
Trikatu Churna (त्रिकटुचूर्ण)	Shunthee + Maricha + Pippali combined powder	46g
Pippali Moola Churna (पिप्पलीमूलचूर्ण)	Piper longum root powder	46g
Shu. Vatsanabha Churna (शु. वत्सनाभचूर्ण)	Purified Aconitum ferox powder	46g
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	46g
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	46g
Purana Guda (पुरानागुड)	Old Jaggery (aged, not fresh)	2,335g (half the total weight of all above)
preparation:
1. In a stone mortar, prepare kajjali from Shu. Parada (46g) and Shu. Gandhaka (46g). Add all other powdered ingredients and mix very well. Sieve through a fine cloth strainer.
2. Prepare old Jaggery syrup (Chaasni): dissolve Purana Guda in water, filter through cloth, and cook to Laddu Chaasni (semi-thick syrup) consistency.
3. Add the mixed kajjali-powder to the hot syrup. Mix thoroughly so all powder is uniformly incorporated.
4. While still warm, form pills of 4 Ratti (500 mg) each, shaped like Jangali Ber (wild jujube). Dry in shade and store in glass vessel.

name: Lokanath Ras
benefits: Lokanath Ras treats significantlys: Yakrit Roga (liver diseases), Pliha Roga (spleen diseases), Udara Roga (abdominal disorders), Gulma (abdominal lump/tumour) and Shwayathu/Shotha (oedema/swelling).
ingredients:
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	1 part
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	1 part
Abhraka Bhasma (अभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated Mica	1 part
Loha Bhasma (लोहभस्म)	Incinerated Iron 	2 parts
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated Copper 	2 parts
Kauri Bhasma (कौड़ीभस्म)	Incinerated Cowrie shell 	6 parts
Nagavalli Svaras (नागवल्ली रस)	Betel leaf juice — for trituration	As required
preparation:
1. Prepare Kajjali by Triturate Shu. Parada (1 part) and Shu. Gandhaka (1 part) together until a smooth black mass forms.
2. Add Abhraka Bhasma (1 part), Loha Bhasma (2 parts), Tamra Bhasma (2 parts) and Kauri Bhasma (6 parts). Mix all thoroughly.
3. Triturate with Nagavalli (betel leaf) juice until well mixed.
4. Heat in Gajaputa (cowdung cake fire). Remove when cooled to body temperature.
5. Form tablets. Dry in shade and store in glass. This is Lokanath Ras.
anupana: With Pippali + Honey; or Haritaki + Jaggery; or Jeera Churna + Gomuthra/Jaggery

name: Agnikumar Loha
benefits: Agnikumar Loha treats significantlys: Yakrit Roga (liver diseases), Pliha Roga (spleen diseases), Udara Roga (abdominal diseases), Gulma (abdominal lump) and Halimaka (advanced anaemia/jaundice).
ingredients:
Tuthya / Tuttha (तूथ्य)	Copper Sulphate	1 Tola
Apamarga / Ramtangana (अपामार्ग)	Achyranthes aspera	1 Tola
Tankana / Suhaga (टंकण/सुहागा)	Borax	1 Tola
Saindhava Namak (सेंधानमक)	Rock Salt	1 Tola

Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	½ Pala
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	½ Pala
preparation:
1. Take each of the 12 herbs/minerals in 1 Tola powder. Add Loha Bhasma — 12 parts (equal to total of the above).
2. Prepare Kajjali from Shu. Parada (½ Pala) and Shu. Gandhaka (½ Pala).
3. Combine Kajjali with all the powders and Triturate together until homogeneous.
4. Take with Ghee and honey. This is Agnikumar Loha.

preparation: 1. Prepare Kajjali from Shu. Parada and Shu. Gandhaka (equal parts). Add all remaining bhasmas and powders in equal parts. Triturate thoroughly.\n2. Give one Bhavana with Sooryamukhi (sunflower) leaf juice and one Bhavana with Bilva (bael) leaf juice.\n3. Triturate again with Sooryamukhi juice and form tablets. Dry in shade. This is Loha Mrityunjaya Ras.

benefits: Treats significantlys many diseases swiftly, like the sun dispels darkness: Yakrit Roga (liver diseases), Gulma (abdominal lump), Udara Roga (abdominal disorders), Agra-mamsa (hepatosplenomegaly/tissue overgrowth), Pliha (spleen diseases), Agni-mandya (indigestion/weak digestive fire) and Aruchi (anorexia/loss of appetite).

name: Pliharnava Ras
benefits: Treats significantlys without doubt: all six types of Pliha (spleen diseases and enlargements), Jwara (fever), Manda Agni (weak digestive fire), Kasa (cough), Shwasa (asthma/breathlessness), Vamana (vomiting) and Bhrama (vertigo/dizziness).

Abhraka Bhasma (अभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated Mica	1 Pala

preparation: 1. Take Shu. Hingula, Shu. Gandhaka, Shu. Tankana, Abhraka Bhasma and Shu. Visha — each 1 Pala. Triturate all together very finely.\n2. Add Pippali (½ Pala) and Mirch (½ Pala) powders. Triturate all together thoroughly.\n3. Form tablets of 1½ Ratti (approx. 187 mg) each. Dry in shade and store in glass. This is Pliharnava Ras.

name: Plihahari Ras
benefits: Treats significantlys without doubt — even incurable (Asadhya) Pliha (spleen diseases/enlargement of all types), Yakrit Roga (liver diseases), Pandu (anaemia/jaundice), Gulma (abdominal lump/tumour) and Bhagandar (fistula-in-ano) and related disorders.

Chitraka Churna (चीताके चूर्ण)	Plumbago zeylanica powder — for administration	As required
preparation: 1. Take all ingredients in the specified quantities and Triturate together thoroughly until homogeneous.\n2. Form tablets of 6 Ratti (approx. 750 mg) each. Dry in shade and store in glass.

name: Plihashardul Ras
benefits: Plihashardul Ras treats significantlys: Pliha (spleen diseases), Agra-mamsa (hepatosplenomegaly), Yakrit (liver diseases), Gulma (abdominal lump — even difficult cases), all Ama-shaya Roga (stomach/intestinal diseases), Udara Roga (abdominal diseases), Shotha (oedema), Vidradhi (abscess), Agni-mandya (indigestion), Jwara (fever) and all types of Pliha-associated fevers.

name: Rohitak Loha
benefits: Rohitak Loha treats significantlys: Pliha Roga (spleen diseases and enlargement), Yakrit Roga (liver diseases) and Agra-mamsa (hepatosplenomegaly/tissue overgrowth).

name: Tamreshwar Vati
benefits: Tamreshwar Vati treats significantlys: Pliha (spleen diseases), Yakrit (liver diseases), Gulma (abdominal lump), Darun Amavata (severe rheumatoid arthritis), Bawasir/Arsha (piles/haemorrhoids), Ghor Udara Roga (severe abdominal diseases), Murchha (fainting), Pandu (anaemia/jaundice), Halimak (advanced anaemia), Grahani (malabsorption/IBS), Atisara (diarrhoea), Yakshma Roga (tuberculosis/wasting) and Shotha (oedema/swelling).

Add Sendha Namak, Loha Bhasma and Tamra Bhasma — 7 Tola each. Mix all together and Triturate thoroughly.\n14.	Form into tablets (Vati). Dry in shade and store in glass. This is Tamreshwar Vati.

name: Yakritadi Loha

preparation: 1.	Take all five ingredients in specified quantities. Triturate all together thoroughly until homogeneous.\n2.	Form tablets of 9 Gunja (9 Ratti = approx. 1125 mg) each. Dry in shade and store in glass. This is Yakritadi Loha.

name: Mehkeshari
benefits: Prameha (Urinary/Diabetic disorders), Madhumeha (Diabetes mellitus), Shukra Pravaha (Spermatorrhoea)

Ghrita Kumari Svaras (घृतकुमारीस्वरस)	Aloe vera juice — for Bhavana	As required

preparation: 1. Take all eight substances (Vanga Bhasma, Svarna Bhasma, Kanta Loha Bhasma, Rasa Sindura, Mukta Bhasma, Dalchini Churna, Sukshma Ela Churna, and Nagakeshara Churna) in equal parts. Powder finely and combine in a mortar.\n2. Give Bhavana with Ghrita Kumari Svaras (Aloe vera juice) until well incorporated.\n3. Form pills of 2 Masha (approx. 250 mg) each. Store in a glass vessel.

name: Chandraprabha Vati — for Sarva Prameha
benefits: Sarva Prameha (all types of urinary/diabetic disorders), Stambhana, Swapna Prameha

Langali Svaras (लाङ्गलीद्रव)	Gloriosa superba extract — 1 day Bhavana	As required
Bhedi Dugdha (भेड़ी दूध)	Sheep's milk — 1 day Bhavana	As required

dose: 500 mg to 1 gm (1 pill — Bera seed size)
preparation: 1. Make fine Churna of all Churna-worthy substances (herbs, minerals). Combine all.\n2. Give Bhavana with Langali Svaras for 1 day; then Bhedi Dugdha (sheep's milk) for 1 day; then Paan Svaras (betel leaf juice) for 1 day (3 total Bhavanas).\n3. Form pills of Bera seed size (approx. 500 mg).

name: Prameha Setu Ras
benefits: Sarva Roga; especially Paittika Prameha (Pitta-type urinary/diabetic disorders)
ingredients:
Rasa Sindura (रससिन्दूर)	Red Mercuric Sulphide (processed mercury)	Equal part
Abhraka Bhasma (अभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated Mica	Equal part
