Triphala Kwatha (त्रिफलाक्वाथ)	Three-fruit decoction — Anupana	As required
Madhu (मधु)	Honey — Anupana	As required
preparation: 1. Take Rasa Sindura and Abhraka Bhasma in equal parts. Triturate both in a mortar with Vata Ksheera (Banyan tree milk sap) for two Prahar (approximately 6 hours) until well incorporated.\n2. Dry the mixture. Place in a Musha (crucible) and fire (Puta Paka).\n3. When Svangasheetal, remove and Triturate in mortar. Store in a glass vessel.
anupana: Triphala Kwatha with Madhu (honey) — especially for Prameha

name: Vangeshwara Ras
benefits: Vataja Prameha (Vata-type Urinary/Diabetic disorders)
ingredients:
Rasa Sindura (रससिन्दूर)	Red Mercuric Sulphide (Rasa Bhasma)	1 part (1 Ratti = 125 mg)
Vanga Bhasma (वङ्गभस्म)	Incinerated Tin 	1 part (1 Ratti = 125 mg)
Madhu (मधु)	Honey — for licking (Lehana)	As required
Mudga Yusha with Amalaki (मुद्रायूष + आमला)	Green gram soup with gooseberry — dietary Pathya	As diet
Takra (तक्र)	Buttermilk — dietary Pathya	As diet
Tilapindi (Khalli) (तिलपिण्डी / खल्ली)	Sesame oil cake — cooked in Takra with Hingu	25 gm
Shu. Hingu (शु. हींग)	Purified Asafoetida — cooked with Tilapindi in Takra	Small quantity
Tila Taila (तिलतेल)	Sesame oil — for cooking vegetables (dietary advice)	As cooking medium
preparation: 1. Take Rasa Sindura and Vanga Bhasma — 1 Ratti each (125 mg each = 250 mg total).\n2. Combine and lick with Madhu (honey). Administering this Lehana (licking preparation) with honey eliminates Vataja Prameha (Vata-type diabetes/urinary disorders).\n3. Dietary Management (Pathya): Prepare Mudga Amalaka Yusha (green gram + gooseberry broth) and give with Takra (buttermilk). Cook Tilapindi (sesame oil cake — 25 gm) in Takra with Shu. Hingu (purified asafoetida) and give to drink — this also helps in Prameha. Avoid excessive Ghrita (ghee); instead cook vegetables in Tila Taila (sesame oil).
anupana: Mudra Yusha with Amalaki and Hingu Takra

name: Vasanta Kusumakar Ras
benefits: Madhumeha, Sarva Prameha, Kshaya, Kasa, Trisha, Shwasa, Rakta Pitta, Visha, Amlapitta
ingredients:
Svarna Bhasma (स्वर्णभस्म)	Incinerated Gold 	2 parts
Abhraka Bhasma (अभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated Mica	2 parts
Loha Bhasma (लोहभस्म)	Incinerated Iron 	3 parts
Rasa Sindura (रससिन्दूर)	Red Mercuric Sulphide	4 parts
Vanga Bhasma (वङ्गभस्म)	Incinerated Tin 	3 parts
Pravala Bhasma (प्रवालभस्म)	Incinerated Coral 	4 parts
Mukta Pishti (मोतीपिष्ठी)	Pearl paste	4 parts
Kasturi (कस्तूरी)	Musk	4 parts
Gau Dugdha (गोदुग्ध)	Cow's milk — 7 Bhavanas	As required
Ikshu Rasa (इक्षुरस)	Sugarcane juice — 7 Bhavanas	As required
Vasa Patra Svaras (वासापत्रस्वरस)	Adhatoda vasica leaf juice — 7 Bhavanas	As required
Haridra Svaras (हरिद्रास्वरस)	Turmeric juice/water — 7 Bhavanas	As required
Kadalikanda Rasa (कदलीकन्दरस)	Banana/plantain juice — 7 Bhavanas	As required
Kamala Pushpa Rasa (कमलपुष्परस)	Lotus flower juice — 7 Bhavanas	As required
Chameli Pushpa Rasa (चमेलीपुष्परस)	Jasminum officinale flower juice — 7 Bhavanas	As required
Ushira Faant (उशीरफाण्ट)	Vetiveria zizanioides cold infusion — 7 Bhavanas	As required
Netrabalaka Faant (नेत्रबालाफाण्ट)	Pavonia odorata cold infusion — 7 Bhavanas	As required
Kasturi Gol (कस्तूरी घोल)	Musk solution — 1 Bhavana (final)	As required
preparation:
1. In a mortar, first Triturate Rasa Sindura finely.
2. Then add all other Bhasmas (Svarna, Abhraka, Loha, Vanga, Pravala, Mukta Pishti) and triturate firmly.
3. Then give 7–7 Bhavanas (levigations) with each of the 10 Bhavana liquids listed (Cow's milk, Sugarcane juice, Vasa Patra Svaras, Turmeric juice/water, Kadalikanda Rasa, Kamala Pushpa Rasa, Chameli Pushpa Rasa, Ushira Faant, Netrabalaka Faant, and Kasturi Gol) — total 70 Bhavanas plus final Kasturi Bhavana (as per text: 50 total Bhavanas with each liquid 7 times).
4. After all Bhavanas are complete, form pills of 1–1 Ratti (125 mg) and dry in shade. Store in a glass vessel.

name: Aananda Bhairav Rasa
benefits: Jeerna Prameha (Chronic Diabetes / Urinary disorders)
ingredients:
Ingredient
(Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Vanga Bhasma (वङ्गभस्म)	Incinerated Tin 	Equal part
Svarna Bhasma (स्वर्णभस्म)	Incinerated Gold 	Equal part
Rasasindura (रससिन्दूर)	Purified Mercuric Sulphide	Equal part
Gunja Moola Churna (गुञ्जामूल)	Abrus precatorius (Rosary pea) root powder — Anupana	As required
preparation: 1. Take equal parts of Vanga Bhasma, Svarna Bhasma and Rasasindura. Triturate together with Madhu (honey) in a mortar.\n2. Form pills of 2 Gunja (250 mg) each. Store in glass vessel. This is Aananda Bhairav Rasa.
anupana: Gunja Moola (Abrus precatorius root) powder and Honey

name: Harishankara Rasa
benefits: Nila Prameha and all types of Prameha (Diabetes / Urinary disorders)
ingredients:
Ingredient
(Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Rasasindura (रससिन्दूर)	Purified Mercuric Sulphide	1 part
Krishna Vajra Abhraka Bhasma (कृष्णवज्राभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated Black Vajra-processed Mica 	1 part
Amalaka Phala Svaras (आमलाफलस्वरस)	Fresh Phyllanthus emblica (Amla) fruit juice — for 7 Bhavanas	As required
preparation: 1. In a mortar, first Triturate Rasasindura thoroughly to fine powder. Add Krishna Vajra Abhraka Bhasma and mix well.\n2. Give 7 Bhavanas with fresh Amalaka (Amla) juice — Triturate and shade-drying each time.\n3. Form pills of 1 Ratti (125 mg) each. Dry in shade and store in glass vessel. This is Harishankara Rasa.

name: Vanga Avaleha
benefits: Treats significantlys all 20 types of Prameha (diabetes and urinary disorders). Especially beneficial in Svapna Meha (nocturnal emission disorder) and general Prameha.
ingredients:
Ingredient
(Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Vanga Bhasma (वङ्गभस्म)	Incinerated Tin 	2 Valla (750 mg); or 60–250 mg per dose
Madhu (मधु)	Honey — for licking with Vanga Bhasma	As required
Shu. Gandhaka (गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur — taken separately with Guda	1 Karsha (10g) equal to Guda
Purana Guda (पुरानागुड़)	Old Jaggery — taken with Gandhaka	1 Karsha (10g) equal to Gandhaka
Guduchi Sattva (गुडूचीसत्त्व)	Tinospora cordifolia pure extract — alternative Anupana	As required
Sharkara (शर्करा)	Sugar — alternative Anupana for Gandhaka	As required
preparation: 1. Take Vanga Bhasma (750 mg or as per dose) and lick with Honey.\n2. Then take Shu. Gandhaka (10g) with old Jaggery (Purana Guda, 10g). Alternatively, take Gandhaka with Guduchi Sattva or with Sugar.\n3. Use daily as per individual strength and condition. This is Vanga Avaleha.
anupana: Madhu (Honey) with Vanga; Purana Guda / Guduchi Sattva / Sharkara with Gandhaka

name: Yogeshvara Rasa
benefits: Prameha, Bahumootra, Ashmari, Mootrakrichha, Vrana, Mahakushtha, Arsha, Bhagandara
ingredients:
Ingredient
(Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	1 part
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	1 part
Loha Bhasma (लोहभस्म)	Incinerated Iron 	1 part
Naga Bhasma (नागभस्म)	Incinerated Lead	1 part
Varatika Bhasma (वराटिकाभस्म)	Incinerated Cowrie shell 	1 part
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated Copper 	1 part
Vanga Bhasma (वङ्गभस्म)	Incinerated Tin 	1 part
Abhraka Bhasma (अभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated Mica 	1 part
Choti Elaichi Churna (छोटी इलायची)	Elettaria cardamomum (Small cardamom) powder	2 parts
Tejpatra Churna (तेजपत्र)	Cinnamomum tamala (Bay leaf) powder	2 parts
Nagarmotha Churna (मोथा)	Cyperus rotundus powder	2 parts
Vayi Vidanga Churna (वायिविडङ्ग)	Embelia ribes (False black pepper) powder	2 parts
Nagkeshar Churna (नागकेशर)	Mesua ferrea (Indian rose chestnut) powder	2 parts
Renuka Beeja Churna (सम्भालू बीज/रेणुका)	Vitex agnus-castus seed powder	2 parts
Amalaka Churna (आँवला)	Phyllanthus emblica (Amla) powder	2 parts
Pippali Moola Churna (पिपरामूल)	Piper longum root powder	2 parts
Amalaka Svaras (आँवलारस)	Phyllanthus emblica (Amla) juice — for 1 Bhavana	As required
preparation: 1. Take all 8 Bhasmas (equal parts) and all 8 herb powders (double parts). Triturate together carefully and thoroughly.\n2. Give one Bhavana (trituration + drying) with Amalaka (Amla) juice.\n3. Form pills of Chana-matra (chickpea size, approx. 250–500 mg). Store in glass vessel. This is Yogeshvara Rasa — as described by Mahadeva (Lord Shiva).

name: Prameha Gaj Simha Rasa
benefits: Prameha (Diabetes / Urinary disorders)
ingredients:
Ingredient
(Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Rasasindura (रससिन्दूर)	Purified Mercuric Sulphide	1 part
Abhraka Bhasma (अभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated Mica  (Upara Rasa = Abhraka Bhasma here)	1 part
Svarna Bhasma (स्वर्णभस्म)	Incinerated Gold 	1 part
Vanga Bhasma (वङ्गभस्म)	Incinerated Tin 	1 part
Naga Bhasma (नागभस्म)	Incinerated Lead	1 part
Shu. Tankana (शु. टङ्कण)	Purified Borax — added in two stages	1/4 part
Chandali / Rakshoghna Pushpa Svaras (चाण्डाली)	Chandali flower juice (Manduki Parni / Bacopa monnieri or Rakshoghna plant)	As required
Rakshasi Pushpa Svaras (राक्षसीपुष्प)	Rakshasi flower juice (uncertain; possibly Datura or Stramonium)	As required
Madhu (मधु)	Honey — used during trituration and in Moosha	As required
Goghrita (गोघृत)	Cow's ghee — added after first Kukkuta Puta	As required
preparation:
1. Triturate all 5 Bhasmas (Rasasindura, Abhraka, Svarna, Vanga, Naga Bhasma) together in a mortar with Chandali and Rakshasi flower juice, Honey and Ghee. Add Tankana (1/4 part). Mix well and form balls. Dry thoroughly in sunlight.
2. Place the dried balls in a Moosha (crucible). Add some Honey into the Moosha. Seal the Moosha with Sandhibandha. Fire in Kukkuta Puta. Allow to cool completely.
3. Remove medicine from Moosha. Add Goghrita (cow's ghee). Place in Moosha again. Seal and fire in Kukkuta Puta a second time.
4. Remove again. Add quarter-part Shu. Tankana. Triturate well. Place in Moosha again, seal and fire in Kukkuta Puta a third time.
5. After complete cooling, powder finely in a mortar and store in glass vessel. This is Prameha Gaj Simha Rasa.

name: Hemgarbha Pottali Ras
benefits: Rajyakshma (Tuberculosis)
ingredients:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Rasa Sindura (रससिन्दूर)	Processed Mercury / Red Mercuric Sulphide	6 grams
Svarna Bhasma (स्वर्णभस्म)	Incinerated Gold 	3 grams
Shu. Gandhaka Churna (शु. गन्धकचूर्ण)	Purified Sulphur powder	6 grams
Badi Varatika (बड़ी वराटिका)	Large Cowrie shells	4–5 pieces
Tankana / Borax (टंकण)	Borax	10 grams
preparation:
17.	In a large stone mortar, Triturate Rasa Sindura to a very fine powder. Add Svarna Bhasma and Shu. Gandhaka powder and triturate together for 1 hour.
18.	Separately, in another mortar, Triturate Tankana (borax) with goat's milk to make a paste (Pesht).
19.	Fill the medicine mixture inside the large Varatika (cowrie shells). Seal the mouth of each cowrie with the Tankana paste.
20.	After drying, arrange the filled cowries in a Sharavasamputa (two clay saucers sealed together). Bind firmly and fire in Gajaputa (cowdung cake fire).
21.	When cooled to body temperature (Svaangasheetal), open the Samputa the next day. Remove the Tankana seal from the mouth of the Varatika.
22.	Triturate the medicine along with the Varatika to a very fine powder (Sukshma Churna). Collect in a glass vessel. This is called 'Hemgarbha Pottali'.

name: Kshayashamak Ras
benefits: Kshaya Roga (Tuberculosis)
ingredients:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	12 grams
Shu. Gandhaka Churna (शु. गन्धकचूर्ण)	Purified Sulphur powder	12 grams
Trikatu Churna (त्रिकटुचूर्ण)	Trikatu powder (Sonth + Mirch + Pippali)	24 grams
Shankha Bhasma (शंखभस्म)	Incinerated Conch shell	48 grams
Varatika Bhasma (वराटिकाभस्म)	Incinerated Cowrie shell 	96 grams
Shu. Tankana (शु. टंकण)	Purified Borax	3 grams
Mirch Churna (मिरचचूर्ण)	Black Pepper powder	195 grams
preparation: 23.	First prepare good Kajjali in a clean mortar by firmly Triturate Parada and Gandhaka together until a smooth black mass forms.\n24.	Add the powders of all other ingredients to the Kajjali and mix well. Triturate for 3 hours until homogeneous.\n25.	Sieve through a fine sieve and collect in a glass vessel. This is 'Kshayashamak Ras' (also called Lokanath Ras by some).

name: Lokanath Ras — 2
benefits: Rajyakshma (Tuberculosis)
ingredients:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	1½ grams (1500 mg)
Shu. Tutha / Tuthya (शु. तुत्थ)	Copper Sulphate	1500 mg
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	12 grams
Shu. Tankana (शु. टंकण)	Purified Borax	12 grams
Shankha Bhasma (शंखभस्म)	Incinerated Conch shell 	12 grams
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated Copper 	23 grams
Varatika Bhasma (वराटिकाभस्म)	Incinerated Cowrie shell (or 9 cowrie shells)	105 grams (Navakharsha)
Arka Patra Svaras (अर्कपत्र स्वरस)	Calotropis gigantea leaf juice — for Bhavana	As required
preparation:
26.	In a clean stone mortar, mix Parada and Gandhaka and Triturate firmly to prepare Kajjali. Add Shu. Tutha and Triturate well.
27.	Then add Shu. Tankana, Shankha Bhasma, Tamra Bhasma and Varatika Bhasma to the Kajjali and mix well.
28.	Give Bhavana with Arka Patra Svaras (Calotropis/Madar leaf juice). Form into a disc (Chakrika). Dry and place in Sharavasamputa. Fire in Varahaputa.
29.	When cooled to body temperature (Svaangasheetal), open the Samputa. Give a second Bhavana with Arka Patra Svaras. Form another Chakrika. Dry, seal in Sharavasamputa and fire again — this time with half the quantity of cowdung cakes (Upala Puta).
30.	When cooled, open the Samputa, remove the medicine, Triturate in mortar and collect in a glass vessel. This is called 'Lokanath Ras'.

name: Mahaveer Ras
benefits: Kshaya (TB), Grahani, Atisara, Kasa, Pandu, Gulma
ingredients:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Shu. Tuthya Bhasma (शु. तुत्थभस्म)	Copper Sulphate ash	6 grams
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	3 grams
Shu. Vatsanabha Churna (शु. वत्सनाभिविषचूर्ण)	Purified Aconitum ferox (aconite) powder	3 grams
Teekshna Loha Bhasma (तीक्ष्णलोहभस्म)	Incinerated Teekshna Iron ash	6 grams
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	12 grams
Moti Pishti (मोतीपिष्ठि)	Pearl powder	12 grams
Rajmriganka Pottali Ras	Added post-firing — 1/4 of total	1/4 of total
Agniparni Svaras (अग्निपर्णी)	Flemingia semialata / Arani juice — Bhavana	As required
Brahmi / Aparajita Svaras (हिरलता)	Bacopa monnieri or Aparajita juice — Bhavana	As required
Bhringaraj Svaras (भृंगराज)	Eclipta prostrata juice — Bhavana	As required
Ardraka Svaras (आद्रिक)	Fresh Ginger juice — Bhavana	As required
Tulasi Svaras (तुलसी)	Basil juice — Bhavana	As required
Langali Kalk (लांगलीकन्द)	Gloriosa superba paste — for Samputa lining	As required
preparation:
31.	Prepare Kajjali from Shu. Parada and Shu. Gandhaka. Add Tuthya Bhasma, Vatsanabha churna, Teekshna Loha Bhasma and Moti Pishti. Triturate all together.
32.	Give sequential Bhavanas — 1 each with: Agniparni (Arani) Svaras, Brahmi/Aparajita Svaras, Bhringaraj Svaras, Ardraka Svaras and Tulasi Svaras.
33.	Form into a large ball. Dry separately. Coat inside of two clay saucers with Langali (Gloriosa superba) paste and dry.
34.	Place the ball (coated with Langali paste) in the clay saucer. Cover, seal and fire in Varahaputa. Cool to body temperature and remove medicine.
35.	Add 1/4 quantity Rajmriganka Pottali Ras and 2 Ratti Shu. Vatsanabha churna. Collect in glass. This is Mahaveer Ras.

name: Panchamrit Ras
benefits: Rajyakshma (Tuberculosis)
ingredients:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Rasa Sindura (रससिन्दूर)	Processed Mercury / Red Mercuric Sulphide	Equal part
Abhraka Bhasma (अभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated Mica 	Equal part
Loha Bhasma (लोहभस्म)	Incinerated Iron 	Equal part
Shu. Shilajatu (शु. शिलाजतु)	Purified Asphaltum / Shilajit	Equal part
Shu. Vatsanabha Churna (शु. वत्सनाभचूर्ण)	Purified Aconitum ferox (aconite) powder	Equal part
Shu. Guggulu (शु. गुग्गुलु)	Purified Commiphora mukul resin	Equal part
Guduchi Kwath (गुडूची काथ)	Tinospora cordifolia decoction — for Guggulu shodhana	As required
Trifala Kwath (त्रिफला काथ)	Trifala decoction — for Guggulu shodhana	As required
preparation:
36.	First purify Guggulu (resin) using Guduchi and Trifala decoction (Shodhana).
37.	In a clean stone mortar, Triturate Rasa Sindura finely. Add Abhraka Bhasma, Loha Bhasma, Shu. Vatsanabha churna and triturate together.
38.	In Trifala decoction, warm Shu. Guggulu and Shu. Shilajatu together until both liquify. Stir continuously. When both are fully dissolved and cooled, mix the Rasa Sindura mixture into the liquid Guggulu in the mortar and triturate thoroughly.
39.	Form tablets of 2 Ratti (250 mg) each. Dry well in shade and store in a glass vessel.If Parada Bhasma is unavailable, substitute with Tamra Bhasma (Incinerated Copper ).

name: Praananatha Ras
benefits: Yakshma/Rajayakshma (TB), Shosha, Udara Roga, Arsha, Grahani, Jwara, Gulma
ingredients:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Loha Bhasma (लोहभस्म)	Incinerated Iron	60 gm (20 Nishka)
Svarna Makshika Bhasma (स्वर्णमाक्षिकभस्म)	Incinerated Chalcopyrite/Iron Pyrite	60 gm
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	3 gm
Tuttha Bhasma (तुत्यभस्म)	Incinerated Copper Sulphate 	3 gm
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	6 gm
Triphala Kwatha (त्रिफलाक्वाथ)	Three-fruit decoction — for trituration	As required
Maricha Churna (मरिचचूर्ण)	Black Pepper powder — added after firing	21 gm (7 Nishka)
Tuttha Bhasma (तुत्यभस्म)	Incinerated Copper Sulphate  — added after firing	30 gm (10 Nishka)
Shu. Tankana (शु. टङ्कण)	Purified Borax — added after firing + cowrie sealing	30 gm (10 Nishka)
Badi Kaudi (Varataka) (बड़ी कौड़ी / वराटका)	Large Cowrie shells — 4 for firing container	4 nos.
Bhringaraja Svaras (भृंगराजस्वरस)	Eclipta alba juice — 7 day Bhavana on Loha Bhasma	3 litres over 7 days
Dhattura Svaras (धत्तूरस्वरस)	Datura leaf juice — for trituration with Loha+Makshika	As required
Bhagini Svaras (भागिनीस्वरस / एक्लिप्टा)	Eclipta alba/Bhangra juice — for trituration	As required
Ardraka Svaras (आर्द्रकस्वरस)	Fresh Ginger juice — for trituration	As required
Bakri Dugdha (बकरीदूध)	Goat's milk — for making Tankana paste (cowrie sealing)	As required
preparation:
1.	First, make good Kajjali of Parada + Gandhaka.
2.	In another mortar, take Loha Bhasma 60 gm and give 7-day Bhavana with Bhringaraja Svaras (3 litres total — 6–7 day process).
3.	Then add Svarna Makshika Bhasma 60 gm to this Loha Bhasma and triturate with Dhatura Svaras, Bhagini Svaras, Triphala Kwatha, and Ardraka Svaras. Fire in Gajaputa. Next day when cool, open Samputa.
4.	Then mix the Kajjali (Parada+Gandhaka) into this fired medicine. Fill into 4 large Varataka (cowrie shells).
5.	Triturate Tankana in goat's milk to make paste and seal the cowrie mouths.
6.	Place all four cowries in Sharava Samputa, clay-seal (Kapadi Mitti), and fire in Gajaputa.
7.	When Svangasheetal, open, remove the clay-coated cowrie shells, remove the Tankana coating, triturate shells with medicine finely.
8.	Then add Maricha Churna 21 gm, Tuttha Bhasma 30 gm, and Shu. Tankana 30 gm — mix thoroughly and store in a glass vessel. This is Praananatha Ras.

name: Rajamrigankura Ras
benefits: Rajyakshma (Tuberculosis)
ingredients:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Rasa Sindura (रससिन्दूर)	Red Mercuric Sulphide	3 parts
Svarna Bhasma (स्वर्णभस्म)	Incinerated Gold 	1 part
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated Copper  (Mruta Tamra)	1 part
Shu. Manaahshila (शु. मन:शिला)	Purified Realgar (Arsenic disulphide)	2 parts
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	2 parts
Shu. Haritala (शु. हरताल)	Purified Orpiment (Arsenic trisulphide)	2 parts
Pippali Churna (पिप्पलीचूर्ण)	Long Pepper powder — Anupana	10 grains per dose
Maricha Churna (मरिचचूर्ण)	Black Pepper powder — Anupana	19 grains per dose
Varataka (Kaudi) (वराटका / कौड़ी)	Large Cowrie shells — 8–10 shells for firing	8–10 nos.
Shu. Tankana (शु. टङ्कण)	Purified Borax — for sealing cowrie mouth	As required
Bakri Dugdha (बकरीदूध)	Goat's milk — toTriturate Tankana paste	As required
preparation:
1.	In a stone mortar, first triturate Rasa Sindura very finely.
2.	Then add all other substances (Svarna Bhasma, Tamra Bhasma, Shu. Manaahshila, Shu. Gandhaka, Shu. Haritala) and triturate together for 1 hour.
3.	Take 8–10 large cowrie shells (the type used as paper weights/Varataka). Triturate Tankana in goat's milk to make a paste.
4.	Fill the triturated medicine into the cowrie shells. Apply the Tankana paste to seal the cowrie mouths. Dry in sun.
5.	Place all medicine-filled cowries in a Sharava Samputa sealed with clay (Kapadi Mitti) and fire in Gajaputa.
6.	Next day when Svangasheetal, open the Samputa, remove the Tankana coating from cowrie mouths, and triturate cowries with medicine together finely.
7.	Filter through 200-mesh strainer and store in a glass vessel. This is Rajamrigankura Ras.

name: Shankeshwara Ras
benefits: Rajyakshma (Tuberculosis)
ingredients:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Shankhanaabha Bhasma (शंखनाभिभस्म)	Conch-navel ash (Turbinella pyrum ash)	3 gm (1 Nishka)
Kaudi Bhasma (Varataka) (कौड़ीभस्म / वराटका)	Incinerated Cowrie shell 	12 gm (4 Nishka)
Shu. Tuttha Bhasma (शु. तुत्यभस्म)	Purified Incinerated Copper Sulphate 	1.5 gm (half Nishka)
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur — equal to all above combined	16.5 gm
Naga Bhasma (नागभस्म)	Incinerated Lead — equal to Gandhaka	16.5 gm
Rasa Sindura (रससिन्दूर)	Red Mercuric Sulphide — equal to Naga Bhasma	16.5 gm
Shu. Tankana (शु. टङ्कण)	Purified Borax — equal to Rasa Sindura	16.5 gm
Pippali Churna (पिप्पलीचूर्ण)	Long Pepper powder — Anupana	As required
Bakri Dugdha (बकरीदूध)	Goat's milk — for Tankana paste (cowrie sealing)	As required
Madhu (मधु)	Honey — Anupana	As required
preparation:
1.	In a stone mortar, first triturate Rasa Sindura finely.
2.	Then add Shankhanaabha Bhasma, Kaudi Bhasma, Shu. Tuttha Bhasma, Shu. Gandhaka, and Naga Bhasma and triturate well with Rasa Sindura for 1 hour.
3.	Take large cowrie shells and fill with the medicine. Triturate Tankana in goat's milk to make paste, and seal the cowrie mouths.
4.	Dry, then place in Sharava Samputa sealed with clay, and fire in Gajaputa.
5.	When Svangasheetal, open, remove Tankana coating, triturate cowries with medicine, filter finely through cloth and store in a glass vessel. This is Shankeshwara Ras.

name: Vajra Ras
benefits: Kshaya Roga / Rajayakshma (Pulmonary Tuberculosis)
ingredients:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Yashadha Bhasma (Kharpara Sattva) (यशदभस्म / खर्परसत्त्व)	Incinerated Zinc oxide — pre-soaked in Svarna 6 months	12 gm (1 Karsha)
Svarna Bhasma (स्वर्णभस्म)	Incinerated Gold 	6 gm
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	18 gm (6 Nishka)
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	24 gm (8 Nishka)
Pravala Bhasma (प्रवालभस्म)	Incinerated Coral 	6 gm
Mukta Bhasma (मुक्ताभस्म)	Incinerated Pearl 	6 gm
Loha Bhasma (लोहभस्म)	Incinerated Iron	6 gm
Naga Bhasma (नागभस्म)	Incinerated Lead 	9 gm
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated Copper 	12 gm
Mahajambira Nimbu Svaras (महाजम्बीरनीरस)	Large lemon juice — forTriturate all ingredients	2 Prastha (~3 litres)
Maricha Churna (मरिचचूर्ण)	Black Pepper powder — for final dose preparation	As required (half Masha per dose)
Chaagneri Svaras (चाङ्गेरीस्वरस)	Oxalis corniculata juice — for trituration (3 hours)	As required (2 Prastha / ~1.5 litres)
Madhu (मधु)	Honey — for mixing dose on betel leaf	As required
Tambula Patra (ताम्बूलपत्र)	Betel leaf — for applying and licking the dose	As required
preparation:
1.	First make Kajjali from Parada + Gandhaka.
2.	Add all Bhasmas (Yashadha, Svarna, Pravala, Mukta, Loha, Naga, Tamra) to the Kajjali and triturate in mortar with Chaagneri Svaras for 9 hours. Note: Neelavata is resinous — leave it out.
3.	Then take Abhraka Bhasma, Kanta Loha Bhasma, and Shu. Haritala — 6–6 gm each. Also add: Ankkolanka Bija Churna (Alangium salvifolium), Malakaanguni Bija Churna, and Shu. Tuttha — 4 Nishka (12 gm) each; Shu. Tankana Kshara 24 gm; Kaudi Bhasma 60 gm.
4.	Triturate everything together in the Chaagneri-triturated medicine with 2 Prastha (1,500 ml) Jambiri Nimbu Svaras for 2–3 days and form a large ball.
5.	Dry, seal in Sharava Samputa, and fire in 1 Khari (2 quintals) rice husk fire.
6.	Then fire once more with Vanaupala (stone cooking) 1 Bhara (94 kg).
7.	When Svangasheetal, triturate in mortar and store in a glass vessel. This is Vajra Ras.
anupana: Maricha Churna + Gandhaka + Madhu on Tambula Patra (betel leaf)

name: Mrigankupottali Ras
benefits: Kshaya / Rajayakshma (Pulmonary Tuberculosis)
ingredients:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Shankhanaabha Churna (शंखनाभिचूर्ण)	Conch-navel powder — for making the Musha crucible	48 gm (16 Nishka)
Rasa Sindura (रससिन्दूर)	Red Mercuric Sulphide	1,500 mg (1.5 gm)
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	3 gm
Pippali Churna (पिप्पलीचूर्ण)	Long Pepper powder — Anupana	As required
Gau Ksheera (गौक्षीर)	Cow's milk — for trituration to make Musha paste	As required
Mritika (Clay) (मिट्टी)	Fine clay — for coating the cloth-wrapped Musha	As required
Vastra (Cloth) (वस्त्र)	Two layers of clean cloth — for wrapping Musha	As required
Madhu (मधु)	Honey — Anupana	As required
preparation:
1.	First, take Shankhanaabha Churna 48 gm and triturate thoroughly with Gau Ksheera (cow's milk) for 4–5 hours.
2.	From this thick paste, form a Musha (small crucible pot) of dose size with a lid — dry well in sunlight.
3.	In a separate clean mortar, triturate Rasa Sindura and Shu. Gandhaka to form very fine powder-mix.
4.	Fill this Rasa Sindura + Gandhaka powder into the dried Shankhanaabhi Musha. Close the lid tightly.
5.	Wrap the sealed Musha in two layers of clean cloth (Vastrabaddha). Apply Mritika (clay) all around the outside. Dry well.
6.	Place this clay-coated Musha inside a Sharava Samputa, seal the Samputa with Kapadi Mitti (clay), and fire in Gajaputa.
7.	Next day when Svangasheetal (cooled to body temperature), open the Samputa, remove the Musha, break it open gently and collect the medicine inside.
8.	Triturate finely in mortar and store in a glass vessel. This is Mrigankupottali Ras.

name: Chandrakala Ras
benefits: Raktapitta, Pitta Roga, Jwara, Mutra-kricchhra
ingredients:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	12 grams
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated Copper 	12 grams
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	24 grams
Chirayata Churna (चिरायताचूर्ण)	Swertia chirata powder	12 grams
Guduchi Satva (गुडूचीसत्त्व)	Tinospora cordifolia extract	12 grams
Pitpapada Churna (पितपापडाचूर्ण)	Fumaria officinalis powder	12 grams
Ushira Churna (उशीरचूर्ण)	Vetiveria zizanioides (khus root) powder	12 grams
Pippali Churna (पिप्पलीचूर्ण)	Long Pepper powder	12 grams
Singhada Churna (सिंघाडाचूर्ण)	Trapa bispinosa (water chestnut) powder	12 grams
Anantamool Churna (अनन्तमूलचूर्ण)	Hemidesmus indicus powder	12 grams
Bhavana Dravyas	Musta Kwath, Dadima Svaras, Durva Svaras, Ketaki Vrikshatva Ras, Sahadevi Ras, Kumari Svaras, Parpata Kwath, Ramashetalika Svaras, Shatavari Svaras — one Bhavana each per day. Then add equal parts of: Tikta Guduchi Satva, Parpata, Ushira and Pippali powder, Shringata and Sariva (Hemidesmus indicus) — as fine powder. Then give 7 Bhavanas with Drakshaadi Kwath (raisin/grape decoction compound). Form tablets of Chanak size (250 mg). Dry in shade and store in glass.	As required
preparation:
41.	Grind Parada and Tamra Bhasma in a mortar for three days, making small balls each time and then drying them on fire. After drying, re-grind until fine.
42.	Combine with Shu. Gandhaka and all 10 dry ingredient powders (12 grams each) and prepare Kajjali.
43.	Give sequential Bhavanas — one per day — with each of the 9 Bhavana dravyas listed above.
44.	Add the fine powders of Tikta Guduchi Satva, Parpata, Ushira, Pippali, Shringata and Sariva (equal parts — 12 grams each) to the Kajjali.
45.	Give 7 Bhavanas with Drakshaadi Kwath. Form tablets of 250 mg (Chanak size). Dry in shade and store in glass. This is Chandrakala Ras.

name: Patoladi Yog
benefits: Raktapitta, Raktasrava (bleeding/haemorrhage)
ingredients:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Patola Patra (पटोलपत्र)	Trichosanthes dioica (pointed gourd) leaf powder	1 part
Loha Bhasma (लोहभस्म)	Incinerated Iron 	1 part
Rasa Sindura (रससिन्दूर)	Processed Mercury / Red Mercuric Sulphide	1 part
Loharivarga Kwath (लोहारिवर्ग काथ)	Loharivarga group decoction — for Bhavana	As required
preparation: 1.	Take Patola Patra (Trichosanthes dioica/pointed gourd leaf), Loha Bhasma (Incinerated Iron ) and Rasa Sindura (processed mercury) — one part each.\n2.	Give Bhavana with the decoction of Loharivarga group of herbs.\n3.	Form tablets of 250 mg. Dry in shade and collect in a glass vessel.

name: Rasendra Yog
benefits: Darunam Raktapittam (Severe haemorrhagic disorders)
ingredients:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Vasa Patra Svaras (वासापत्रस्वरस)	Adhatoda vasica (Malabar nut) leaf juice	12 ml (1 Karsha)
Rasa Sindura (रससिन्दूर)	Processed Mercury / Red Mercuric Sulphide	125 mg
Madhu (मधु)	Honey	5 grams
Shakkara / Cheeni (शक्कर/चीनी)	Sugar	5 grams
dose: 125 mg Rasa Sindura + 12 ml Vasa juice
preparation: 46.	Take fresh Vasa (Adhatoda vasica) leaves and extract juice to get 12 ml (1 Karsha).\n47.	Mix Rasa Sindura (125 mg), Madhu (5 grams) and Shakkara (5 grams) together with the Vasa juice.\n48.	Administer as Lehan (licking preparation) — take morning and evening daily.
anupana: Vasa Patra Svaras + Madhu + Sugar (as Lehan)

name: Kapardak Ras
benefits: Raktapitta
ingredients:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Rasa Sindura (रस सिन्दूर)	Processed Mercury / Red Mercuric Sulphide	As required
Karpasa Pushpa Ras (कपास के फूलों का रस)	Cotton flower juice (Gossypium herbaceum)	For 1-day trituration
Varatika (कौड़ियाँ)	Cowrie shells — as container for firing	As required
Kali Mirch (काली मिर्च)	Black Pepper — double the medicine weight	Double quantity
Ghrita (घी)	Ghee — for Lehan and for cooking Udumbara	As required
Udumbara Phala (गूलर का फल)	Ripe Ficus racemosa (cluster fig) fruits	As required
Sita / Mishri (मिश्री)	Sugar candy	As required
preparation:
49.	Grind Rasa Sindura thoroughly in cotton flower (Karpasa Pushpa) juice for one full day.
50.	Fill the medicine inside cowrie shells (Varatika). Seal inside an Andhamusha (blind crucible) and fire in Puta.
51.	When cooled to body temperature (Svaang Sheetal), remove andTriturate very finely with double quantity of black pepper (Kali Mirch).
52.	Administer 1 Gunja (125 mg) as Lehan (licking) with Ghee. This is Kapardak Ras.
53.	Additionally: Cook ripe Udumbara (Gular/Ficus racemosa) fruits in Ghee with Mishri (sugar candy). Eat with Kali Mirch totreats significantly even difficult (Dussadhya) Raktapitta.
reference: Bharat Bhaishajya Ratnakar, part-1, verse 950, page no 285

name: Amalkyadi Loha
benefits: Amalkyadi Lohatreats significantlys: Raktapitta (haemorrhagic disorders), Amla-pitta (hyperacidity/acid reflux) and many diseases of Pitta and Vata origin. It is also: Vrishya (aphrodisiac/reproductive tonic), Agni-deepana (digestive stimulant/kindles Agni) and Balakarak (strength-giving).
ingredients:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Amalaa / Amalaki (आमला)	Phyllanthus emblica (Indian gooseberry)	1 part
Peepal / Pippali (पीपल)	Long Pepper (Piper longum)	1 part
Cheeni / Sita (चीनी/सिता)	Sugar / Mishri (sugar candy)	2 parts
Loha Bhasma (लोहभस्म)	Incinerated Iron 	4 parts
preparation: 54.	Take Amalaki powder (1 part), Pippali powder (1 part), Cheeni/Sita (2 parts) and Loha Bhasma (4 parts).\n55.	Grind all finely and mix thoroughly until homogeneous.\n56.	Store in a glass vessel. This is Amalkyadi Loha (also called Yogaraja).
reference: Bharat Bhaishajya Ratnakar, part-1, verse 443, page no 153

name: Arkeshwar Ras
benefits: Sudaarunam Raktapitta (Severe haemorrhagic disorders)
ingredients:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated Copper 	Equal part
Vanga Bhasma (बंगभस्म)	Incinerated Tin 	Equal part
Abhraka Bhasma (अभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated Mica 	Equal part
Svarna Makshika Bhasma (स्वर्णमाक्षिकभस्म)	Incinerated Chalcopyrite/Iron Pyrite 	Equal part
Guduchi Svaras (गुरुचके स्वरस)	Tinospora cordifolia juice — 21-day Bhavana	As required
preparation: 57.	Take all four Bhasmas in equal parts and mix together.\n58.	Give Bhavana with Guduchi (Giloy/Tinospora cordifolia) juice for 21 consecutive days (3 Saptaha = 3 weeks),Triturate well each day.\n59.	After Bhavana is complete, give Puta (fire). When cooled (Svaangasheetal), remove andTriturate well. This is Arkeshwar Ras.
anupana: Adusa/Vasa Svaras or Ksheeravidari Kanda Churna

name: Raktapitta Ankusha Ras
benefits: Raktapitta, Grahani-Raktadosha, Jwara, Daha
ingredients:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Jeera Kwath (जीरा क्वाथ)	Cumin decoction — for administration	As required
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	12 grams
Shu. Hingula (शु. हिंगुल)	Purified Cinnabar (Mercuric Sulphide)	12 grams
Murgi ka Anda (मुर्गी का अण्डा)	Hen's eggs — for trituration medium	2 eggs
Shu. Tankana-kshara (शु. टंकणक्षार)	Purified Borax alkali	12 grams
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	12 grams
Goghrita (गोघृत)	Cow's Ghee	12 grams
preparation:
60.	Grind Parada and Hingula together in a mortar. Give Bhavana with hen's egg liquid (Murgi ka Anda Drava). Process through Urddhvapatan Yantra (upward distillation apparatus) to obtain distilled Parada.
61.	In a clean mortar, triturate the Urddhvapatita Parada (distilled mercury) with Shu. Gandhaka to make good Kajjali.
62.	Add Tankana-kshara (borax alkali) and triturate. Then triturate the whole Kajjali with Goghrita (cow's ghee).
63.	Form tablets of 250 mg each. Dry in shade and collect in glass vessel. This is Raktapitta Ankusha Ras.
64.	Administer 250 mg with Jeera Kwath (cumin decoction) for 3 days up to 3 Masha (3 months).

name: Rasamrit Ras
benefits: Pitta, Amla-pitta, Raktapitta, Sarva Jwara
ingredients:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	1 part
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	2 parts
Svarna Makshika Bhasma (स्वर्णमाक्षिकभस्म)	Incinerated Chalcopyrite/Iron Pyrite 	1 part
Shu. Shilajatu (शु. शिलाजीत)	Purified Asphaltum / Shilajit	1 part
Guduchi (गुरुच)	Tinospora cordifolia	1 part
Chandana (चन्दन)	Sandalwood (Santalum album)	1 part
Munakka / Draksha (मुनका)	Raisins (Vitis vinifera)	1 part
Mahua / Madhupushpa (महुआ)	Madhuca longifolia flower	1 part
Dhania (धनियाँ)	Coriander (Coriandrum sativum)	1 part
Kutaja Chhal (कुटज की छाल)	Holarrhena antidysenterica bark	1 part
Indrajou / Kutaja Beej (इन्द्रजौ)	Holarrhena antidysenterica seeds	1 part
Dhataki Phool (धायके फूल)	Woodfordia fruticosa flowers	1 part
Neem Patti (नीमकी पत्ती)	Azadirachta indica (neem) leaf	1 part
Mulethi / Yashtimadhu (मुलेठी)	Glycyrrhiza glabra (licorice)	1 part
Madhu (मधु)	Honey — for mixing and administration	As required
Mishri (मिश्री)	Sugar candy — for administration	As required
Dharoshna Dugdha (धारोष्ण दूध)	Fresh warm milk — for administration	As required
preparation: 1.	Prepare Kajjali from Shu. Parada (1 part) and Shu. Gandhaka (2 parts). Add all other ingredients (each 1 part) as fine powders.\n2.	Add honey and Mishri. Triturate all together thoroughly until homogeneous.\n3.	Store in a glass vessel. This is Rasamrit Ras.

name: Shatashoolyas Loha
benefits: Raktapitta (haemorrhagic disorders), Trishna, Daha, Jwara, Vamana, Raktapitta
ingredients:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Shatavara / Shatamool (शतावर)	Asparagus racemosus	Equal part
Mishri / Sita (मिश्री)	Sugar candy	Equal part
Dhania (धनियाँ)	Coriander (Coriandrum sativum)	Equal part
Nagakeshar (नागकेशार)	Mesua ferrea (cobra's saffron)	Equal part
Chandana (चन्दन)	Sandalwood (Santalum album)	Equal part
Trifala (त्रिफला)	Haritaki + Bibhitaki + Amalaki	Equal part
Sonth (सोंठ)	Dry Ginger	Equal part
Mirch (मिर्च)	Black Pepper	Equal part
Pippali (पिप्पली)	Long Pepper	Equal part
Motha (मोथा)	Cyperus rotundus (nut grass)	Equal part
Vayavidanga (बायविडंग)	Embelia ribes	Equal part
Chitraka (चीता)	Plumbago zeylanica	Equal part
Til (तिल)	Sesame (Sesamum indicum)	Equal part
Adusa / Vasa (अड़ूसा)	Adhatoda vasica — for auxiliary decoction	As required
Munakka (मुनका)	Raisins — for auxiliary decoction	As required
Harad / Haritaki (हरड़)	Terminalia chebula — for auxiliary decoction	As required
Loha Bhasma (लोहभस्म)	Incinerated Iron 	Equal to total of all above
Abhraka Bhasma (अभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated Mica  — for auxiliary treatment	As required
Pitpapada Svaras (पितपापडे रस)	Fumaria officinalis juice — auxiliary	As required
preparation: 68.	Take all 13 herbal ingredients in equal powder parts. Add Loha Bhasma equal to the combined total weight.\n69.	Grind all together thoroughly and form tablets (Goli). Dry in shade and store in glass. This is Shatashoolyas Loha.
anupana: With Abhraka Bhasma + Pitpapada juice; or Adusa + Munakka + Harad decoction with sugar

name: Sudhanidhi Ras
benefits: Raktapitta (Haemorrhagic disorders)
ingredients:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Shu. Parada (शु. पारा)	Purified Mercury	Equal part
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	Equal part
Svarna Makshika Bhasma (स्वर्णमाक्षिकभस्म)	Incinerated Chalcopyrite/Iron Pyrite 	Equal part
Loha Bhasma (लोहभस्म)	Incinerated Iron 	Equal part
Trifala Kwath (त्रिफलाके काथ)	Trifala decoction — for cooking	As required
Gomutra (गोमूत्र)	Cow's urine — alternative to Trifala Kwath	As required
preparation:
70.	Take Shu. Parada and Shu. Gandhaka in equal parts. Prepare Kajjali (black sulphide mixture) in a clean mortar.
71.	Add Svarna Makshika Bhasma and Loha Bhasma (equal parts each). Mix all together.
72.	Place all in a Loha Patra (iron vessel). Cook with Trifala Kwath or Gomutra using Upala (cowdung cake) fire until solidified.
73.	When cooked, remove and form tablets (Goli). Store in glass. This is Sudhanidhi Ras.

name: Shirорogari Ras
benefits: Suryavarta (solar migraine) and Shiro Roga (head diseases)
ingredients:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Parada Bhasma (पारदभस्म)	Incinerated Mercury ash	Equal part
Abhraka Bhasma (अभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated Mica 	Equal part
Tiksna Loha Bhasma (तीक्ष्णलोहभस्म)	Incinerated Iron 	Equal part
Kanta Loha Bhasma (कान्तलोहभस्म)	Incinerated Magnetic Iron	Equal part
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated Copper 	Equal part
Snuhi Ksheera (स्नुहीक्षीर)	Euphorbia nerifolia milk sap — for 1-day trituration	As required
preparation: 1. Take Parada Bhasma, Abhraka Bhasma, Tiksna Loha Bhasma, Kanta Loha Bhasma, and Tamra Bhasma — all in equal parts. Combine in a mortar and triturate with Snuhi Ksheera (Euphorbia nerifolia milk sap) for one full day.\n2. Form pills of Udad (black gram/urad) size. Store in a glass vessel.
reference: Bharat Bhaishajya Ratnakar, part-5, verse 7588, page no 121

name: Chandrakanta Ras — Shiroroga Hari
benefits: Suryavarta (solar headache/migraine) and all Shiro Roga (head diseases)
ingredients:
Take equal parts (Sama Bhaga) of all five Bhasmas:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Rasa Sindura (रससिन्दूर)	Red Mercuric Sulphide (Mruta Soota)	Equal part
Abhraka Bhasma (अभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated Mica 	Equal part
Tiksna Loha Bhasma (तीक्ष्णलोहभस्म)	Incinerated Iron 	Equal part
Kanta Loha Bhasma (कान्तलोहभस्म)	Incinerated Magnetic Iron	Equal part
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated Copper 	Equal part
Snuhi Ksheera (स्नुहीक्षीर)	Euphorbia nerifolia milk sap — for 1-day Bhavana	As required
Sukhoshan Goghrita (सुखोष्ण गोघृत)	Warm cow's ghee — Anupana vehicle	As required
preparation:
1. Take all five Bhasmas in equal parts and combine in a mortar.
2. Triturate together with Snuhi Ksheera (Euphorbia nerifolia milk sap) for one full day, giving repeated Bhavana until well incorporated.
3. Form pills of 1 Masha (Udad/black gram size = 65 mg / half Ratti). Dry in shade and store in a glass vessel.
4. Consuming 1–2 pills with Sukhoshan Goghrita (warm cow's ghee) cures Suryavarta (solar migraine/headache that worsens with sunlight) and all types of Shiro Roga (head diseases) within one week.
5. This is called Chandrakanta Ras — also known as Shiroroga Hari (Treats significantlyer of head diseases).

name: Mahalakshmi Vilas Ras
benefits: Sannipata Roga (tri-Dosha fever/critical conditions) and Shiro Roga (head diseases)
ingredients:
Take equal parts of all the following:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Loha Bhasma (लोहभस्म)	Incinerated Iron 	Equal part
Abhraka Bhasma (अभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated Mica 	Equal part
Shu. Visha (शु. विष)	Purified Aconitum ferox (Vatsanabha)	Equal part
Musta Churna (मुस्ताचूर्ण / मोथा)	Cyperus rotundus powder	Equal part
Haritaki Churna (हरीतकीचूर्ण / हरां)	Terminalia chebula powder	Equal part
Bibhitaka Churna (बिभीतकचूर्ण / बहेड़ा)	Terminalia bellirica powder	Equal part
Amalaki Churna (आमलकीचूर्ण / आँवला)	Emblica officinalis powder	Equal part
Shunthi Churna (शुण्ठीचूर्ण / सोंठ)	Dry Ginger powder	Equal part
Maricha Churna (मरिचचूर्ण / मिर्च)	Black Pepper powder	Equal part
Pippali Churna (पिप्पलीचूर्ण)	Long Pepper powder	Equal part
Dhattura Bija Churna (धत्तूरेबीजचूर्ण)	Datura metel seed powder	Equal part
Vriddhadaru Bija Churna (विधारेकेबीजचूर्ण)	Argyreia nervosa seed powder	Equal part
Indraashana Bija Churna (इन्द्राशनबीजचूर्ण / भॉगकेबीज)	Citrullus colocynthis seed powder (both large + small)	Equal part
Laghu Gokshura Churna (लघुगोखरूचूर्ण)	Small Tribulus terrestris powder	Equal part
Brihad Gokshura Churna (बृहदगोखरूचूर्ण)	Large Tribulus terrestris powder	Equal part
Pippali Mool Churna (पिप्पलीमूलचूर्ण)	Long Pepper root powder	Equal part
Dhattura Svaras (धत्तूरस्वरस)	Datura metel juice — for Bhavana	As required
preparation: 1. Take all the above substances in equal parts and prepare fine Churna. Combine all together in a mortar.\n2. Give Bhavana with Dhattura Svaras (Datura metel juice). Triturate well and form pills of 2 Gunja (approx. 250 mg) each.\n3. Dry in shade and store in a glass vessel.

name: Mihirodaya Vati
benefits: Ardhavabhedaka, Anantavata, Suryavarta, Shankha Roga — all Shiro Roga
ingredients:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Loha Bhasma (लोहभस्म)	Incinerated Iron 	1 part
Abhraka Bhasma (अभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated Mica 	1 part
Svarna Bhasma (स्वर्णभस्म)	Incinerated Gold 	1 part
Vidruma / Pravala Bhasma (विद्रुम / मूँगाभस्म)	Incinerated Coral 	1 part
Kanta Pashana Bhasma (कान्तपाषाण / राजपट्टक)		1 part
Rasa Sindura (रससिन्दूर)	Red Mercuric Sulphide	2 parts
Jatamamsi Kwatha (जटामांसीक्वाथ)	Nardostachys jatamansi decoction — 1 day Bhavana	As required
Haritaki Churna (हरीतकीचूर्ण)	Terminalia chebula powder — Anupana	As required
Gau Dugdha (गौदुग्ध)	Cow's milk — Anupana vehicle	As required
preparation: 1. Take Loha Bhasma, Abhraka Bhasma, Svarna Bhasma, Pravala Bhasma, and Kanta Pashana Bhasma — 1 part each — and Rasa Sindura 2 parts. Combine all in a mortar and triturate together.\n2. Give 1 day Bhavana with Eranda Mool Svaras (castor root juice), and then 1 day Bhavana with Jatamamsi Kwatha (Spikenard decoction). Form pills of 2 Ratti (250 mg) each. Dry in shade and store in a glass vessel.
anupana: Haritaki Churna (or Murabba) with Gau Dugdha (milk)
reference: Bharat Bhaishajya Ratnakar, part-4, verse 5606, page no 234

name: Rasachandrika Vati
benefits: Shiro Roga (all head diseases), Amavata (rheumatoid arthritis), Shleshma Roga (Kapha diseases), Manyastambha (cervical spondylosis), Galagraha (throat obstruction), Grahani (malabsorption), Shleepada (elephantiasis), Antravriddhi (hernia), Bhagandara (anal fistula), Kamala (jaundice), Shotha (oedema), Pandu (anaemia), Peenas (sinusitis), Arsha (haemorrhoids), and Guda Roga (rectal diseases).
ingredients:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Trailokya Vijaya Bija (त्रैलोक्यविजयाबीज)	Cannabis sativa seed	Equal part
Unmatta Bija (उन्मत्त बीज / धतूरेकेबीज)	Datura metel seed	Equal part
Kantakari Bija (कण्टकारीबीज / कटेरीकेबीज)	Solanum xanthocarpum seed	Equal part
Ela Bija (एलाबीज / इलाइचीबीज)	Cardamom seed	Equal part
Vriddhadaru Bija (वृद्धिदारु बीज / विधारेकेबीज)	Argyreia nervosa seed	Equal part
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	Equal part
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury — for Kajjali with Gandhaka	Equal part
Ardraka Svaras (आर्द्रकस्वरस)	Fresh Ginger juice — for Bhavana	As required
Sheetal Jala (शीतल जल)	Cold water — Anupana	As required
preparation: 1. First make Kajjali from Shu. Parada and Shu. Gandhaka in a mortar.\n2. Then add all other seed powders (Trailokya Vijaya Bija, Unmatta/Datura Bija, Kantakari Bija, Ela Bija, Vriddhadaru Bija) — all in equal parts — to the Kajjali and triturate together.\n3. Give Bhavana with Ardraka Svaras (fresh ginger juice). Triturate until well incorporated. Form pills of Kalaya (pea) size. Store in a glass vessel.
anupana: Sheetal Jala (cold water) with Pathya diet

name: Shirovajra Ras
benefits: All types of Shiro Roga — Vataja, Pittaja, Kaphaja, Sannipataja
ingredients:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Shu. Parada (शु. पारा)	Purified Mercury	1 Pala
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	1 Pala
Loha Bhasma (लोहभस्म)	Incinerated Iron 	1 Pala
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated Copper 	1 Pala
Guggulu (गुग्गुलु)	Commiphora mukul resin	4 Pala
Trifala Churna (त्रिफलाका चूर्ण)	Trifala powder (Haritaki + Bibhitaki + Amalaki)	2 Pala
Mulethi / Yashtimadhu (मुलेठी)	Glycyrrhiza glabra (licorice)	1 Tola
Pippali (पिप्पली)	Long Pepper (Piper longum)	1 Tola
Sonth (सोंठ)	Dry Ginger (Zingiber officinale)	1 Tola
Gokshura (गोखरू)	Tribulus terrestris	1 Tola
Vayavidanga (वायविडंग)	Embelia ribes	1 Tola
Dashamool (दशमूल)	Ten-roots compound	1 Tola
Dashamool Kwath (दशमूलके रस)	Ten-roots decoction — for Bhavana	As required
Ghrita (घी)	Ghee — for tablet forming	As required
preparation: 74.	Prepare Kajjali from Shu. Parada and Shu. Gandhaka (1 Pala each). Add Loha Bhasma and Tamra Bhasma (1 Pala each).\n75.	Add Guggulu (4 Pala), Trifala churna (2 Pala) and all herbal powders (1 Tola each). Mix all together.\n76.	Give Bhavana with Dashamool (ten roots) decoction. Form tablets of 1 Masha each with Ghee. Dry in shade. This is Shirovajra Ras.
anupana: Goat's milk (Chhagi Dugdha), Honey or Milk

name: Triyushanadi Gutika
benefits: Treats significantlys: Mukha Roga (oral/mouth diseases), Shiro Roga (head diseases/headaches), Bhrama (vertigo/giddiness), Patal (corneal opacity), Timira (dim vision/cataract), Pithaka (pimples/skin eruptions), Shukra Roga (white oral lesions), Arbuda (tumours/growths) and Palita Roga (premature greying of hair). It is also Pishttahara (removes white lesions), Mukha-Kamakara (enhances attractiveness of face/mouth) and increases Kamashakti (sexual vigour).
ingredients:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Sonth (सोंठ)	Dry Ginger	1 Karsha each
Mirch (मिर्च)	Black Pepper	1 Karsha
Peepal / Pippali (पीपल)	Long Pepper	1 Karsha
Atees / Ativisha (अतीस)	Aconitum heterophyllum	1 Karsha
Javakhar (जवाखार)	Potassium carbonate	1 Karsha
Sajji-khar (सजी-खार)	Sodium carbonate	1 Karsha
Harad / Haritaki (हरड़)	Terminalia chebula	1 Karsha
Baheda / Bibhitaki (बहेड़ा)	Terminalia bellirica	1 Karsha
Amla / Amalaki (आमला)	Phyllanthus emblica	1 Karsha
Nisoth / Trivrit (निसोत)	Ipomoea turpethum	1 Karsha
Danti-mool (दन्तीमूल)	Baliospermum montanum root	1 Karsha
Vasa / Adusa (वासा)	Adhatoda vasica	1 Karsha
Lodhra (लोध)	Symplocos racemosa	1 Karsha
Tagar (तगर)	Valeriana wallichii	1 Karsha
Chandana (चन्दन)	Sandalwood (Santalum album)	1 Karsha
Gajpeepal (गजपील)	Scindapsus officinalis	1 Karsha
Sugandha-bala (सुगन्धबाला)	Pavonia odorata	1 Karsha

Pokharmool (पोखरमूल)	Inula racemosa root	1 Karsha
Motha (मोथा)	Cyperus rotundus	1 Karsha
Kutaki (कुटकी)	Picrorhiza kurroa	1 Karsha
Kavaphala (काबफल)	Randia dumetorum	1 Karsha
Indrajou (इन्द्रजौ)	Holarrhena antidysenterica seed	1 Karsha
Dalchini (दालचीनी)	Cinnamomum zeylanicum (cinnamon)	1 Karsha
Tejpat (तेजपात)	Cinnamomum tamala (Indian bay leaf)	1 Karsha
Nagarmotha (नागरमोथा)	Cyperus scariosus	1 Karsha
Neelkamal (नीलकमल)	Nymphaea caerulea (blue lotus)	1 Karsha
Kachchi-mooli (कच्ची-मूली)	Fresh radish (Raphanus sativus)	1 Karsha
Shu. Haritala (शु. हरताल)	Purified Orpiment (Arsenic trisulphide)	1 Karsha
Jayaphala (जायफल)	Nutmeg (Myristica fragrans)	1 Karsha
Shilajatu (शिलाजतु)	Purified Asphaltum / Shilajit	8 Pala
Loha Bhasma (लोहभस्म)	Incinerated Iron 	8 Pala
Bansalochana (बंसलोचन)	Bambusa arundinacea (bamboo) silica/tabasheer	2 Karsha
preparation:
77.	Take all 31 herbal/mineral ingredients in specified quantities andperform Mardana procedure each to fine powder.
78.	Combine all powders together with Shilajatu (8 Pala), Loha Bhasma (8 Pala) and Bansalochana (2 Karsha).
79.	Grind all together with water (Pani) in a mortar until homogeneous. Form tablets (Goli). Dry in shade. This is Triyushanadi Gutika.
80.	For Kamashakti (sexual vigour) enhancement, take with milk.
reference: Bharat Bhaishajya Ratnakar, part-2, verse 2784, page no 406

name: Abhrakadi Vati
benefits: Suryavarta (migraine/sun-related headache) and Shiro Roga
ingredients:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Abhraka-jirna Parada / Abhraka Bhasma (अभ्रकजीर्णपारद / अभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated Mica-saturated mercury or Mica	Equal part
Parada Bhasma / Rasa Sindura (पारदभस्म = रससिन्दूर)	Processed Mercury / Red Mercuric Sulphide	Equal part
Snuhi Ksheera (स्नुहीक्षीर)	Euphorbia neriifolia (Sehund) latex/milk	Equal part
Kanta Loha Bhasma (कान्तलोहभस्म)	Incinerated Magnetic iron (magnetite) 	Equal part
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated Copper 	Equal part
preparation: 81.	Take all five ingredients in equal parts.perform Mardana procedure all together in a mortar with water (Jala) until homogeneous.\n82.	Form tablets of 125 mg each. Dry in shade and store in a glass vessel. This is Abhrakadi Vati.

name: Gaganmukh Ras
benefits: Suryavarta — Mastakashool Bheda (migraine/piercing headache)
ingredients:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Abhraka Grasa Parada (अभ्रक ग्रास युक्त पारद)	Mercury saturated with Mica (Abhraka-processed mercury)	Equal part
Teekshna Loha Bhasma (तीक्षण लोहभस्म)	Teekshna (sharp) Incinerated Iron 	Equal part
Kanta Loha Bhasma (कान्त लोहभस्म)	Incinerated Magnetic iron (magnetite) 	Equal part
Snuhi Ksheera (स्नुही / सेंड-थूहर दूध)	Euphorbia neriifolia (Sehund) latex/milk — forperform Mardana procedureing	As required
preparation: 83.	Take Abhraka Grasa Parada, Teekshna Loha Bhasma and Kanta Loha Bhasma in equal parts.\n84.	Grind all together in Snuhi (Sehund cactus) milk (Bhanda milk) until homogeneous. Dry and store safely. This is Gaganmukh Ras.
reference: Bharat Bhaishajya Ratnakar, part-2, verse 1489, page no 83

name: Suryoday Ras
benefits: Sarva-moordhagadaapaha (treats significantly of all head diseases)
ingredients:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Parada Bhasma (पारद-भस्म)	Incinerated Mercury 	Equal part (1 part)
Abhraka Bhasma (अभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated Mica  	Equal part (1 part)
Teekshna Loha Bhasma (तीक्षण लोह-भस्म)	Teekshna (sharp) Incinerated Iron 	Equal part (1 part)
Shu. Gandhaka (शुद्ध गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	Equal part (1 part)
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्र-भस्म)	Incinerated Copper 	Equal part (1 part)
Snuhi Ksheera (स्नुही / सेंड-थूहर दूध)	Euphorbia neriifolia (Sehund cactus) milk — for 1-day trituration	As required
Madhu (शहद)	Honey — for administration (in Loha Patra)	As required
preparation: 85.	Take all five Bhasma/mineral ingredients in equal parts. Mix together thoroughly.\n86.	Grind all together in Snuhi (Sehund cactus) milk for 1 day. Dry and store safely. This is Suryoday Ras.
reference: Bharat Bhaishajya Ratnakar, part-2, verse 2784, page no 406

name: Shwasahara Vataka
benefits: Kasa (cough) and Shwas (asthma/breathing disorders)
ingredients:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	50 gm (1 Pala)
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	50 gm (1 Pala)
Shunthi Churna (शुण्ठीचूर्ण)	Dry Ginger powder (part of Trikatu)	50 gm
Pippali Churna (पिप्पलीचूर्ण)	Long Pepper powder (part of Trikatu)	50 gm
Maricha Churna (मरिचचूर्ण)	Black Pepper powder (part of Trikatu)	50 gm
Vanga Bhasma (वङ्गभस्म)	Incinerated Tin 	50 gm (1 Pala)
Gomutra (गोमूत्र)	Cow's urine — for 3-day secondary trituration	As required
Vasa Patra Svaras (वासापत्रस्वरस)	Adhatoda vasica leaf juice — Anupana	As required
Madhu (मधु)	Honey — Anupana	As required
preparation:
1. In a stone mortar, firmly triturate Parada and Gandhaka together to form good Kajjali.
2. Then add all other substances (Shunthi Churna, Maricha Churna, Pippali Churna, Vanga Bhasma) and triturate all together for 3 days.
3. Then add Gomutra (cow's urine) and triturate for another 3 days.
4. Classical pill size: 12 gm (Aksha/1 Karsha); modern preparation: 375 mg pills. Dry in shade and store in a glass vessel.
anupana: Vasa Patra Svaras (Adhatoda vasica leaf juice) with Madhu (honey)

name: Hikkanaashana Ras
benefits: Hikka (hiccups), Vaiswarya (hoarseness), Kasa (cough)
ingredients:
Metals/minerals in progressively increasing proportions (total 21 parts); then 13 herb powders each 1 part equal to each other:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	1 part
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	2 parts
Abhraka Bhasma (अभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated Mica (Dhanyaabhra)	3 parts
Shu. Haritala (शु. हरताल)	Purified Orpiment (Arsenic trisulphide)	4 parts
Svarna Makshika Bhasma (स्वर्णमाक्षिकभस्म)	Incinerated Chalcopyrite/Iron Pyrite 	5 parts
Shu. Manaahshila (शु. मन:शिला)	Purified Realgar (Arsenic disulphide)	6 parts
Vacha Churna (वचचूर्ण)	Acorus calamus powder	1 part each
Kushtha Churna (कूठचूर्ण)	Saussurea lappa powder	1 part each
Haridra Churna (हल्दीचूर्ण)	Curcuma longa (turmeric) powder	1 part each
Yavakshara (यवक्षार)	Barley alkali (Incinerated Hordeum vulgare )	1 part each
Chitraka Churna (चित्रकचूर्ण)	Plumbago zeylanica powder	1 part each
Patha Churna (पाठाचूर्ण)	Cissampelos pareira powder	1 part each
Kilahara / Langali Churna (किलहारी / लांगलीचूर्ण)	Gloriosa superba powder	1 part each
Trikatu Churna (त्रिकटुचूर्ण)	Shunthi+Maricha+Pippali combined	1 part each
Saindhava Lavana (सैन्धवचूर्ण)	Rock salt powder	1 part each
Shu. Vatsanabha Churna (शु. वत्सनाभचूर्ण)	Purified Aconitum ferox powder	1 part each
Baheda Tvak Churna (बहेड़ात्वकचूर्ण)	Terminalia bellirica bark powder	1 part each
preparation:
1. In a stone mortar, firmly triturate Parada + Gandhaka to form good Kajjali.
2. Then add Haritala and Manaahshila to the Kajjali and triturate well.
3. Then add all Bhasmas (Abhraka Bhasma, Svarna Makshika Bhasma) and mix thoroughly.
4. Triturate all 13 herbal powders (Vacha, Kushtha, Haridra, Yavakshara, Chitraka, Patha, Kilahara/Langali, Trikatu, Saindhava, Shu. Vatsanabha, Baheda Tvak) into the mineral Kajjali.
5. Give 7 Bhavanas with Bhringaraja Svaras (Eclipta alba/Bhringraj juice).
6. Form 500 mg pills, dry in shade, and store in a glass vessel.
7. This Hikkanaashana Ras cures Hikka (hiccups), Swara Bhanga/Vaiswarya (hoarseness/voice disorders), and Kasa (cough).

name: Saugandhika Yoga
benefits: Shwas Roga (asthma/breathing disorders) and Kapha Roga
ingredients:
Take equal parts:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Shu. Gandhaka Churna (शु. गन्धकचूर्ण)	Purified Sulphur powder	Equal part (1 gm per dose)
Maricha Churna (मरिचचूर्ण)	Black Pepper powder	Equal part (1 gm per dose)
preparation:
1. Take Shu. Gandhaka Churna and Maricha Churna in equal parts.
2. Triturate together in a mortar and store in a glass vessel. This is called "Saugandhika Yoga".
3. Take 1 gm each (2 gm total) morning and evening with Goghrita (cow's ghee).
4. Consuming this treats significantlys Shwas Roga (asthma/respiratory distress) and Kapha Roga (Kapha diseases including bronchitis, mucus disorders).
5. Vimarsha (Commentary) – Some texts give the reading as 'Shwasaakasakshaayapaham' (also treats significantlys Kasa/cough and Kshaya/wasting/TB) — thus this simple two-ingredient combination may also be beneficial in respiratory wasting conditions.

name: Suryavarta Ras — for Shwas Roga
benefits: Shwas Roga (asthma/breathing disorders)
ingredients:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	1 part
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	1.5 parts
Shu. Tamra Patra (शु. ताम्रपत्र)	Purified Copper foil sheets — the medium for processing	1.5 parts
preparation:
1. In a stone mortar, triturate Shu. Parada and Shu. Gandhaka for 2–3 days to form good Kajjali.
2. Then give Bhavana with Ghrita Kumari Svaras (Aloe vera juice) and triturate this Kajjali for 1 Yama (3 hours) until well incorporated.
3. Apply the Kajjali paste onto Tamra Patra (copper foil) on both sides evenly. Place the Kajjali-coated copper foil in a Sharava vessel. Seal the Sharava with Kapadi Mitti (clay).
4. Fill the remaining space in the vessel/pot with sand (Baluka). Cook on a stove/fire for 1 full day (Handa Yantra/sand bath method).
5. Next day when Svangasheetal, open the Samputa, remove the medicine, perform Mardana procedure in mortar, and store in a glass vessel.
6. This is "Suryavarta Ras". Take 250 mg with Madhu or Goghrita — treats significantlys Shwas Roga powerfully.

name: Shwasantaka Ras
benefits: Kasa (cough), Shwas (asthma), Gulma, Shoola, Jathara, Pandu (anaemia)
ingredients:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	160 gm (16 parts)
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated Copper — equal to Parada	160 gm
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur — half of Parada	80 gm
Saindhava Lavana Churna (सैन्धवलवणचूर्ण)	Rock salt powder — equal to Gandhaka	80 gm
Pippali Churna (पिप्पलीचूर्ण)	Long Pepper powder	60 gm (6 parts)
preparation:
1. In a stone mortar, firmly triturate Parada + Gandhaka to form good Kajjali.
2. Then add Tamra Bhasma and triturate for 1 day.
3. Then add Saindhava Lavana Churna and Pippali Churna and mix well.
4. Give Bhavana with Jambira Nimbu Svaras (lemon juice) and form a large ball. Dry thoroughly.
5. Place in Sharava Samputa sealed with clay and fire in Baluka Yantra (sand bath) for 1 full day.
6. When Svangasheetal, open the Samputa and remove the medicine.
7. Perform Mardana procedure in mortar, filter through cloth, and store in a glass vessel.
8. Licking this Ras — Shwasantaka Ras — treats significantlys Kasa (cough), Shwas Roga (asthma), Gulma (abdominal gaseous condition/tumour), Shoola (colic), Jathara (abdominal disorder), and Pandu (anaemia).
anupana: Smell (Gandha)	Not specifically mentioned\nColor (Varna)	Not specifically mentioned\nTaste (Svad)	Not specifically mentioned

name: Shiladi Churna
benefits: Shwasa (asthma), Kasa (cough), Kapha Roga
ingredients:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Shu. Manashila (शु. मन:शिला)	Purified Realgar (Arsenic disulphide)	Equal part
Ghritabhrijita Heeng (घृतभर्जित हींग)	Asafoetida fried in ghee	Equal part
Vidanga Churna (विडङ्गचूर्ण)	Embelia ribes powder	Equal part
Mirch Churna (मिरचचूर्ण)	Black Pepper powder	Equal part
Kooth Churna (कूठचूर्ण)	Saussurea lappa (Costus root) powder	Equal part
Sendha Lavan Churna (सैन्धवलवणचूर्ण)	Rock Salt powder	Equal part
preparation: 1.	Take all six ingredients in equal parts.perform Mardana procedure  all together in a mortar to fine powder.\n2.	Collect in a glass vessel. This is Shiladi Churna.\n3.	For administration: Take 1 Karsha (approx. 12 grams) mixed with honey and Goghrita in unequal (Vishama) proportions and lick (Lehan).

name: Kajjali Yog
benefits: Hikka Roga (Hiccough)
ingredients:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated Copper 	Equal part
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	Equal part
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	Equal part
preparation:
1.	In a clean stone mortar, firmly triturate Shu. Parada and Shu. Gandhaka together until smooth black Kajjali is formed.
2.	Add Tamra Bhasma and triturate for one Prahar (approx. 3 hours). Collect in a glass vessel. This is Kajjali Yog.
Vimarsha (Commentary Note)
Some commentators also make a ball of the Kajjali + Tamra Bhasma mixture triturated with Goghrita, seal in a Sharavasamputa, bind firmly and cook in Bhalukayuntra (sand bath) for 6 hours. The next day, when cooled (Svaangasheetal), the medicine is removed, ground in mortar and collected in glass. This is also used at 125 mg dose.

name: Neelkanth Ras
benefits: Kasa (cough), Shwasa (asthma)

preparation: 1.	Prepare Kajjali from Shu. Parada and Shu. Gandhaka in a clean stone mortar. Add all other fine powders to the Kajjali and triturate together.\n2.	Prepare a firm Chasni (syrup/candy) from old Guda (jaggery) — using double the total powder weight (38 parts Guda for 19 parts powder).\n3.	Mix the combined powder into the Guda Chasni and form tablets of 6 grams each (Kola/jujube-seed size). Note: if Guda is added directly without making Chasni, tablets will melt. Collect in glass vessel. This is Neelkanth Ras.

name: Surya Ras
benefits: Kasa (cough), Shwasa (asthma)
ingredients:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity

Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	Equal part (1 part)
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated Copper 	Equal part (1 part)
Abhraka Bhasma (अभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated Mica	Equal part (1 part)
Peepar / Pippali Churna (पीपरचूर्ण)	Long Pepper powder	Equal part (1 part)
Shunthi Churna (शुण्ठीचूर्ण)	Dry Ginger powder	Equal part (1 part)
Mirch Churna (मिरचचूर्ण)	Black Pepper powder	Equal part (1 part)
Bibhitaka Churna (बिभीतकचूर्ण)	Terminalia bellirica powder	Equal part (1 part)
Shu. Vatsanabha Churna (शु. वत्सनाभचूर्ण)	Purified Aconitum ferox powder	Equal part (1 part)
preparation: 1.	In a clean stone mortar, firmly triturate Shu. Parada and Shu. Gandhaka until good Kajjali is formed.\n2.	Add Tamra Bhasma and Abhraka Bhasma to the Kajjali and triturate well.\n3.	Add all five fine powders (Pippali, Shunthi, Mirch, Bibhitaka, Vatsanabha) successively and triturate well. Collect in glass. This is Surya Ras.

name: Shwaskasari Keshari Ras
benefits: Kasa (cough), Shwasa (asthma)
ingredients:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Rajata Bhasma (रजतभस्म)	Incinerated Silver 	Equal part (1 part)
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated Copper 	Equal part (1 part)
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	Equal part (1 part)
Shu. Manashila (शु. मन:शिला)	Purified Realgar (Arsenic disulphide)	Equal part (1 part)
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur — first quantity	Equal part (1 part)
Shu. Haritala (शु. हरताल)	Purified Orpiment (Arsenic trisulphide)	Equal part (1 part)
Trikatu Kwath (त्रिकटु काथ)	Trikatu decoction — 7 Bhavanas post-Puta	As required
preparation:
1.	Prepare Kajjali from Shu. Parada and Shu. Gandhaka. Add Shu. Manashila and Shu. Haritala and make combined Kajjali. Add Rajata Bhasma and Tamra Bhasma. Triturate all together.
2.	Give 3-hour Bhavanas separately with: Vasa Patra Svaras, Tulasi Svaras and Ardraka Svaras (one each).
3.	Form a large ball. Dry well. Wrap in Mrittanya (clay). Fire in Bhudhara Puta (earth pit fire) for 2 Yama (6 hours).
4.	When cooled, open and remove medicine.perform Mardana procedure  in mortar. Add equal quantity of Shu. Gandhaka again.
5.	Give 7–7 Bhavanas with Vasa Patra Svaras and Trikatu Kwath. Form tablets of 375 mg each. Dry in shade and store in glass. This is Shwaskasari Keshari Ras.

name: Garbhachintamani Ras
benefits: Garbhini Roga (pregnancy disorders) and Sarva Roga (general diseases)
ingredients:
Take equal parts of all the following:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Jatiphala Churna (जायफलचूर्ण)	Myristica fragrans (Nutmeg) powder	Equal part
Shu. Tankana Churna (शु. टङ्कणचूर्ण / भुना सोहागा)	Purified Borax (roasted) powder	Equal part
Trikatu Churna / Vyosha (त्रिकटुचूर्ण / व्योष)	Shunthi+Maricha+Pippali combined	Equal part
Shu. Gandhaka Churna (शु. गन्धकचूर्ण)	Purified Sulphur powder	Equal part
Shu. Hingula / Shu. Singirif (शु. हिंगुल / शु. सिंगरिफ)	Purified Cinnabar (Mercuric Sulphide)	Equal part
preparation: Vidhi – Take all five substances (Jatiphala Churna, Shu. Tankana Churna, Trikatu/Vyosha, Shu. Gandhaka, Shu. Hingula) in equal parts. Combine in a mortar and triturate together for 2 Prahar (approximately 6 hours). Add Jambira Nimbu Svaras (lemon juice) gradually and form pills of 2 Gunja (250 mg). Dry in shade and store in a glass vessel. Take with Ardraka Svaras (fresh ginger juice) and follow with warm water. Like the sun dispelling darkness, this Ras treats significantlys all diseases — it is especially indicated for Stri Roga (women's diseases) and Garbhini (pregnancy-related conditions).

name: Chandrakala Rasa
benefits: Chandrakala Rasa treats significantlys: all Pittaja and Vata-Pittaja diseases; internal and external Daaha (burning sensation); great heat-fever; Bhrama (giddiness); Moorchha (fainting); excess blood-flow in weakened women; Urdhva and Adho Raktapitta (haemorrhage upward and downward); blood vomiting; and all Mootrakrichha (urinary pain/dysuria). It does not cause Agnimaandya. Especially useful in summer (Greeshma) and autumn (Sharad) seasons.
ingredients:
Ingredient
(Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	1 Tola (12g)
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated Copper 	1 Tola (12g)
Abhraka Bhasma (अभ्रकभस्म)	Incinerated Mica  	1 Tola (12g)
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	2 Tola (24g) — double
Kutki (कुटकी)	Picrorhiza kurroa powder — added to base	Equal part
Giloy Sattva / Guduchi Sattva (गिलोयका सत)	Tinospora cordifolia pure extract — added	Equal part
Khas (खस)	Vetiveria zizanioides (Khus grass root) powder	Equal part
Madhavee Lataa (माधवी लता)	Hiptage benghalensis powder	Equal part
Safed Chandan (सफेद चन्दन)	Santalum album (White sandalwood) powder	Equal part
Sariva (सारिवा)	Hemidesmus indicus (Indian sarsaparilla) powder	Equal part
Drakshaadi Kashaya (द्राक्षादिकषाय)	Drakshaadi Gana (grape + cooling herbs) decoction — 7 final Bhavanas	As required
preparation:
1. Prepare kajjali from Shu. Parada (1 Tola), Tamra Bhasma (1 Tola), Abhraka Bhasma (1 Tola) and Shu. Gandhaka (2 Tolas).
2. Give sequential 1-day Bhavanas with each juice separately: Nagarmotha, Dadima, Duurva, Ketaki Kali, Sahadevi, Ghritkumari, Pitta Papada, Ramsheetal and Shatavari.
3. Add equal parts of: Kutki, Giloy Sattva, Pitta Papada, Khas, Madhavee Lata, Safed Chandan and Sariva. Mix all together.
4. Give 7 more Bhavanas with Drakshaadi Gana decoction.
5. Form a ball, wrap in leaves (Pattas) and bury in a grain heap for 7 days. Remove, pound and form pills of Chana-matra (chickpea size). Store in glass vessel. This is Chandrakala Rasa.
reference: Bharat Bhaishajya Ratnakar, part-2, verse 1884, page no 207

benefits: Jalodar (Ascites/Abdominal dropsy)
ingredients:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	4 parts
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	8 parts
Haldi Churna (हल्दीचूर्ण)	Turmeric powder (Curcuma longa)	2 parts
Trifala Churna (त्रिफलाचूर्ण)	Trifala powder (Haritaki + Bibhitaki + Amalaki)	2 parts
Haritaki Churna (हरीतकीचूर्ण)	Terminalia chebula powder	2 parts
Trikatu Churna (त्रिकटुचूर्ण)	Trikatu powder (Sonth + Mirch + Pippali)	8 parts
Shwetjira Churna (श्वेतजीराचूर्ण)	White cumin seed powder (Cuminum cyminum)	8 parts
Dantimool Churna (दन्तीमूलचूर्ण)	Baliospermum montanum root powder	8 parts
Chitrakamool Kwath (चित्रकमूलकाथ)	Plumbago zeylanica root decoction — 7 Bhavanas	As required
preparation: 6.	Prepare Kajjali from Shu. Parada (4 parts) and Shu. Gandhaka (8 parts). Add all herbal powders in specified parts and triturate together.\n7.	Give 7 Bhavanas each — in this order: first Eranda Taila, then Jayanti Svaras, Snuhi Ksheera, Bhringaraj Svaras and Chitrakamool Kwath. (Eranda Taila must be given first — if given last, medicine will remain wet and not dry.)\n8.	Collect in glass vessel. This is Mahavihara Ras. Take 375 mg with warm water (Ushna Jala).

name: Mrityunjaya Ras
benefits: Mrityunjaya Rasa treats significantlys: Vata diseases, Kapha diseases, Mandagni (weak digestion), Jwara (fever), Shool (colic pain), all Maharoga (major diseases), Udara Roga (abdominal diseases), Yakrit Roga (liver), Pandu (anaemia/jaundice), Shotha (oedema), Gulma (abdominal lump), Grahani (malabsorption/IBS), Pliha (spleen), Vibandhana (constipation), Vamana (vomiting), Gulma with cough, Shwasa (asthma) and Hikka (hiccough
ingredients:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Tankana-kshara Churna (टंकणक्षारचूर्ण)	Borax alkali powder	10 grams
Shunthi Churna (शुण्ठीचूर्ण)	Dry Ginger powder	10 grams
Mirch Churna (मिरचचूर्ण)	Black Pepper powder	10 grams
Pippali Churna (पिप्पलीचूर्ण)	Long Pepper powder	10 grams

Vida Lavan Churna (विडलवणचूर्ण)	Vida/Black salt powder	10 grams
Sauvarcha Lavan Churna (सौर्वचलवणचूर्ण)	Sauvarcha salt powder	10 grams
Sambhar Lavan Churna (साम्भरलवणचूर्ण)	Sambhar salt powder	10 grams
Sauf Churna (सौफचूर्ण)	Fennel seed (Foeniculum vulgare) powder	10 grams
preparation: 9.	Prepare good Kajjali from Shu. Parada and Shu. Gandhaka (110 grams each).\n10.	Add all 11 salt/herb powders (10 grams each) to the Kajjali. Mix all together.\n11.	Give Jala (water) Bhavana and triturate for 3 days. Form tablets of 625 mg (5 Ratti). Dry in shade. Store in glass. This is Mrityunjaya Ras.

name: Surechanak Ras
benefits: Virechana, Shotha, Gulma, Jalodar, Pliha Roga
ingredients:
Ingredients

preparation: 12.	Firmly triturate Shu. Parada and Shu. Gandhaka in a mortar to prepare good Kajjali.\n13.	Add Shu. Nishthusha Jaypala, Shunthi churna, Mirch churna and Shu. Tankana. Mix all well and give Jala (water) Bhavana.\n14.	Form tablets of 125 mg each. Dry well in shade. Store in glass. This is Surechanak Ras.

This is Trailokasundar Ras.

name: Jimutakadi Vati — Udaraghna Ras
benefits: Sampurna Udara Roga (all abdominal diseases)
preparation: 18.	Prepare Kajjali from Shu. Parada and Shu. Gandhaka. Add Shu. Manashila and Shu. Haritala and triturate for 1 more day.\n19.	Add all remaining fine powders (Abhraka Bhasma, Loha Bhasma, Tamra Bhasma, Trikatu, Chitraka, Kooth, Safed Musli, Vatsanabha, Ajwain) to the Kajjali and triturate together.\n20.	Give 1-day Bhavana with Nimbuka-Neera (lemon/citron juice). Form tablets of 500 mg (4 Ratti). Dry in shade and store in glass. This is Jimutakadi Vati (Udaraghna Ras).

name: Vaishvanara Ras
benefits: Jalodara (ascites/abdominal fluid accumulation)
ingredients:
Take all the following in equal parts (2 parts each):
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury (Kajjali with Gandhaka)	2 parts
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur	2 parts
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated Copper 	2 parts
Shu. Shilajatu (शु. शिलाजीत)	Purified Shilajit (mineral pitch) — dissolved in warm water	2 parts
Kanta Loha Bhasma (कान्तलोहभस्म)	Incinerated Iron (magnetic) 	2 parts
Trikatu Churna (त्रिकटुचूर्ण)	Shunthi+Maricha+Pippali combined powder	2 parts
Chitraka Mool Churna (चित्रकमूलचूर्ण)	Plumbago zeylanica root powder	2 parts
Kushtha Churna (कुष्ठचूर्ण)	Saussurea lappa powder	2 parts
Nirgundi Churna (निर्गुण्डीचूर्ण)	Vitex negundo powder	2 parts
Shweta Musali Churna (श्वेतमुशलीचूर्ण)	Chlorophytum borivilianum powder	2 parts
Shu. Vatsanabha Churna (शु. वत्सनाभचूर्ण)	Purified Aconitum ferox powder	2 parts
Ajamoda Churna (अजमोदाचूर्ण)	Apium graveolens (carom/ajwain family) powder	2 parts
Nimba Kwatha (निम्बक्वाथ)	Neem (Azadirachta indica) decoction — 21 Bhavanas	As required
preparation: Vidhi – First make Kajjali from Shu. Parada and Shu. Gandhaka in a stone mortar. Then add Tamra Bhasma and mix. Add fine Churna of all other herbal substances (Trikatu, Chitraka, Kushtha, Nirgundi, Shweta Musali, Shu. Vatsanabha, Ajamoda). Dissolve Shu. Shilajatu in warm water and add. Mix all together and give 21 Bhavanas with Nimba Kwatha (neem decoction). After that give 7 more Bhavanas with Bhringaraja Svaras (Eclipta alba juice). Finally, add a little Madhu (honey) and knead to form 500 mg pills. Dry well in shade and store in a glass vessel. This is "Vaishvanara Ras". Give this medicine at night with Ushna Udaka (warm water). This Vaishvanara Yoga dries up the accumulated fluid in Jalodara (ascites).

name: Vajrakshara
benefits: Agnimandya, Ajeerna, Gulma, Udara Roga, Shotha, Shoola
ingredients:
Group A — Kshara base (12 gm each); Group B — Herb Churnas (6 gm each):
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Samudra Lavana Churna (सामुद्रलवणचूर्ण)	Sea salt powder	12 gm each
Saindhava Lavana Churna (सैन्धवलवणचूर्ण)	Rock salt powder	12 gm each
Kacha Lavana Churna (काचलवणचूर्ण)	Rock crystal salt powder	12 gm each
Yavakshara Churna (यवक्षारचूर्ण)	Barley alkali powder	12 gm each
Souvarchala Lavana Churna (सौवर्चललवणचूर्ण)	Souvarchala salt powder	12 gm each
Shu. Tankana Churna (शु. टङ्कणचूर्ण)	Purified Borax powder	12 gm each
Sarjika Kshara Churna (सर्जिक्षारचूर्ण)	Sodium carbonate (Sajji Khar) powder	12 gm each
Shunthi Churna (शुण्ठीचूर्ण)	Dry Ginger powder	6 gm each
Maricha Churna (मरिचचूर्ण)	Black Pepper powder	6 gm each
Pippali Churna (पिप्पलीचूर्ण)	Long Pepper powder	6 gm each
Amalaki Churna (आमलाचूर्ण)	Phyllanthus emblica powder	6 gm each
Haritaki Churna (हरीतकीचूर्ण)	Terminalia chebula powder	6 gm each
Baheda Churna (बहेड़ाचूर्ण)	Terminalia bellirica powder	6 gm each
Jeera Churna (जीराचूर्ण)	Cuminum cyminum (Cumin) powder	6 gm each
Haridra Churna (हल्दीचूर्ण)	Curcuma longa (Turmeric) powder	6 gm each
Chitraka Mool Churna (चित्रकमूलचूर्ण)	Plumbago zeylanica root powder	6 gm each
Chavya Churna (चव्यचूर्ण)	Piper cubeba powder	6 gm each
dose: 3 gm (classical texts say 2 Nishka = 6 gm but 3 gm is recommended)
preparation: Vidhi – Combine all 7 Kshara substances (Samudra Lavana, Saindhava, Kacha Lavana, Yavakshara, Souvarchala, Tankana, Sarjika) in equal parts and make fine Churna. Mix in a mortar and give Bhavanas with Arka Ksheera (Calotropis milk) and Snuhi Ksheera (Euphorbia milk) together for 3 days. Apply the moist paste on Arka Patra (Calotropis leaves) and wrap. Seal in Antahputa (inner clay vessel) and fire in Kukkuta Puta. No extra fuel is needed — only the dry leaves burn. When Svangasheetal, open — the fired salts with leaves. Remove and powder together in the mortar. Separately make fine Churna of the 10 herbal substances (Trikatu, Triphala, Jeera, Haridra, Chitraka, Chavya) in equal parts. Combine half of the Kshara with half the herbal Churna (equal ratio). Store in glass vessel. This is Vajrakshara — said to have been proclaimed by Bhagavan Shankara himself. Use 3 gm dose. Anupana varies by Dosha: warm water for Vata; ghee for Pitta; Gomutra for Kapha; Kanji for Tridosha. Indicated for all Udara Roga, Gulma, Shotha, Shoola, and Ajeerna.

name: Udayamartanda Ras
benefits: Shotha (oedema/swelling) and Bhagandara (anal fistula)
ingredients:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Shu. Tamra Patra (Sukshma) (शु. ताम्र का सूक्ष्म पत्र)	Purified Copper foil — cut into fine small pieces with scissors	46 gm (1 Pala)
Shu. Gandhaka Churna (शु. गन्धकचूर्ण)	Purified Sulphur powder — equal weight to Tamra Patra	46 gm (1 Pala)
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury — added after sunlight processing stage	12 gm (1 Tola / 1 Karsha)
Jala (जल)	Water — for forming 125 mg pills (optional)	As required
preparation: Vidhi – Cut fine Shu. Tamra Patra (purified copper foil) into small pieces with scissors. Place Tamra pieces and Shu. Gandhaka Churna (equal weight — 1 Pala each) in a stone mortar and triturate together for 3 hours. Then add Jambiri Nimbu Svaras and triturate firmly for 1 full day. Keep in strong direct sunlight (Teekhna Dhup) for 1 day. When the lemon juice has dissolved and absorbed the copper (Tamra Dravibhuta), add Shu. Parada 1 Tola (12 gm) and triturate again until a well-incorporated Kajjali is formed. Store in a glass vessel.\nVimarsha (Commentary) – A similar formulation called Tamradrutiras appears in Chapter 18 of the same text. That text also states that Kajjali applied on Tamra Patra and kept in strong sunlight causes Tamra Druti (copper dissolution). However, in practice this does not fully occur by sunlight alone. Therefore, the copper-foil-coated Kajjali must be repeatedly fired (8–10 times) in Sharava Samputa (sealed clay vessel) to properly form proper Tamra Bhasma before the medicine is fit for consumption. Puta firing is therefore essential.

name: Vinodavidyadhara Ras
benefits: Udara Roga (abdominal diseases) and Rechaneeya conditions (purgation-responsive)
ingredients:
Take equal parts of all three:
Ingredient (Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Shu. Parada (शु. पारद)	Purified Mercury	Equal part
Shu. Gandhaka (शु. गन्धक)	Purified Sulphur (for Kajjali with Parada)	Equal part
Shu. Tankana (शु. टङ्कण)	Purified Borax	Equal part
Shu. Jayapala Bija (शु. जयपाल बीज)	Purified Croton tiglium (Purging Croton) seeds	Equal part
preparation: Vidhi – First make Kajjali by firmly triturating Shu. Parada and Shu. Gandhaka in a stone mortar. Then add Shu. Tankana (purified borax) and Shu. Jayapala Bija (purified Croton tiglium seeds) in equal parts to the Kajjali. Triturate all together firmly. Dry in shade and store in a glass vessel.

name: Apasmara Nashana Rasa
benefits: Apasmara (Epilepsy / Seizure disorder)
ingredients:
Ingredients (Equal Parts — 1 Karsha = 12g each)
Ingredient
(Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Samudra Phena (समुद्रफेन)	Sepia officinalis (Cuttlefish bone) powder	1 Karsha (12g)
Haridra Churna (हरिद्राचूर्ण)	Curcuma longa (Turmeric) powder	1 Karsha (12g)
Jyotishmati Beeja Churna (ज्योतिष्मतीबीजचूर्ण)	Celastrus paniculatus (Intellect tree) seed powder	1 Karsha (12g)
Nasya Oil Ingredients
Ingredient
(Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Vacha (वच)	Acorus calamus powder	1 Karsha (10g)
Shunthee (शुण्ठी)	Zingiber officinale dry ginger powder	1 Karsha (10g)
Maricha (मरिच)	Piper nigrum black pepper powder	1 Karsha (10g)
Pippali (पिप्पल)	Piper longum long pepper powder	1 Karsha (10g)
Vidanga (विडङ्ग)	Embelia ribes powder	1 Karsha (10g)
preparation:
1. Prepare kajjali from Shu. Parada and Shu. Gandhaka. Add Mana:shila and Tuttha; perform Mardana procedure. Add all remaining Bhasmas, Samudra Phena, Haridra and Jyotishmati Beeja; perform Mardana procedure all together. Give Nimbu svaras and leave overnight. Perform Mardana procedure again in the morning.
2. Apply a thick coat of this paste to a copper bowl (1 Pala weight). Allow to dry. Place the paste-coated bowl upside-down (Adhomukha) inside a small earthen Haandi. Cover with a Sharaava (flat lid) and seal with Sandhibandha. Fill remaining space in the Haandi with cow-dung ash. Seal the Haandi mouth with another Sharaava-Samput. Dry completely.
3. Cook on a stove for 2 full days and nights. When completely cool, open and remove the copper bowl. Perform Mardana procedure the bowl together with the adhered medicine. Make fine powder. Store in glass vessel. This is Apasmara Nashana Rasa.
4. Nasya Oil: Cook the 5 herb powders in mustard oil with water in an iron kadahi until all water evaporates. Filter when cool. Store in glass vessel.

name: Bhootankusha Rasa
benefits: Bhootonmada (Mania from spirit possession); Apasmara (Epilepsy)
ingredients:

(Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity

preparation: 1. Perform Mardana procedure all Bhasmas and mineral ingredients together in a mortar with Bhringaraja juice, Chitraka juice and Snuhi (Sehund) milk throughout the day.\n2. In the evening, form balls/tablets, seal in Samput and fire in Gaja Puta. When cool, collect and store. This is Bhootankusha Rasa.
anupana: Dietary Regimen (Pathya)	Buffalo ghee, milk, heavy food; avoid pungent and rough food; bitter gourd fomentation; pungent oil body massage

name: Bhoot Bhairav Rasa
benefits: Apasmara (Epilepsy) â€” supreme remedy; Unmada (Insanity)
ingredients:
Ingredient
(Sanskrit)	English Name	Quantity
Rasasindura (रससिन्दूर)	Purified Mercuric Sulphide	Equal part
Tamra Bhasma (ताम्रभस्म)	Incinerated Copper    	Equal part
Loha Bhasma (लौहभस्म)	Incinerated Iron   	Equal part
Shu. Mana:shila (शु. मैनसिल)	Purified Realgar (Arsenic disulphide)	Equal part
